1253.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Client.HttpClientTransportOptions">
1254.             <summary>
1255.             Provides options for configuring <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Client.HttpClientTransport"/> instances.
1256.             </summary>
1257.         </member>
1258.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Client.HttpClientTransportOptions.Endpoint">
1259.             <summary>
1260.             Gets or sets the base address of the server for SSE connections.
1261.             </summary>
1262.             <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException">The value is <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
1263.             <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentException">The value is not an absolute URI, or does not use the HTTP or HTTPS scheme.</exception>
1264.         </member>
1265.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Client.HttpClientTransportOptions.TransportMode">
1266.             <summary>
1267.             Gets or sets the transport mode to use for the connection.
1268.             </summary>
1269.             <value>
1270.             The transport mode to use for the connection. The default is <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Client.HttpTransportMode.AutoDetect"/>.
1271.             </value>
1272.             <remarks>
1273.             When set to <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Client.HttpTransportMode.AutoDetect"/> (the default), the client will first attempt to use
1274.             Streamable HTTP transport and automatically fall back to SSE transport if the server doesn't support it.
1275.             </remarks>
1276.             <seealso href="https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2025-11-25/basic/transports#streamable-http">Streamable HTTP transport specification</seealso>.
1277.             <seealso href="https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2024-11-05/basic/transports#http-with-sse">HTTP with SSE transport specification</seealso>.
1278.         </member>
1279.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Client.HttpClientTransportOptions.Name">
1280.             <summary>
1281.             Gets or sets a transport identifier used for logging purposes.
1282.             </summary>
1283.         </member>
1284.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Client.HttpClientTransportOptions.ConnectionTimeout">
1285.             <summary>
1286.             Gets or sets a timeout used to establish the initial connection to the SSE server.
1287.             </summary>
1288.             <value>
1289.             The timeout used to establish the initial connection to the SSE server. The default is 30 seconds.
1290.             </value>
1291.             <remarks>
1292.             This timeout controls how long the client waits for:
1293.             <list type="bullet">
1294.               <item><description>The initial HTTP connection to be established with the SSE server.</description></item>
1295.               <item><description>The endpoint event to be received, which indicates the message endpoint URL.</description></item>
1296.             </list>
1297.             If the timeout expires before the connection is established, a <see cref="T:System.TimeoutException"/> is thrown.
1298.             </remarks>
1299.         </member>
1300.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Client.HttpClientTransportOptions.AdditionalHeaders">
1301.             <summary>
1302.             Gets or sets custom HTTP headers to include in requests to the SSE server.
1303.             </summary>
1304.             <remarks>
1305.             Use this property to specify custom HTTP headers that should be sent with each request to the server.
1306.             </remarks>
1307.         </member>
1308.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Client.HttpClientTransportOptions.KnownSessionId">
1309.             <summary>
1310.             Gets or sets a session identifier that should be reused when connecting to a Streamable HTTP server.
1311.             </summary>
1312.             <remarks>
1313.             <para>
1314.             When non-<see langword="null"/>, the transport assumes the server already created the session and will include the
1315.             specified session identifier in every HTTP request. This allows reconnecting to an existing session created in a
1316.             previous process. This option is only supported by the Streamable HTTP transport mode.
1317.             </para>
1318.             <para>
1319.             Clients should pair this with
1320.             <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClient.ResumeSessionAsync(ModelContextProtocol.Client.IClientTransport,ModelContextProtocol.Client.ResumeClientSessionOptions,ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClientOptions,Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILoggerFactory,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>
1321.             to skip the initialization handshake when rehydrating a previously negotiated session.
1322.             </para>
1323.             </remarks>
1324.         </member>
1325.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Client.HttpClientTransportOptions.OwnsSession">
1326.             <summary>
1327.             Gets or sets a value indicating whether this transport endpoint is responsible for ending the session on dispose.
1328.             </summary>
1329.             <remarks>
1330.             When <see langword="true"/> (default), the transport sends a DELETE request that informs the server the session is
1331.             complete. Set this to <see langword="false"/> when creating a transport used solely to bootstrap session information
1332.             that will later be resumed elsewhere.
1333.             </remarks>
1334.         </member>
1335.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Client.HttpClientTransportOptions.OAuth">
1336.             <summary>
1337.             Gets or sets the authorization provider to use for authentication.
1338.             </summary>
1339.         </member>
1340.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Client.HttpClientTransportOptions.MaxReconnectionAttempts">
1341.             <summary>
1342.             Gets or sets the maximum number of consecutive reconnection attempts when an SSE stream is disconnected.
1343.             </summary>
1344.             <value>
1345.             The maximum number of reconnection attempts. The default is 5.
1346.             </value>
1347.             <remarks>
1348.             When an SSE stream is disconnected (e.g., due to a network issue), the client will attempt to
1349.             reconnect using the Last-Event-ID header to resume from where it left off. This property controls
1350.             how many consecutive reconnection attempts are made before giving up. The counter resets to zero
1351.             on each successful stream read, so this value only limits consecutive failures.
1352.             </remarks>
1353.         </member>
1354.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Client.HttpClientTransportOptions.DefaultReconnectionInterval">
1355.             <summary>
1356.             Gets or sets the default interval at which the client attempts reconnection after an SSE stream is disconnected.
1357.             </summary>
1358.             <remarks>
1359.             <para>
1360.             The default value is 1 second.
1361.             </para>
1362.             <para>
1363.             If the server sends a message specifying a different reconnection interval, that new value will be used for all
1364.             subsequent reconnection attempts for that stream.
1365.             </para>
1366.             </remarks>
1367.         </member>
1368.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Client.HttpTransportMode">
1369.             <summary>
1370.             Specifies the transport mode for HTTP client connections.
1371.             </summary>
1372.         </member>
1373.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.Client.HttpTransportMode.AutoDetect">
1374.             <summary>
1375.             Automatically detect the appropriate transport by trying Streamable HTTP first, then falling back to SSE if that fails.
1376.             This is the recommended mode for maximum compatibility.
1377.             </summary>
1378.         </member>
1379.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.Client.HttpTransportMode.StreamableHttp">
1380.             <summary>
1381.             Use only the Streamable HTTP transport.
1382.             </summary>
1383.         </member>
1384.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.Client.HttpTransportMode.Sse">
1385.             <summary>
1386.             Use only the HTTP with SSE transport.
1387.             </summary>
1388.         </member>
1389.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Client.IClientTransport">
1390.             <summary>
1391.             Represents a transport mechanism for Model Context Protocol (MCP) client-to-server communication.
1392.             </summary>
1393.             <remarks>
1394.             <para>
1395.             The <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Client.IClientTransport"/> interface abstracts the communication layer between MCP clients
1396.             and servers, allowing different transport protocols to be used interchangeably.
1397.             </para>
1398.             <para>
1399.             When creating an <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClient"/>, <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClient.CreateAsync(ModelContextProtocol.Client.IClientTransport,ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClientOptions,Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILoggerFactory,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/> is typically used, and is
1400.             provided with the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Client.IClientTransport"/> based on expected server configuration.
1401.             </para>
1402.             </remarks>
1403.         </member>
1404.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Client.IClientTransport.Name">
1405.             <summary>
1406.             Gets a transport identifier, used for logging purposes.
1407.             </summary>
1408.         </member>
1409.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.Client.IClientTransport.ConnectAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)">
1410.             <summary>
1411.             Asynchronously establishes a transport session with an MCP server and returns a transport for the duplex message stream.
1412.             </summary>
1413.             <param name="cancellationToken">The <see cref="T:System.Threading.CancellationToken"/> to monitor for cancellation requests. The default is <see cref="P:System.Threading.CancellationToken.None"/>.</param>
1414.             <returns>An interface for the duplex message stream.</returns>
1415.             <remarks>
1416.             <para>
1417.             This method is responsible for initializing the connection to the server using the specific transport
1418.             mechanism implemented by the derived class. The returned <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ITransport"/> interface
1419.             provides methods to send and receive messages over the established connection.
1420.             </para>
1421.             <para>
1422.             The lifetime of the returned <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ITransport"/> instance is typically managed by the
1423.             <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClient"/> that uses this transport. When the client is disposed, it will dispose
1424.             the transport session as well.
1425.             </para>
1426.             <para>
1427.             This method is used by <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClient"/> to initialize the connection.
1428.             </para>
1429.             </remarks>
1430.             <exception cref="T:System.InvalidOperationException">The transport connection could not be established.</exception>
1431.         </member>
1432.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClient">
1433.             <summary>
1434.             Represents an instance of a Model Context Protocol (MCP) client session that connects to and communicates with an MCP server.
1435.             </summary>
1436.             <summary>
1437.             Represents an instance of a Model Context Protocol (MCP) client session that connects to and communicates with an MCP server.
1438.             </summary>
1439.         </member>
1440.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClient.#ctor">
1441.             <summary>
1442.             Initializes a new instance of the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClient"/> class.
1443.             </summary>
1444.         </member>
1445.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClient.ServerCapabilities">
1446.             <summary>
1447.             Gets the capabilities supported by the connected server.
1448.             </summary>
1449.             <exception cref="T:System.InvalidOperationException">The client is not connected.</exception>
1450.         </member>
1451.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClient.ServerInfo">
1452.             <summary>
1453.             Gets the implementation information of the connected server.
1454.             </summary>
1455.             <remarks>
1456.             <para>
1457.             This property provides identification details about the connected server, including its name and version.
1458.             It is populated during the initialization handshake and is available after a successful connection.
1459.             </para>
1460.             <para>
1461.             This information can be useful for logging, debugging, compatibility checks, and displaying server
1462.             information to users.
1463.             </para>
1464.             </remarks>
1465.             <exception cref="T:System.InvalidOperationException">The client is not connected.</exception>
1466.         </member>
1467.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClient.ServerInstructions">
1468.             <summary>
1469.             Gets any instructions describing how to use the connected server and its features.
1470.             </summary>
1471.             <remarks>
1472.             <para>
1473.             This property contains instructions provided by the server during initialization that explain
1474.             how to effectively use its capabilities. They should focus on guidance that helps a model
1475.             use the server effectively and should avoid duplicating tool, prompt, or resource descriptions.
1476.             </para>
1477.             <para>
1478.             This can be used by clients to improve an LLM's understanding of how to use the server.
1479.             It can be thought of like a "hint" to the model and can be added to a system prompt.
1480.             </para>
1481.             </remarks>
1482.         </member>
1483.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClient.Completion">
1484.             <summary>
1485.             Gets a <see cref="T:System.Threading.Tasks.Task`1"/> that completes when the client session has completed.
1486.             </summary>
1487.             <remarks>
1488.             <para>
1489.             The task always completes successfully. The result provides details about why the session
1490.             completed. Transport implementations may return derived types with additional strongly-typed
1491.             information, such as <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Client.StdioClientCompletionDetails"/>.
1492.             </para>
1493.             <para>
1494.             For graceful closure (e.g., explicit disposal), <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Client.ClientCompletionDetails.Exception"/>
1495.             will be <see langword="null"/>. For unexpected closure (e.g., process crash, network failure),
1496.             it may contain an exception that caused or that represents the failure.
1497.             </para>
1498.             </remarks>
1499.         </member>
1500.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClient.CreateAsync(ModelContextProtocol.Client.IClientTransport,ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClientOptions,Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILoggerFactory,System.Threading.CancellationToken)">
1501.             <summary>Creates an <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClient"/>, connecting it to the specified server.</summary>
1502.             <param name="clientTransport">The transport instance used to communicate with the server.</param>
1503.             <param name="clientOptions">
1504.             A client configuration object that specifies client capabilities and protocol version.
1505.             If <see langword="null"/>, details based on the current process are used.
1506.             </param>
1507.             <param name="loggerFactory">A logger factory for creating loggers for clients.</param>
1508.             <param name="cancellationToken">The <see cref="T:System.Threading.CancellationToken"/> to monitor for cancellation requests. The default is <see cref="P:System.Threading.CancellationToken.None"/>.</param>

1801.             <exception cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.McpException">The request failed or the server returned an error response.</exception>
1802.             <remarks>
1803.             <para>
1804.             This method provides a convenient way to subscribe to resource updates and handle notifications in a single call.
1805.             The returned <see cref="T:System.IAsyncDisposable"/> manages both the subscription and the notification handler registration.
1806.             When disposed, it automatically unsubscribes from the resource and removes the handler.
1807.             </para>
1808.             <para>
1809.             The handler will only be invoked for notifications related to the specified resource URI.
1810.             Notifications for other resources are filtered out automatically.
1811.             </para>
1812.             </remarks>
1813.         </member>

1902.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClient.CallToolAsTaskAsync(System.String,System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyDictionary{System.String,System.Object},ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskMetadata,System.IProgress{ModelContextProtocol.ProgressNotificationValue},ModelContextProtocol.RequestOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)">
1903.             <summary>
1904.             Invokes a tool on the server as a task for long-running operations.
1905.             </summary>
1906.             <param name="toolName">The name of the tool to call on the server.</param>
1907.             <param name="arguments">An optional dictionary of arguments to pass to the tool.</param>
1908.             <param name="taskMetadata">Metadata for task augmentation, including optional TTL. If <see langword="null"/>, an empty metadata is used.</param>
1909.             <param name="progress">An optional progress reporter for server notifications.</param>
1910.             <param name="options">Optional request options including metadata, serialization settings, and progress tracking.</param>
1911.             <param name="cancellationToken">The <see cref="T:System.Threading.CancellationToken"/> to monitor for cancellation requests. The default is <see cref="P:System.Threading.CancellationToken.None"/>.</param>
1912.             <returns>
1913.             An <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTask"/> representing the created task. Use <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClient.GetTaskAsync(System.String,ModelContextProtocol.RequestOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/> to poll for status updates
1914.             and <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClient.GetTaskResultAsync(System.String,ModelContextProtocol.RequestOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/> to retrieve the final result.
1915.             </returns>
1916.             <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="toolName"/> is <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
1917.             <exception cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.McpException">The request failed or the server returned an error response.</exception>
1918.             <remarks>
1919.             <para>
1920.             Task-augmented tool calls allow long-running operations to be executed asynchronously. Instead of blocking
1921.             until the tool completes, the server immediately returns a task identifier that can be used to poll for
1922.             status updates and retrieve the final result.
1923.             </para>
1924.             <para>
1925.             The server must advertise task support via <c>capabilities.tasks.requests.tools.call</c> and the tool
1926.             must have <c>execution.taskSupport</c> set to <c>"optional"</c> or <c>"required"</c>.
1927.             </para>
1928.             </remarks>
1929.         </member>
1930.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClient.GetTaskAsync(System.String,ModelContextProtocol.RequestOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)">

1937.             <returns>The current state of the task.</returns>
1938.             <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="taskId"/> is <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
1939.             <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentException"><paramref name="taskId"/> is empty or composed entirely of whitespace.</exception>
1940.             <exception cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.McpException">The request failed or the server returned an error response.</exception>
1941.         </member>
1942.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClient.GetTaskResultAsync(System.String,ModelContextProtocol.RequestOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)">
1943.             <summary>
1944.             Retrieves the result of a completed task, blocking until the task reaches a terminal state.
1945.             </summary>
1946.             <param name="taskId">The unique identifier of the task whose result to retrieve.</param>
1947.             <param name="options">Optional request options including metadata, serialization settings, and progress tracking.</param>
1948.             <param name="cancellationToken">The <see cref="T:System.Threading.CancellationToken"/> to monitor for cancellation requests. The default is <see cref="P:System.Threading.CancellationToken.None"/>.</param>
1949.             <returns>The raw JSON result of the task.</returns>
1950.             <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="taskId"/> is <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
1951.             <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentException"><paramref name="taskId"/> is empty or composed entirely of whitespace.</exception>
1952.             <exception cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.McpException">The request failed or the server returned an error response.</exception>
1953.             <remarks>
1954.             This method sends a tasks/result request to the server, which will block until the task completes if it hasn't already.
1955.             The server handles all polling logic internally.
1956.             </remarks>
1957.         </member>

1967.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClient.ListTasksAsync(ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ListTasksRequestParams,System.Threading.CancellationToken)">
1968.             <summary>
1969.             Retrieves a list of tasks from the server.
1970.             </summary>
1971.             <param name="requestParams">The request parameters to send in the request.</param>
1972.             <param name="cancellationToken">The <see cref="T:System.Threading.CancellationToken"/> to monitor for cancellation requests. The default is <see cref="P:System.Threading.CancellationToken.None"/>.</param>
1973.             <returns>The result of the request as provided by the server.</returns>
1974.             <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="requestParams"/> is <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
1975.             <exception cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.McpException">The request failed or the server returned an error response.</exception>
1976.             <remarks>
1977.             The <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClient.ListTasksAsync(ModelContextProtocol.RequestOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/> overload retrieves all tasks by automatically handling pagination.
1978.             This overload works with the lower-level <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ListTasksRequestParams"/> and <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ListTasksResult"/>, returning the raw result from the server.
1979.             Any pagination needs to be managed by the caller.
1980.             </remarks>
1981.         </member>
1982.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClient.CancelTaskAsync(System.String,ModelContextProtocol.RequestOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)">
1983.             <summary>
1984.             Cancels a running task on the server.
1985.             </summary>
1986.             <param name="taskId">The unique identifier of the task to cancel.</param>
1987.             <param name="options">Optional request options including metadata, serialization settings, and progress tracking.</param>
1988.             <param name="cancellationToken">The <see cref="T:System.Threading.CancellationToken"/> to monitor for cancellation requests. The default is <see cref="P:System.Threading.CancellationToken.None"/>.</param>
1989.             <returns>The updated state of the task after cancellation.</returns>
1990.             <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="taskId"/> is <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
1991.             <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentException"><paramref name="taskId"/> is empty or composed entirely of whitespace.</exception>
1992.             <exception cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.McpException">The request failed or the server returned an error response.</exception>
1993.             <remarks>
1994.             Cancelling a task requests that the server stop execution. The server may not immediately cancel the task,
1995.             and may choose to allow the task to complete if it's close to finishing.
1996.             </remarks>
1997.         </member>
1998.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClient.PollTaskUntilCompleteAsync(System.String,ModelContextProtocol.RequestOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)">
1999.             <summary>
2000.             Polls a task until it reaches a terminal status (completed, failed, or cancelled).
2001.             </summary>
2002.             <param name="taskId">The unique identifier of the task to poll.</param>
2003.             <param name="options">Optional request options including metadata, serialization settings, and progress tracking.</param>
2004.             <param name="cancellationToken">The <see cref="T:System.Threading.CancellationToken"/> to monitor for cancellation requests. The default is <see cref="P:System.Threading.CancellationToken.None"/>.</param>
2005.             <returns>The task in its terminal state.</returns>
2006.             <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="taskId"/> is <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
2007.             <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentException"><paramref name="taskId"/> is empty or composed entirely of whitespace.</exception>
2008.             <remarks>
2009.             <para>
2010.             This method repeatedly calls <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClient.GetTaskAsync(System.String,ModelContextProtocol.RequestOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/> until the task reaches a terminal status.
2011.             It respects the <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTask.PollInterval"/> returned by the server to determine how long
2012.             to wait between polling attempts.
2013.             </para>
2014.             <para>
2015.             For retrieving the actual result of a completed task, use <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClient.GetTaskResultAsync(System.String,ModelContextProtocol.RequestOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>.
2016.             </para>
2017.             </remarks>
2018.         </member>

2052.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClientHandlers">
2053.             <summary>
2054.             Provides a container for handlers used in the creation of an MCP client.
2055.             </summary>
2056.             <remarks>
2057.             <para>
2058.             This class provides a centralized collection of delegates that implement various capabilities of the Model Context Protocol.
2059.             </para>
2060.             <para>
2061.             Each handler in this class corresponds to a specific client endpoint in the Model Context Protocol and
2062.             is responsible for processing a particular type of message. The handlers are used to customize
2063.             the behavior of the MCP client by providing implementations for the various protocol operations.
2064.             </para>
2065.             <para>
2066.             When a server sends a message to the client, the appropriate handler is invoked to process it
2067.             according to the protocol specification. Which handler is selected
2068.             is done based on an ordinal, case-sensitive string comparison.
2069.             </para>
2070.             </remarks>
2071.         </member>
2072.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClientHandlers.NotificationHandlers">
2073.             <summary>Gets or sets notification handlers to register with the client.</summary>
2074.             <remarks>
2075.             <para>
2076.             When constructed, the client will enumerate these handlers, which might contain multiple handlers per notification method key, once.
2077.             The client will not re-enumerate the sequence after initialization.
2078.             </para>
2079.             <para>
2080.             Notification handlers allow the client to respond to server-sent notifications for specific methods.
2081.             Each key in the collection is a notification method name, and each value is a callback that will be invoked
2082.             when a notification with that method is received.
2083.             </para>
2084.             <para>
2085.             Handlers provided via <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClientHandlers.NotificationHandlers"/> will be registered with the client for the lifetime of the client.
2086.             For transient handlers, you can use <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.McpSession.RegisterNotificationHandler(System.String,System.Func{ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcNotification,System.Threading.CancellationToken,System.Threading.Tasks.ValueTask})"/> to register a handler that can
2087.             then be unregistered by disposing of the <see cref="T:System.IAsyncDisposable"/> returned from the method.
2088.             </para>
2089.             </remarks>
2090.         </member>
2091.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClientHandlers.RootsHandler">
2092.             <summary>
2093.             Gets or sets the handler for <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.RootsList"/> requests.
2094.             </summary>
2095.             <remarks>
2096.             This handler is invoked when the server sends a <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.RootsList"/> request to retrieve available roots.
2097.             The handler receives request parameters and should return a <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ListRootsResult"/> containing the collection of available roots.
2098.             </remarks>
2099.         </member>
2100.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClientHandlers.ElicitationHandler">
2101.             <summary>
2102.             Gets or sets the handler for processing <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.ElicitationCreate"/> requests.
2103.             </summary>
2104.             <remarks>
2105.             <para>
2106.             This handler function is called when an MCP server requests the client to provide additional
2107.             information during interactions. The client must set this property for the elicitation capability to work.
2108.             </para>
2109.             <para>
2110.             The handler receives message parameters and a cancellation token.
2111.             It should return a <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ElicitResult"/> containing the response to the elicitation request.
2112.             </para>
2113.             </remarks>
2114.         </member>
2115.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClientHandlers.SamplingHandler">
2116.             <summary>
2117.             Gets or sets the handler for processing <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.SamplingCreateMessage"/> requests.
2118.             </summary>
2119.             <remarks>
2120.             <para>
2121.             This handler function is called when an MCP server requests the client to generate content
2122.             using an AI model. The client must set this property for the sampling capability to work.
2123.             </para>
2124.             <para>
2125.             The handler receives message parameters, a progress reporter for updates, and a
2126.             cancellation token. It should return a <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CreateMessageResult"/> containing the
2127.             generated content.
2128.             </para>
2129.             <para>
2130.             You can create a handler using the <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.AIContentExtensions.CreateSamplingHandler(Microsoft.Extensions.AI.IChatClient,System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions)"/> extension
2131.             method with any implementation of <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.IChatClient"/>.
2132.             </para>
2133.             </remarks>
2134.         </member>
2135.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClientHandlers.TaskStatusHandler">
2136.             <summary>
2137.             Gets or sets the handler for processing <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.NotificationMethods.TaskStatusNotification"/> notifications.
2138.             </summary>
2139.             <remarks>
2140.             <para>
2141.             This handler is called when the server sends a task status notification to inform the client
2142.             about changes to a task's state. These notifications are optional and clients MUST NOT rely
2143.             on receiving them.
2144.             </para>
2145.             <para>
2146.             The handler receives the updated <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTask"/> object containing the current task state,
2147.             including its status, status message, and timestamps.
2148.             </para>
2149.             <para>
2150.             This handler is typically used to update UI or trigger actions based on task progress

2235.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClientOptions.ClientInfo">
2236.             <summary>
2237.             Gets or sets information about this client implementation, including its name and version.
2238.             </summary>
2239.             <remarks>
2240.             <para>
2241.             This information is sent to the server during initialization to identify the client.
2242.             It's often displayed in server logs and can be used for debugging and compatibility checks.
2243.             </para>
2244.             <para>
2245.             When not specified, information sourced from the current process is used.
2246.             </para>
2247.             </remarks>
2248.         </member>
2249.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClientOptions.Capabilities">

2252.             </summary>
2253.         </member>
2254.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClientOptions.ProtocolVersion">
2255.             <summary>
2256.             Gets or sets the protocol version to request from the server, using a date-based versioning scheme.
2257.             </summary>
2258.             <remarks>
2259.             <para>
2260.             The protocol version is a key part of the initialization handshake. The client and server must
2261.             agree on a compatible protocol version to communicate successfully.
2262.             </para>
2263.             <para>
2264.             If non-<see langword="null"/>, this version will be sent to the server, and the handshake
2265.             will fail if the version in the server's response does not match this version.
2266.             If <see langword="null"/>, the client will request the latest version supported by the server
2267.             but will allow any supported version that the server advertises in its response.
2268.             </para>
2269.             </remarks>
2270.         </member>
2271.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClientOptions.InitializationTimeout">
2272.             <summary>
2273.             Gets or sets a timeout for the client-server initialization handshake sequence.
2274.             </summary>
2275.             <value>
2276.             The timeout for the client-server initialization handshake sequence. The default value is 60 seconds.
2277.             </value>
2278.             <remarks>
2279.             <para>
2280.             This timeout determines how long the client will wait for the server to respond during
2281.             the initialization protocol handshake. If the server doesn't respond within this timeframe,
2282.             an exception is thrown.
2283.             </para>
2284.             <para>
2285.             Setting an appropriate timeout prevents the client from hanging indefinitely when
2286.             connecting to unresponsive servers.
2287.             </para>
2288.             </remarks>
2289.         </member>
2290.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClientOptions.Handlers">
2291.             <summary>
2292.             Gets or sets the container of handlers used by the client for processing protocol messages.
2293.             </summary>
2294.         </member>
2295.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClientOptions.TaskStore">
2296.             <summary>
2297.             Gets or sets the task store for managing client-side tasks.
2298.             </summary>
2299.             <remarks>
2300.             <para>
2301.             When a task store is configured, the client will support task-augmented requests from the server.
2302.             This allows the server to request sampling or elicitation as tasks, which the client executes
2303.             asynchronously and allows the server to poll for status and results.
2304.             </para>
2305.             <para>
2306.             If not set, task-augmented requests will not be supported, and the client will not advertise
2307.             task capabilities to the server.
2308.             </para>
2309.             </remarks>
2310.         </member>
2311.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClientOptions.SendTaskStatusNotifications">
2312.             <summary>
2313.             Gets or sets a value indicating whether the client should send task status notifications to the server.
2314.             </summary>
2315.             <value>
2316.             <see langword="true"/> to send task status notifications; <see langword="false"/> otherwise.
2317.             The default is <see langword="true"/>.
2318.             </value>
2319.             <remarks>
2320.             When enabled and a <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClientOptions.TaskStore"/> is configured, the client will send optional
2321.             <c>notifications/tasks/status</c> notifications to inform the server of task state changes.
2322.             Servers MUST NOT rely on receiving these notifications and should continue polling via <c>tasks/get</c>.
2323.             </remarks>
2324.         </member>
2325.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClientPrompt">
2326.             <summary>

2329.             <remarks>
2330.             <para>
2331.             This class provides a client-side wrapper around a prompt defined on an MCP server. It allows
2332.             retrieving the prompt's content by sending a request to the server with optional arguments.
2333.             Instances of this class are typically obtained by calling <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClient.ListPromptsAsync(ModelContextProtocol.RequestOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>.
2334.             </para>
2335.             <para>
2336.             Each prompt has a name and optionally a description, and it can be invoked with arguments
2337.             to produce customized prompt content from the server.
2338.             </para>
2339.             </remarks>

2341.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClientPrompt.#ctor(ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClient,ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Prompt)">

2344.             </summary>
2345.             <param name="client">The <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClient"/> instance to use for invoking the prompt.</param>
2346.             <param name="prompt">The protocol <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Prompt"/> definition describing the prompt's metadata.</param>
2347.             <remarks>
2348.             <para>
2349.             This constructor enables reusing cached prompt definitions across different <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClient"/> instances
2350.             without needing to call <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClient.ListPromptsAsync(ModelContextProtocol.RequestOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/> on every reconnect. This is particularly useful
2351.             in scenarios where prompt definitions are stable and network round-trips should be minimized.
2352.             </para>
2353.             <para>
2354.             The provided <paramref name="prompt"/> must represent a prompt that is actually available on the server
2355.             associated with the <paramref name="client"/>. Attempting to invoke a prompt that doesn't exist on the
2356.             server will result in an <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.McpException"/>.
2357.             </para>

2551.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClientTool">
2552.             <summary>
2553.             Provides an <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/> that calls a tool via an <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClient"/>.
2554.             </summary>
2555.             <remarks>
2556.             <para>
2557.             The <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClientTool"/> class encapsulates an <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClient"/> along with a description of
2558.             a tool available via that client, allowing it to be invoked as an <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/>. This enables integration
2559.             with AI models that support function calling capabilities.
2560.             </para>
2561.             <para>
2562.             Tools retrieved from an MCP server can be customized for model presentation using methods like
2563.             <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClientTool.WithName(System.String)"/> and <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClientTool.WithDescription(System.String)"/> without changing the underlying tool functionality.
2564.             </para>
2565.             <para>
2566.             Typically, you would get instances of this class by calling the <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClient.ListToolsAsync(ModelContextProtocol.RequestOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>
2567.             method on an <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClient"/> instance.
2568.             </para>
2569.             </remarks>
2570.         </member>
2571.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClientTool.#ctor(ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClient,ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Tool,System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions)">
2572.             <summary>
2573.             Initializes a new instance of the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClientTool"/> class.
2574.             </summary>
2575.             <param name="client">The <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClient"/> instance to use for invoking the tool.</param>
2576.             <param name="tool">The protocol <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Tool"/> definition describing the tool's metadata and schema.</param>
2577.             <param name="serializerOptions">
2578.             The JSON serialization options governing argument serialization. If <see langword="null"/>,
2579.             <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.McpJsonUtilities.DefaultOptions"/> is used.
2580.             </param>
2581.             <remarks>
2582.             <para>
2583.             This constructor enables reusing cached tool definitions across different <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClient"/> instances
2584.             without needing to call <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClient.ListToolsAsync(ModelContextProtocol.RequestOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/> on every reconnect. 
2585.             This is particularly useful in scenarios where tool definitions are stable and network round-trips should be minimized.
2586.             </para>
2587.             <para>
2588.             The provided <paramref name="tool"/> must represent a tool that is actually available on the server
2589.             associated with the <paramref name="client"/>. Attempting to invoke a tool that doesn't exist on the
2590.             server will result in an <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.McpException"/>.
2591.             </para>
2592.             </remarks>
2593.             <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="client"/> or <paramref name="tool"/> is <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
2594.         </member>

2604.             like <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClientTool.WithName(System.String)"/> or <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClientTool.WithDescription(System.String)"/>.

2628.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClientTool.CallAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyDictionary{System.String,System.Object},System.IProgress{ModelContextProtocol.ProgressNotificationValue},ModelContextProtocol.RequestOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)">
2629.             <summary>
2630.             Invokes the tool on the server.
2631.             </summary>
2632.             <param name="arguments">An optional dictionary of arguments to pass to the tool. Each key represents a parameter name,
2633.             and its associated value represents the argument value.
2634.             </param>
2635.             <param name="progress">
2636.             An optional <see cref="T:System.IProgress`1"/> to have progress notifications reported to it. Setting this to a non-<see langword="null"/>
2637.             value will result in a progress token being included in the call, and any resulting progress notifications during the operation
2638.             routed to this instance.
2639.             </param>
2640.             <param name="options">
2641.             Optional request options including metadata, serialization settings, and progress tracking.
2642.             </param>
2643.             <param name="cancellationToken">The <see cref="T:System.Threading.CancellationToken"/> to monitor for cancellation requests. The default is <see cref="P:System.Threading.CancellationToken.None"/>.</param>
2644.             <returns>
2645.             A task containing the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolResult"/> from the tool execution. The response includes
2646.             the tool's output content, which can be structured data, text, or an error message.
2647.             </returns>
2648.             <remarks>
2649.             The base <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction.InvokeAsync(Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/> method is overridden to invoke this <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClientTool.CallAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyDictionary{System.String,System.Object},System.IProgress{ModelContextProtocol.ProgressNotificationValue},ModelContextProtocol.RequestOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/> method.
2650.             The only difference in behavior is that <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction.InvokeAsync(Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/> serializes the resulting <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolResult"/>
2651.             such that the <see cref="T:System.Object"/> returned is a <see cref="T:System.Text.Json.JsonElement"/> containing the serialized <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolResult"/>.
2652.             This <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClientTool.CallAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyDictionary{System.String,System.Object},System.IProgress{ModelContextProtocol.ProgressNotificationValue},ModelContextProtocol.RequestOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/> method is intended to be called directly by user code, whereas the base <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction.InvokeAsync(Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>
2653.             is intended to be used polymorphically via the base class, typically as part of an <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.IChatClient"/> operation.
2654.             </remarks>
2655.             <exception cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.McpException">The server could not find the requested tool, or the server encountered an error while processing the request.</exception>
2656.             <example>
2657.             <code language="csharp">
2658.             var result = await tool.CallAsync(
2659.                 new Dictionary<string, object?>
2660.                 {
2661.                     ["message"] = "Hello MCP!"
2662.                 });
2663.             </code>
2664.             </example>
2665.         </member>
2666.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClientTool.WithName(System.String)">
2667.             <summary>
2668.             Creates a new instance of the tool but modified to return the specified name from its <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClientTool.Name"/> property.
2669.             </summary>
2670.             <param name="name">The model-facing name to give the tool.</param>
2671.             <returns>A new instance of <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClientTool"/> with the provided name.</returns>
2672.             <remarks>
2673.             <para>
2674.             This method is useful for optimizing the tool name for specific models or for prefixing the tool name
2675.             with a namespace to avoid conflicts.
2676.             </para>
2677.             <para>
2678.             Changing the name can help with:
2679.             </para>
2680.             <list type="bullet">
2681.               <item>Making the tool name more intuitive for the model.</item>
2682.               <item>Preventing name collisions when using tools from multiple sources.</item>
2683.               <item>Creating specialized versions of a general tool for specific contexts.</item>
2684.             </list>
2685.             <para>
2686.             When invoking <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction.InvokeAsync(Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>, the MCP server will still be called with
2687.             the original tool name, so no mapping is required on the server side. This new name only affects
2688.             the value returned from this instance's <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AITool.Name"/>.
2689.             </para>
2690.             </remarks>
2691.         </member>
2692.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClientTool.WithDescription(System.String)">
2693.             <summary>
2694.             Creates a new instance of the tool but modified to return the specified description from its <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClientTool.Description"/> property.
2695.             </summary>
2696.             <param name="description">The description to give the tool.</param>
2697.             <remarks>
2698.             <para>
2699.             Changing the description can help the model better understand the tool's purpose or provide more
2700.             context about how the tool should be used. This is particularly useful when:
2701.             </para>
2702.             <list type="bullet">
2703.               <item>The original description is too technical or lacks clarity for the model.</item>
2704.               <item>You want to add example usage scenarios to improve the model's understanding.</item>
2705.               <item>You need to tailor the tool's description for specific model requirements.</item>
2706.             </list>
2707.             <para>
2708.             When invoking <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction.InvokeAsync(Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>, the MCP server will still be called with
2709.             the original tool description, so no mapping is required on the server side. This new description only affects
2710.             the value returned from this instance's <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AITool.Description"/>.
2711.             </para>
2712.             </remarks>
2713.             <returns>A new instance of <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClientTool"/> with the provided description.</returns>
2714.         </member>
2715.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClientTool.WithProgress(System.IProgress{ModelContextProtocol.ProgressNotificationValue})">
2716.             <summary>
2717.             Creates a new instance of the tool but modified to report progress via the specified <see cref="T:System.IProgress`1"/>.
2718.             </summary>
2719.             <param name="progress">The <see cref="T:System.IProgress`1"/> to which progress notifications should be reported.</param>
2720.             <remarks>
2721.             <para>
2722.             Adding an <see cref="T:System.IProgress`1"/> to the tool does not impact how it is reported to any AI model.
2723.             Rather, when the tool is invoked, the request to the MCP server will include a unique progress token,
2724.             and any progress notifications issued by the server with that progress token while the operation is in
2725.             flight will be reported to the <paramref name="progress"/> instance.
2726.             </para>
2727.             <para>
2728.             Only one <see cref="T:System.IProgress`1"/> can be specified at a time. Calling <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClientTool.WithProgress(System.IProgress{ModelContextProtocol.ProgressNotificationValue})"/> again
2729.             overwrites any previously specified progress instance.
2730.             </para>
2731.             </remarks>
2732.             <returns>A new instance of <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClientTool"/>, configured with the provided progress instance.</returns>
2733.             <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="progress"/> is <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
2734.         </member>
2735.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClientTool.WithMeta(System.Text.Json.Nodes.JsonObject)">
2736.             <summary>
2737.             Creates a new instance of the tool but modified to include the specified metadata in tool call requests.
2738.             </summary>
2739.             <param name="meta">
2740.             The metadata to include in tool call requests. This will be serialized as the <c>_meta</c> field
2741.             in the JSON-RPC request parameters.
2742.             </param>
2743.             <remarks>
2744.             <para>
2745.             Adding metadata to the tool allows you to pass additional protocol-level information with each tool call.
2746.             This can be useful for tracing, logging, or passing context information to the server.
2747.             </para>
2748.             <para>
2749.             Only one metadata object can be specified at a time. Calling <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClientTool.WithMeta(System.Text.Json.Nodes.JsonObject)"/> again
2750.             will overwrite any previously specified metadata object. If passed <see langword="null"/>,
2751.             any previously supplied metadata will be removed.
2752.             </para>
2753.             <para>
2754.             The metadata is passed through to the server as-is, merged with any protocol-level metadata
2755.             such as progress tokens when <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClientTool.WithProgress(System.IProgress{ModelContextProtocol.ProgressNotificationValue})"/> is also used. If a <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.RequestOptions"/>
2756.             is passed to <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClientTool.CallAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyDictionary{System.String,System.Object},System.IProgress{ModelContextProtocol.ProgressNotificationValue},ModelContextProtocol.RequestOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>, the metadata from both <paramref name="meta"/> and its
2757.             <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.RequestOptions"/> will be merged, preferring values from the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.RequestOptions"/> in
2758.             case of conflicts.
2759.             </para>
2760.             </remarks>
2761.             <returns>A new instance of <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClientTool"/>, configured with the provided metadata.</returns>
2762.         </member>

2929.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Client.StdioClientTransportOptions.EnvironmentVariables">
2930.             <summary>
2931.             Gets or sets environment variables to set for the server process.
2932.             </summary>
2933.             <remarks>
2934.             <para>
2935.             This property allows you to specify environment variables that will be set in the server process's
2936.             environment. Setting these variables is useful for passing configuration, authentication information, or runtime flags
2937.             to the server without modifying its code.
2938.             </para>
2939.             <para>
2940.             By default, when starting the server process, the server process will inherit the current environment's variables,
2941.             as discovered via <see cref="M:System.Environment.GetEnvironmentVariables"/>. After those variables are found, the entries
2942.             in this <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Client.StdioClientTransportOptions.EnvironmentVariables"/> dictionary are used to augment and overwrite the entries read from the environment.
2943.             That includes removing the variables for any of this collection's entries with a null value.
2944.             </para>
2945.             </remarks>
2946.         </member>
2947.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Client.StdioClientTransportOptions.ShutdownTimeout">
2948.             <summary>
2949.             Gets or sets the timeout to wait for the server to shut down gracefully.
2950.             </summary>
2951.             <value>
2952.             The amount of time to wait for the server to shut down gracefully. The default is 5 seconds.
2953.             </value>
2954.             <remarks>
2955.             <para>
2956.             This property dictates how long the client should wait for the server process to exit cleanly during shutdown
2957.             before forcibly terminating it. This balances giving the server enough time to clean up
2958.             resources and not hanging indefinitely if a server process becomes unresponsive.
2959.             </para>
2960.             </remarks>
2961.         </member>

3079.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Annotations">
3080.             <summary>
3081.             Represents annotations that can be attached to content, resources, and resource templates.
3082.             </summary>
3083.             <remarks>
3084.             Annotations enable filtering and prioritization of content for different audiences.
3085.             See the <see href="https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/specification/blob/main/schema/">schema</see> for details.
3086.             </remarks>
3087.         </member>
3088.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Annotations.Audience">
3089.             <summary>
3090.             Gets or sets the intended audience for this content as an array of <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Role"/> values.
3091.             </summary>
3092.         </member>
3093.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Annotations.Priority">
3094.             <summary>
3095.             Gets or sets a value indicating how important this data is for operating the server.
3096.             </summary>
3097.             <remarks>
3098.             The value is a floating-point number between 0 and 1, where 0 represents the lowest priority
3099.             and 1 represents the highest priority.
3100.             </remarks>
3101.         </member>
3102.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Annotations.LastModified">
3103.             <summary>
3104.             Gets or sets the moment the resource was last modified.
3105.             </summary>
3106.             <remarks>
3107.             The corresponding JSON should be an ISO 8601 formatted string (for example, \"2025-01-12T15:00:58Z\").
3108.             Examples of when the resource was last modified include last activity in an open file or when the resource was attached.
3109.             </remarks>
3110.         </member>
3111.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Argument">
3112.             <summary>
3113.             Represents an argument used in completion requests to provide context for auto-completion functionality.
3114.             </summary>
3115.             <remarks>
3116.             This class is used when requesting completion suggestions for a particular field or parameter.
3117.             See the <see href="https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/specification/blob/main/schema/">schema</see> for details.
3118.             </remarks>
3119.         </member>
3120.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Argument.Name">
3121.             <summary>
3122.             Gets or sets the name of the argument being completed.
3123.             </summary>
3124.         </member>
3125.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Argument.Value">
3126.             <summary>
3127.             Gets or sets the current partial text value for which completion suggestions are requested.
3128.             </summary>
3129.             <remarks>
3130.             This property represents the text that has been entered so far and for which completion
3131.             options should be generated.
3132.             </remarks>
3133.         </member>
3134.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.BlobResourceContents">
3135.             <summary>
3136.             Represents the binary contents of a resource in the Model Context Protocol.
3137.             </summary>
3138.             <remarks>
3139.             <para>
3140.             <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.BlobResourceContents"/> is used when binary data needs to be exchanged through
3141.             the Model Context Protocol. The binary data is represented as base64-encoded UTF-8 bytes
3142.             in the <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.BlobResourceContents.Blob"/> property.
3143.             </para>
3144.             <para>
3145.             This class inherits from <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ResourceContents"/>, which also has a sibling implementation
3146.             <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.TextResourceContents"/> for text-based resources. When working with resources, the
3147.             appropriate type is chosen based on the nature of the content.
3148.             </para>
3149.             <para>
3150.             See the <see href="https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/specification/blob/main/schema/">schema</see> for more details.
3151.             </para>
3152.             </remarks>
3153.         </member>
3154.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.BlobResourceContents.FromBytes(System.ReadOnlyMemory{System.Byte},System.String,System.String)">
3155.             <summary>
3156.             Creates a <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.BlobResourceContents"/> from raw data.
3157.             </summary>
3158.             <param name="bytes">The raw unencoded data.</param>
3159.             <param name="uri">The URI of the blob resource.</param>
3160.             <param name="mimeType">The optional MIME type of the data.</param>
3161.             <returns>A new <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.BlobResourceContents"/> instance.</returns>
3162.         </member>
3163.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.BlobResourceContents.#ctor">
3164.             <summary>Initializes a new instance of the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.BlobResourceContents"/> class.</summary>
3165.         </member>
3166.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.BlobResourceContents.Blob">
3167.             <summary>
3168.             Gets or sets the base64-encoded UTF-8 bytes representing the binary data of the item.
3169.             </summary>
3170.             <remarks>
3171.             Setting this value will invalidate any cached value of <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.BlobResourceContents.DecodedData"/>.
3172.             </remarks>
3173.         </member>
3174.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.BlobResourceContents.DecodedData">
3175.             <summary>
3176.             Gets the decoded data represented by <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.BlobResourceContents.Blob"/>.
3177.             </summary>
3178.             <remarks>
3179.             <para>
3180.             When getting, this member will decode the value in <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.BlobResourceContents.Blob"/> and cache the result.
3181.             Subsequent accesses return the cached value unless <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.BlobResourceContents.Blob"/> is modified.
3182.             </para>
3183.             </remarks>
3184.         </member>
3185.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolRequestParams">
3186.             <summary>
3187.             Represents the parameters used with a <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.ToolsCall"/> request from a client to invoke a tool provided by the server.
3188.             </summary>
3189.             <remarks>
3190.             The server will respond with a <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolResult"/> containing the result of the tool invocation.
3191.             See the <see href="https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/specification/blob/main/schema/">schema</see> for details.
3192.             </remarks>
3193.         </member>
3194.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolRequestParams.Name">
3195.             <summary>Gets or sets the name of the tool to invoke.</summary>
3196.         </member>
3197.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolRequestParams.Arguments">
3198.             <summary>
3199.             Gets or sets optional arguments to pass to the tool when invoking it on the server.
3200.             </summary>
3201.             <remarks>
3202.             This dictionary contains the parameter values to be passed to the tool. Each key-value pair represents 
3203.             a parameter name and its corresponding argument value.
3204.             </remarks>
3205.         </member>
3206.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolRequestParams.Task">
3207.             <summary>
3208.             Gets or sets optional task metadata to augment this request with task execution.
3209.             </summary>
3210.             <remarks>
3211.             When present, indicates that the requestor wants this operation executed as a task.
3212.             The receiver must support task augmentation for this specific request type.
3213.             </remarks>
3214.         </member>
3215.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolResult">
3216.             <summary>
3217.             Represents the result of a <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.ToolsCall"/> request from a client to invoke a tool provided by the server.
3218.             </summary>
3219.             <remarks>
3220.             <para>
3221.             Tool execution errors (including input validation errors, API failures, and business logic errors)
3222.             should be reported inside the result object with <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolResult.IsError"/> set to <see langword="true"/>,
3223.             rather than as a <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcError"/>. This allows language models to see error details
3224.             and potentially self-correct in subsequent requests.
3225.             </para>
3226.             <para>
3227.             To return a validation or business-logic error from a tool method, either throw an <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.McpException"/>
3228.             (whose <see cref="P:System.Exception.Message"/> will be included in the error result), or declare the tool's return type
3229.             as <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolResult"/> so it can be returned directly with <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolResult.IsError"/> set to <see langword="true"/>
3230.             and details in <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolResult.Content"/>. Using <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolResult"/> as the return type gives the tool full control
3231.             over both success and error responses.
3232.             </para>
3233.             <para>
3234.             Protocol-level errors (such as unknown tool names, malformed requests that fail schema validation,
3235.             or server errors) should be reported as MCP protocol error responses using <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.McpErrorCode"/>.
3236.             </para>
3237.             <para>
3238.             See the <see href="https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/specification/blob/main/schema/">schema</see> for details.
3239.             </para>
3240.             </remarks>
3241.         </member>
3242.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolResult.Content">
3243.             <summary>
3244.             Gets or sets the response content from the tool call.
3245.             </summary>
3246.         </member>
3247.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolResult.StructuredContent">
3248.             <summary>
3249.             Gets or sets an optional JSON object representing the structured result of the tool call.
3250.             </summary>
3251.         </member>
3252.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolResult.IsError">
3253.             <summary>
3254.             Gets or sets a value that indicates whether the tool call was unsuccessful.
3255.             </summary>
3256.             <value>
3257.             <see langword="true"/> to signify that the tool execution failed; <see langword="false"/> if it was successful.
3258.             </value>
3259.             <remarks>
3260.             <para>
3261.             Tool execution errors (including input validation errors, API failures, and business logic errors)
3262.             are reported with this property set to <see langword="true"/> and details in the <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolResult.Content"/>
3263.             property, rather than as protocol-level errors.
3264.             </para>
3265.             <para>
3266.             This design allows language models to receive detailed error feedback and potentially self-correct
3267.             in subsequent requests. For example, if a date parameter is in the wrong format or out of range,
3268.             the error message in <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolResult.Content"/> can explain the issue, enabling the model to retry
3269.             with corrected parameters.
3270.             </para>
3271.             </remarks>
3272.         </member>
3273.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolResult.Task">
3274.             <summary>
3275.             Gets or sets the task data for the newly created task.
3276.             </summary>
3277.             <remarks>
3278.             This property is populated only for task-augmented tool calls. When present, the other properties
3279.             (<see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolResult.Content"/>, <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolResult.StructuredContent"/>, <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolResult.IsError"/>) may not be populated.
3280.             The actual tool result can be retrieved later via <c>tasks/result</c>.
3281.             </remarks>
3282.         </member>
3283.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CancelledNotificationParams">
3284.             <summary>
3285.             Represents a notification indicating that a request has been cancelled by the client,
3286.             and that any associated processing should cease immediately.
3287.             </summary>
3288.             <remarks>
3289.             This class is typically used in conjunction with the <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.NotificationMethods.CancelledNotification"/>
3290.             method identifier. When a client sends this notification, the server should attempt to
3291.             cancel any ongoing operations associated with the specified request ID.
3292.             </remarks>
3293.         </member>
3294.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CancelledNotificationParams.RequestId">
3295.             <summary>
3296.             Gets or sets the ID of the request to cancel.
3297.             </summary>
3298.             <remarks>
3299.             This value must match the ID of an in-flight request that the sender wishes to cancel.
3300.             </remarks>
3301.         </member>
3302.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CancelledNotificationParams.Reason">
3303.             <summary>
3304.             Gets or sets an optional string describing the reason for the cancellation request.
3305.             </summary>
3306.         </member>
3307.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CancelMcpTaskRequestParams">
3308.             <summary>
3309.             Represents the parameters for a tasks/cancel request to explicitly cancel a task.
3310.             </summary>
3311.             <remarks>
3312.             <para>
3313.             Receivers must reject cancellation requests for tasks already in a terminal status
3314.             (<see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatus.Completed"/>, <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatus.Failed"/>, or
3315.             <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatus.Cancelled"/>) with error code -32602 (Invalid params).
3316.             </para>
3317.             <para>
3318.             Upon receiving a valid cancellation request, receivers should attempt to stop the task
3319.             execution and must transition the task to <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatus.Cancelled"/> status
3320.             before sending the response.
3321.             </para>
3322.             </remarks>
3323.         </member>
3324.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CancelMcpTaskRequestParams.TaskId">
3325.             <summary>
3326.             Gets or sets the unique identifier of the task to cancel.
3327.             </summary>
3328.         </member>
3329.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CancelMcpTaskResult">
3330.             <summary>
3331.             Represents the result of a tasks/cancel request.
3332.             </summary>
3333.             <remarks>
3334.             The result contains the updated task state after cancellation. The task will be in
3335.             <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatus.Cancelled"/> status if the cancellation was successful.
3336.             </remarks>
3337.         </member>
3338.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CancelMcpTaskResult.TaskId">
3339.             <summary>
3340.             Gets or sets the task ID.
3341.             </summary>
3342.         </member>
3343.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CancelMcpTaskResult.Status">
3344.             <summary>
3345.             Gets or sets the current status of the task (should be <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatus.Cancelled"/>).
3346.             </summary>
3347.         </member>
3348.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CancelMcpTaskResult.StatusMessage">
3349.             <summary>
3350.             Gets or sets an optional message describing the cancellation.
3351.             </summary>
3352.         </member>
3353.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CancelMcpTaskResult.CreatedAt">
3354.             <summary>
3355.             Gets or sets the ISO 8601 timestamp when the task was created.
3356.             </summary>
3357.         </member>
3358.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CancelMcpTaskResult.LastUpdatedAt">
3359.             <summary>
