3360.             Gets or sets the ISO 8601 timestamp when the task status was last updated.
3361.             </summary>
3362.         </member>
3363.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CancelMcpTaskResult.TimeToLive">
3364.             <summary>
3365.             Gets or sets the time to live (retention duration) from creation before the task may be deleted.
3366.             </summary>
3367.         </member>
3368.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CancelMcpTaskResult.PollInterval">
3369.             <summary>
3370.             Gets or sets the suggested time between status checks.
3371.             </summary>
3372.         </member>
3373.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ClientCapabilities">
3374.             <summary>
3375.             Represents the capabilities that a client supports.
3376.             </summary>
3377.             <remarks>
3378.             <para>
3379.             Capabilities define the features and functionality that a client can handle when communicating with an MCP server.
3380.             These are advertised to the server during the initialize handshake.
3381.             </para>
3382.             <para>
3383.             See the <see href="https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/specification/blob/main/schema/">schema</see> for details.
3384.             </para>
3385.             </remarks>
3386.         </member>
3387.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ClientCapabilities.Experimental">
3388.             <summary>
3389.             Gets or sets experimental, non-standard capabilities that the client supports.
3390.             </summary>
3391.             <remarks>
3392.             <para>
3393.             The <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ClientCapabilities.Experimental"/> dictionary allows clients to advertise support for features that are not yet
3394.             standardized in the Model Context Protocol specification. This extension mechanism enables
3395.             future protocol enhancements while maintaining backward compatibility.
3396.             </para>
3397.             <para>
3398.             Values in this dictionary are implementation-specific and should be coordinated between client
3399.             and server implementations. Servers should not assume the presence of any experimental capability
3400.             without checking for it first.
3401.             </para>
3402.             </remarks>
3403.         </member>
3404.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ClientCapabilities.Roots">
3405.             <summary>
3406.             Gets or sets the client's roots capability, which are entry points for resource navigation.
3407.             </summary>
3408.             <remarks>
3409.             <para>
3410.             When <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ClientCapabilities.Roots"/> is non-<see langword="null"/>, the client indicates that it can respond to
3411.             server requests for listing root URIs. Root URIs serve as entry points for resource navigation in the protocol.
3412.             </para>
3413.             <para>
3414.             The server can use <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServer.RequestRootsAsync(ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ListRootsRequestParams,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/> to request the list of
3415.             available roots from the client, which will trigger the client's <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClientHandlers.RootsHandler"/>.
3416.             </para>
3417.             </remarks>
3418.         </member>
3419.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ClientCapabilities.Sampling">
3420.             <summary>
3421.             Gets or sets the client's sampling capability, which indicates whether the client
3422.             supports issuing requests to an LLM on behalf of the server.
3423.             </summary>
3424.         </member>
3425.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ClientCapabilities.Elicitation">
3426.             <summary>
3427.             Gets or sets the client's elicitation capability, which indicates whether the client
3428.             supports elicitation of additional information from the user on behalf of the server.
3429.             </summary>
3430.         </member>
3431.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ClientCapabilities.Tasks">
3432.             <summary>
3433.             Gets or sets the client's tasks capability for supporting task-augmented requests.
3434.             </summary>
3435.             <remarks>
3436.             <para>
3437.             The tasks capability enables servers to augment their requests with tasks for long-running
3438.             operations. When present, servers can request that certain operations (like sampling or
3439.             elicitation) execute asynchronously, with the ability to poll for status and retrieve results later.
3440.             </para>
3441.             <para>
3442.             See <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTasksCapability"/> for details on configuring which operations support tasks.
3443.             </para>
3444.             </remarks>
3445.         </member>
3446.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ClientCapabilities.Extensions">
3447.             <summary>
3448.             Gets or sets optional MCP extensions that the client supports.
3449.             </summary>
3450.             <remarks>
3451.             <para>
3452.             Keys are extension identifiers in reverse domain notation with an extension name
3453.             (e.g., <c>"io.modelcontextprotocol/oauth-client-credentials"</c>), and values are
3454.             per-extension settings objects. An empty object indicates support with no additional settings.
3455.             </para>
3456.             <para>
3457.             Extensions provide a framework for extending the Model Context Protocol while maintaining
3458.             interoperability. Clients advertise extension support via this field during the initialization handshake.
3459.             </para>
3460.             </remarks>
3461.         </member>
3462.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CompleteContext">
3463.             <summary>
3464.             Represents additional context information for completion requests.
3465.             </summary>
3466.             <remarks>
3467.             This context provides information that helps the server generate more relevant 
3468.             completion suggestions, such as previously resolved variables in a template.
3469.             </remarks>
3470.         </member>
3471.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CompleteContext.Arguments">
3472.             <summary>
3473.             Gets or sets previously-resolved variables in a URI template or prompt.
3474.             </summary>
3475.         </member>
3476.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CompleteRequestParams">
3477.             <summary>
3478.             Represents the parameters used with a <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.CompletionComplete"/> request from 
3479.             a client to ask a server for auto-completion suggestions.
3480.             </summary>
3481.             <remarks>
3482.             <para>
3483.             <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.CompletionComplete"/> is used in the Model Context Protocol completion workflow
3484.             to provide intelligent suggestions for partial inputs related to resources, prompts, or other referenceable entities.
3485.             The completion mechanism in MCP allows clients to request suggestions based on partial inputs.
3486.             The server will respond with a <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CompleteResult"/> containing matching values.
3487.             </para>
3488.             <para>
3489.             See the <see href="https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/specification/blob/main/schema/">schema</see> for details.
3490.             </para>
3491.             </remarks>
3492.         </member>
3493.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CompleteRequestParams.Ref">
3494.             <summary>
3495.             Gets or sets the reference's information.
3496.             </summary>
3497.         </member>
3498.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CompleteRequestParams.Argument">
3499.             <summary>
3500.             Gets or sets the argument information for the completion request, specifying what is being completed
3501.             and the current partial input.
3502.             </summary>
3503.         </member>
3504.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CompleteRequestParams.Context">
3505.             <summary>
3506.             Gets or sets additional, optional context for completions.
3507.             </summary>
3508.         </member>
3509.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CompleteResult">
3510.             <summary>
3511.             Represents the server's response to a <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.CompletionComplete"/> request, 
3512.             containing suggested values for a given argument.
3513.             </summary>
3514.             <remarks>
3515.             <para>
3516.             <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CompleteResult"/> is returned by the server in response to a <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.CompletionComplete"/> 
3517.             request from the client. It provides suggested completions or valid values for a specific argument in a tool or resource reference.
3518.             </para>
3519.             <para>
3520.             The result contains a <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CompleteResult.Completion"/> object with suggested values, pagination information,
3521.             and the total number of available completions. This is similar to auto-completion functionality in code editors.
3522.             </para>
3523.             <para>
3524.             Clients typically use this to implement auto-suggestion features when users are inputting parameters
3525.             for tool calls or resource references.
3526.             </para>
3527.             <para>
3528.             See the <see href="https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/specification/blob/main/schema/">schema</see> for details.
3529.             </para>
3530.             </remarks>
3531.         </member>
3532.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CompleteResult.Completion">
3533.             <summary>
3534.             Gets or sets the completion object containing the suggested values and pagination information.
3535.             </summary>
3536.             <remarks>
3537.             If no completions are available for the given input, the <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Completion.Values"/> 
3538.             collection will be empty.
3539.             </remarks>
3540.         </member>
3541.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Completion">
3542.             <summary>
3543.             Represents a completion object in the server's response to a <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.CompletionComplete"/> request.
3544.             </summary>
3545.             <remarks>
3546.             See the <see href="https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/specification/blob/main/schema/">schema</see> for details.
3547.             </remarks>
3548.         </member>
3549.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Completion.Values">
3550.             <summary>
3551.             Gets or sets an array of completion values (auto-suggestions) for the requested input.
3552.             </summary>
3553.             <remarks>
3554.             This collection contains the actual text strings to be presented to users as completion suggestions.
3555.             The array will be empty if no suggestions are available for the current input.
3556.             Per the specification, this collection should not exceed 100 items.
3557.             </remarks>
3558.         </member>
3559.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Completion.Total">
3560.             <summary>
3561.             Gets or sets the total number of completion options available.
3562.             </summary>
3563.             <remarks>
3564.             This value can exceed the number of values actually sent in the response.
3565.             </remarks>
3566.         </member>
3567.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Completion.HasMore">
3568.             <summary>
3569.             Gets or sets a value that indicates whether there are additional completion options beyond
3570.             those provided in the current response, even if the exact total is unknown.
3571.             </summary>
3572.         </member>
3573.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CompletionsCapability">
3574.             <summary>
3575.             Represents the completions capability for providing auto-completion suggestions
3576.             for prompt arguments and resource references.
3577.             </summary>
3578.             <remarks>
3579.             <para>
3580.             When enabled, this capability allows a Model Context Protocol server to provide
3581.             auto-completion suggestions. This capability is advertised to clients during the initialize handshake.
3582.             </para>
3583.             <para>
3584.             The primary function of this capability is to improve the user experience by offering
3585.             contextual suggestions for argument values or resource identifiers based on partial input.
3586.             </para>
3587.             <para>
3588.             See the <see href="https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/specification/blob/main/schema/">schema</see> for details.
3589.             </para>
3590.             <para>
3591.             This class is intentionally empty as the Model Context Protocol specification does not
3592.             currently define additional properties for completions capabilities. Future versions of the
3593.             specification might extend this capability with additional configuration options.
3594.             </para>
3595.             </remarks>
3596.         </member>
3597.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ContentBlock">
3598.             <summary>
3599.             Represents content within the Model Context Protocol (MCP).
3600.             </summary>
3601.             <remarks>
3602.             <para>
3603.             The <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ContentBlock"/> class is a fundamental type in the MCP that can represent different forms of content
3604.             based on the <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ContentBlock.Type"/> property. Derived types like <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.TextContentBlock"/>, <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ImageContentBlock"/>,
3605.             and <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.EmbeddedResourceBlock"/> provide the type-specific content.
3606.             </para>
3607.             <para>
3608.             This class is used throughout the MCP for representing content in messages, tool responses,
3609.             and other communication between clients and servers.
3610.             </para>
3611.             <para>
3612.             See the <see href="https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/specification/blob/main/schema/">schema</see> for more details.
3613.             </para>
3614.             </remarks>
3615.         </member>
3616.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ContentBlock.#ctor">
3617.             <summary>Prevent external derivations.</summary>
3618.         </member>
3619.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ContentBlock.Type">
3620.             <summary>
3621.             When overridden in a derived class, gets the type of content.
3622.             </summary>
3623.             <value>
3624.             The type of content. Valid values include "image", "audio", "text", "resource", "resource_link", "tool_use", and "tool_result".
3625.             </value>
3626.             <remarks>
3627.             This value determines the structure of the content object.
3628.             </remarks>
3629.         </member>
3630.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ContentBlock.Annotations">
3631.             <summary>
3632.             Gets or sets optional annotations for the content.
3633.             </summary>
3634.             <remarks>
3635.             These annotations can be used to specify the intended audience (<see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Role.User"/>, <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Role.Assistant"/>, or both)
3636.             and the priority level of the content. Clients can use this information to filter or prioritize content for different roles.
3637.             </remarks>
3638.         </member>
3639.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ContentBlock.Meta">
3640.             <summary>
3641.             Gets or sets metadata reserved by MCP for protocol-level metadata.
3642.             </summary>
3643.             <remarks>
3644.             Implementations must not make assumptions about its contents.
3645.             </remarks>
3646.         </member>
3647.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ContentBlock.Converter">
3648.             <summary>
3649.             Provides a <see cref="T:System.Text.Json.Serialization.JsonConverter"/> for <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ContentBlock"/>.
3650.             </summary>
3651.             <remarks>
3652.             Provides a polymorphic converter for the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ContentBlock"/> class that doesn't require
3653.             setting <see cref="P:System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions.AllowOutOfOrderMetadataProperties"/> explicitly.
3654.             </remarks>
3655.         </member>
3656.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ContentBlock.Converter.Read(System.Text.Json.Utf8JsonReader@,System.Type,System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions)">
3657.             <inheritdoc/>
3658.         </member>
3659.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ContentBlock.Converter.Write(System.Text.Json.Utf8JsonWriter,ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ContentBlock,System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions)">
3660.             <inheritdoc/>
3661.         </member>
3662.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.TextContentBlock">
3663.             <summary>Represents text provided to or from an LLM.</summary>
3664.         </member>
3665.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.TextContentBlock.Type">
3666.             <inheritdoc/>
3667.         </member>
3668.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.TextContentBlock.Text">
3669.             <summary>
3670.             Gets or sets the text content of the message.
3671.             </summary>
3672.         </member>
3673.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.TextContentBlock.ToString">
3674.             <inheritdoc/>
3675.         </member>
3676.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ImageContentBlock">
3677.             <summary>Represents an image provided to or from an LLM.</summary>
3678.         </member>
3679.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ImageContentBlock.FromBytes(System.ReadOnlyMemory{System.Byte},System.String)">
3680.             <summary>
3681.             Creates an <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ImageContentBlock"/> from decoded image bytes.
3682.             </summary>
3683.             <param name="bytes">The unencoded image bytes.</param>
3684.             <param name="mimeType">The MIME type of the image.</param>
3685.             <returns>A new <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ImageContentBlock"/> instance.</returns>
3686.             <remarks>
3687.             This method stores the provided bytes as <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ImageContentBlock.DecodedData"/> and lazily encodes them to base64 UTF-8 bytes for <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ImageContentBlock.Data"/>.
3688.             </remarks>
3689.             <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="mimeType"/> is <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
3690.             <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentException"><paramref name="mimeType"/> is empty or composed entirely of whitespace.</exception>
3691.         </member>
3692.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ImageContentBlock.#ctor">
3693.             <summary>Initializes a new instance of the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ImageContentBlock"/> class.</summary>
3694.         </member>
3695.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ImageContentBlock.Type">
3696.             <inheritdoc/>
3697.         </member>
3698.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ImageContentBlock.Data">
3699.             <summary>
3700.             Gets or sets the base64-encoded UTF-8 bytes representing the image data.
3701.             </summary>
3702.             <remarks>
3703.             Setting this value will invalidate any cached value of <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ImageContentBlock.DecodedData"/>.
3704.             </remarks>
3705.         </member>
3706.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ImageContentBlock.DecodedData">
3707.             <summary>
3708.             Gets the decoded image data represented by <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ImageContentBlock.Data"/>.
3709.             </summary>
3710.             <remarks>
3711.             <para>
3712.             When getting, this member will decode the value in <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ImageContentBlock.Data"/> and cache the result.
3713.             Subsequent accesses return the cached value unless <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ImageContentBlock.Data"/> is modified.
3714.             </para>
3715.             </remarks>
3716.         </member>
3717.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ImageContentBlock.MimeType">
3718.             <summary>
3719.             Gets or sets the MIME type (or "media type") of the content, specifying the format of the data.
3720.             </summary>
3721.             <remarks>
3722.             Common values include "image/png" and "image/jpeg".
3723.             </remarks>
3724.         </member>
3725.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.AudioContentBlock">
3726.             <summary>Represents audio provided to or from an LLM.</summary>
3727.         </member>
3728.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.AudioContentBlock.FromBytes(System.ReadOnlyMemory{System.Byte},System.String)">
3729.             <summary>
3730.             Creates an <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.AudioContentBlock"/> from decoded audio bytes.
3731.             </summary>
3732.             <param name="bytes">The unencoded audio bytes.</param>
3733.             <param name="mimeType">The MIME type of the audio.</param>
3734.             <returns>A new <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.AudioContentBlock"/> instance.</returns>
3735.             <remarks>
3736.             This method stores the provided bytes as <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.AudioContentBlock.DecodedData"/> and lazily encodes them to base64 UTF-8 bytes for <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.AudioContentBlock.Data"/>.
3737.             </remarks>
3738.             <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="mimeType"/> is <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
3739.             <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentException"><paramref name="mimeType"/> is empty or composed entirely of whitespace.</exception>
3740.         </member>
3741.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.AudioContentBlock.#ctor">
3742.             <summary>Initializes a new instance of the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.AudioContentBlock"/> class.</summary>
3743.         </member>
3744.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.AudioContentBlock.Type">
3745.             <inheritdoc/>
3746.         </member>
3747.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.AudioContentBlock.Data">
3748.             <summary>
3749.             Gets or sets the base64-encoded UTF-8 bytes representing the audio data.
3750.             </summary>
3751.             <remarks>
3752.             Setting this value will invalidate any cached value of <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.AudioContentBlock.DecodedData"/>.
3753.             </remarks>
3754.         </member>
3755.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.AudioContentBlock.DecodedData">
3756.             <summary>
3757.             Gets the decoded audio data represented by <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.AudioContentBlock.Data"/>.
3758.             </summary>
3759.             <remarks>
3760.             <para>
3761.             When getting, this member will decode the value in <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.AudioContentBlock.Data"/> and cache the result.
3762.             Subsequent accesses return the cached value unless <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.AudioContentBlock.Data"/> is modified.
3763.             </para>
3764.             </remarks>
3765.         </member>
3766.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.AudioContentBlock.MimeType">
3767.             <summary>
3768.             Gets or sets the MIME type (or "media type") of the content, specifying the format of the data.
3769.             </summary>
3770.             <remarks>
3771.             Common values include "audio/wav" and "audio/mp3".
3772.             </remarks>
3773.         </member>
3774.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.EmbeddedResourceBlock">
3775.             <summary>Represents the contents of a resource, embedded into a prompt or tool call result.</summary>
3776.             <remarks>
3777.             It is up to the client how best to render embedded resources for the benefit of the LLM and/or the user.
3778.             </remarks>
3779.         </member>
3780.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.EmbeddedResourceBlock.Type">
3781.             <inheritdoc/>
3782.         </member>
3783.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.EmbeddedResourceBlock.Resource">
3784.             <summary>
3785.             Gets or sets the resource content of the message when <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.EmbeddedResourceBlock.Type"/> is "resource".
3786.             </summary>
3787.             <remarks>
3788.             <para>
3789.             Resources can be either text-based (<see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.TextResourceContents"/>) or
3790.             binary (<see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.BlobResourceContents"/>), allowing for flexible data representation.
3791.             Each resource has a URI that can be used for identification and retrieval.
3792.             </para>
3793.             </remarks>
3794.         </member>
3795.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ResourceLinkBlock">
3796.             <summary>Represents a resource that the server is capable of reading, included in a prompt or tool call result.</summary>
3797.             <remarks>
3798.             Resource links returned by tools are not guaranteed to appear in the results of `resources/list` requests.
3799.             </remarks>
3800.         </member>
3801.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ResourceLinkBlock.Type">
3802.             <inheritdoc/>
3803.         </member>
3804.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ResourceLinkBlock.Uri">
3805.             <summary>
3806.             Gets or sets the URI of this resource.
3807.             </summary>
3808.         </member>
3809.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ResourceLinkBlock.Name">
3810.             <summary>
3811.             Gets or sets a human-readable name for this resource.
3812.             </summary>
3813.         </member>
3814.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ResourceLinkBlock.Title">
3815.             <summary>
3816.             Gets or sets a title for this resource.
3817.             </summary>
3818.             <remarks>
3819.             This is intended for UI and end-user contexts. It is optimized to be human-readable and easily understood,
3820.             even by those unfamiliar with domain-specific terminology.
3821.             If not provided, <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ResourceLinkBlock.Name"/> can be used for display.
3822.             </remarks>
3823.         </member>
3824.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ResourceLinkBlock.Description">
3825.             <summary>
3826.             Gets or sets a description of what this resource represents.
3827.             </summary>
3828.             <remarks>
3829.             <para>
3830.             This description can be used by clients to improve the LLM's understanding of available resources. It can be thought of like a \"hint\" to the model.
3831.             </para>
3832.             <para>
3833.             The description should provide clear context about the resource's content, format, and purpose.
3834.             This helps AI models make better decisions about when to access or reference the resource.
3835.             </para>
3836.             <para>
3837.             Client applications can also use this description for display purposes in user interfaces
3838.             or to help users understand the available resources.
3839.             </para>
3840.             </remarks>
3841.         </member>
3842.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ResourceLinkBlock.MimeType">
3843.             <summary>
3844.             Gets or sets the MIME type of this resource.
3845.             </summary>
3846.             <remarks>
3847.             <para>
3848.             <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ResourceLinkBlock.MimeType"/> specifies the format of the resource content, helping clients to properly interpret and display the data.
3849.             Common MIME types include "text/plain" for plain text, "application/pdf" for PDF documents,
3850.             "image/png" for PNG images, and "application/json" for JSON data.
3851.             </para>
3852.             <para>
3853.             This property can be <see langword="null"/> if the MIME type is unknown or not applicable for the resource.
3854.             </para>
3855.             </remarks>
3856.         </member>
3857.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ResourceLinkBlock.Size">
3858.             <summary>
3859.             Gets or sets the size of the raw resource content (before base64 encoding), in bytes, if known.
3860.             </summary>
3861.             <remarks>
3862.             This value can be used by applications to display file sizes and estimate context window usage.
3863.             </remarks>
3864.         </member>
3865.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ResourceLinkBlock.Icons">
3866.             <summary>
3867.             Gets or sets an optional list of icons for this resource.
3868.             </summary>
3869.             <remarks>
3870.             This can be used by clients to display the resource's icon in a user interface.
3871.             </remarks>
3872.         </member>
3873.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ToolUseContentBlock">
3874.             <summary>Represents a request from the assistant to call a tool.</summary>
3875.         </member>
3876.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ToolUseContentBlock.Type">
3877.             <inheritdoc/>
3878.         </member>
3879.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ToolUseContentBlock.Id">
3880.             <summary>
3881.             Gets or sets a unique identifier for this tool use.
3882.             </summary>
3883.             <remarks>
3884.             This ID is used to match tool results to their corresponding tool uses.
3885.             </remarks>
3886.         </member>
3887.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ToolUseContentBlock.Name">
3888.             <summary>
3889.             Gets or sets the name of the tool to call.
3890.             </summary>
3891.         </member>
3892.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ToolUseContentBlock.Input">
3893.             <summary>
3894.             Gets or sets the arguments to pass to the tool, conforming to the tool's input schema.
3895.             </summary>
3896.         </member>
3897.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ToolResultContentBlock">
3898.             <summary>Represents the result of a tool use, provided by the user back to the assistant.</summary>
3899.         </member>
3900.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ToolResultContentBlock.Type">
3901.             <inheritdoc/>
3902.         </member>
3903.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ToolResultContentBlock.ToolUseId">
3904.             <summary>
3905.             Gets or sets the ID of the tool use this result corresponds to.
3906.             </summary>
3907.             <remarks>
3908.             This value must match the ID from a previous <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ToolUseContentBlock"/>.
3909.             </remarks>
3910.         </member>
3911.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ToolResultContentBlock.Content">
3912.             <summary>
3913.             Gets or sets the unstructured result content of the tool use.
3914.             </summary>
3915.             <remarks>
3916.             This value has the same format as CallToolResult.Content and can include text, images,
3917.             audio, resource links, and embedded resources.
3918.             </remarks>
3919.         </member>
3920.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ToolResultContentBlock.StructuredContent">
3921.             <summary>
3922.             Gets or sets an optional structured result object.
3923.             </summary>
3924.             <remarks>
3925.             If the tool defined an outputSchema, this object should conform to that schema.
3926.             </remarks>
3927.         </member>
3928.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ToolResultContentBlock.IsError">
3929.             <summary>
3930.             Gets or sets a value that indicates whether the tool use resulted in an error.
3931.             </summary>
3932.             <value>
3933.             <see langword="true"/> if the tool use resulted in an error; <see langword="false"/> if it succeeded. The default is <see langword="false"/>.
3934.             </value>
3935.             <remarks>
3936.             If <see langword="true"/>, the content typically describes the error that occurred.
3937.             </remarks>
3938.         </member>
3939.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ContextInclusion">
3940.             <summary>
3941.             Specifies the context inclusion options for a request in the Model Context Protocol (MCP).
3942.             </summary>
3943.             <remarks>
3944.             <para>
3945.             See the <see href="https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/specification/blob/main/schema/">schema</see> for details.
3946.             </para>
3947.             <para>
3948.             <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ContextInclusion"/>, and in particular <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ContextInclusion.ThisServer"/> and <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ContextInclusion.AllServers"/>, are deprecated.
3949.             Servers should only use these values if the client declares <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ClientCapabilities.Sampling"/> with
3950.             <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.SamplingCapability.Context"/> set. These values might be removed in future spec releases.
3951.             </para>
3952.             </remarks>
3953.         </member>
3954.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ContextInclusion.None">
3955.             <summary>
3956.             No context should be included.
3957.             </summary>
3958.         </member>
3959.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ContextInclusion.ThisServer">
3960.             <summary>
3961.             Context from the server that sent the request should be included.
3962.             </summary>
3963.             <remarks>
3964.             This value is soft-deprecated. Servers should only use this value if the client
3965.             declares ClientCapabilities.Sampling.Context.
3966.             </remarks>
3967.         </member>
3968.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ContextInclusion.AllServers">
3969.             <summary>
3970.             Context from all servers that the client is connected to should be included.
3971.             </summary>
3972.             <remarks>
3973.             This value is soft-deprecated. Servers should only use this value if the client
3974.             declares ClientCapabilities.Sampling.Context.
3975.             </remarks>
3976.         </member>
3977.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CreateMessageRequestParams">
3978.             <summary>
3979.             Represents the parameters used with a <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.SamplingCreateMessage"/>
3980.             request from a server to sample an LLM via the client.
3981.             </summary>
3982.             <remarks>
3983.             See the <see href="https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/specification/blob/main/schema/">schema</see> for details.
3984.             </remarks>
3985.         </member>
3986.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CreateMessageRequestParams.IncludeContext">
3987.             <summary>
3988.             Gets or sets an indication as to which server contexts should be included in the prompt.
3989.             </summary>
3990.             <remarks>
3991.             <para>
3992.             The client might ignore this request.
3993.             </para>
3994.             <para>
3995.             <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ContextInclusion"/>, and in particular <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ContextInclusion.ThisServer"/> and
3996.             <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ContextInclusion.AllServers"/>, are deprecated. Servers should only use these values if the client
3997.             declares <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ClientCapabilities.Sampling"/> with <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.SamplingCapability.Context"/> set.
3998.             These values might be removed in future spec releases.
3999.             </para>
4000.             </remarks>
4001.         </member>
4002.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CreateMessageRequestParams.MaxTokens">
4003.             <summary>
4004.             Gets or sets the maximum number of tokens to generate in the LLM response, as requested by the server.
4005.             </summary>
4006.             <remarks>
4007.             <para>
4008.             A token is generally a word or part of a word in the text. Setting this value helps control
4009.             response length and computation time. The client can choose to sample fewer tokens than requested.
4010.             </para>
4011.             <para>
4012.             The client must respect the <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CreateMessageRequestParams.MaxTokens"/> parameter.
4013.             </para>
4014.             </remarks>
4015.         </member>
4016.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CreateMessageRequestParams.Messages">
4017.             <summary>
4018.             Gets or sets the messages requested by the server to be included in the prompt.
4019.             </summary>
4020.             <remarks>
4021.             The list of messages in a sampling request should not be retained between separate requests.
4022.             </remarks>
4023.         </member>
4024.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CreateMessageRequestParams.Metadata">
4025.             <summary>
4026.             Gets or sets optional metadata to pass through to the LLM provider.
4027.             </summary>
4028.             <remarks>
4029.             <para>
4030.             The format of this metadata is provider-specific and can include model-specific settings or
4031.             configuration that isn't covered by standard parameters. This allows for passing custom parameters
4032.             that are specific to certain AI models or providers.
4033.             </para>
4034.             <para>
4035.             The client may modify or ignore metadata.
4036.             </para>
4037.             </remarks>
4038.         </member>
4039.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CreateMessageRequestParams.ModelPreferences">
4040.             <summary>
4041.             Gets or sets the server's preferences for which model to select.
4042.             </summary>
4043.             <remarks>
4044.             <para>
4045.             The client might ignore these preferences.
4046.             </para>
4047.             <para>
4048.             These preferences help the client make an appropriate model selection based on the server's priorities
4049.             for cost, speed, intelligence, and specific model hints.
4050.             </para>
4051.             <para>
4052.             When multiple dimensions are specified (cost, speed, intelligence), the client should balance these
4053.             based on their relative values. If specific model hints are provided, the client should evaluate them
4054.             in order and prioritize them over numeric priorities.
4055.             </para>
4056.             </remarks>
4057.         </member>
4058.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CreateMessageRequestParams.StopSequences">
4059.             <summary>
4060.             Gets or sets optional sequences of characters that signal the LLM to stop generating text when encountered.
4061.             </summary>
4062.             <remarks>
4063.             <para>
4064.             When the model generates any of these sequences during sampling, text generation stops immediately,
4065.             even if the maximum token limit hasn't been reached. This behavior is useful for controlling generation
4066.             endings or preventing the model from continuing beyond certain points.
4067.             </para>
4068.             <para>
4069.             Stop sequences are typically case-sensitive, and the LLM will only stop generation when a produced
4070.             sequence exactly matches one of the provided sequences. Common uses include ending markers like "END", punctuation
4071.             like ".", or special delimiter sequences like "###".
4072.             </para>
4073.             <para>
4074.             The client may modify or ignore stop sequences.
4075.             </para>
4076.             </remarks>
4077.         </member>
4078.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CreateMessageRequestParams.SystemPrompt">
4079.             <summary>
4080.             Gets or sets an optional system prompt the server wants to use for sampling.
4081.             </summary>
4082.             <remarks>
4083.             The client might modify or omit this prompt.
4084.             </remarks>
4085.         </member>
4086.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CreateMessageRequestParams.Temperature">
4087.             <summary>
4088.             Gets or sets the temperature to use for sampling, as requested by the server.
4089.             </summary>
4090.             <remarks>
4091.             <para>
4092.             Temperature controls randomness in model responses. Higher values produce higher randomness,
4093.             and lower values produce more stable output. The valid range depends on the model provider.
4094.             </para>
4095.             <para>
4096.             The client may modify or ignore this value.
4097.             </para>
4098.             </remarks>
4099.         </member>
4100.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CreateMessageRequestParams.Tools">
4101.             <summary>
4102.             Gets or sets tools that the model can use during generation.
4103.             </summary>
4104.             <remarks>
4105.             The tool definitions in this array are scoped to this sampling request.
4106.             They do not need to correspond to tools registered on the server via <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.ToolsList"/>.
4107.             </remarks>
4108.         </member>
4109.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CreateMessageRequestParams.ToolChoice">
4110.             <summary>
4111.             Gets or sets controls for how the model uses tools.
4112.             </summary>
4113.             <remarks>
4114.             This controls whether and how the model uses the request-scoped <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CreateMessageRequestParams.Tools"/> during sampling.
4115.             </remarks>
4116.         </member>
4117.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CreateMessageRequestParams.Task">
4118.             <summary>
4119.             Gets or sets optional task metadata to augment this request with task execution.
4120.             </summary>
4121.             <remarks>
4122.             When present, indicates that the requestor wants this operation executed as a task.
4123.             The receiver must support task augmentation for this specific request type.
4124.             </remarks>
4125.         </member>
4126.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CreateMessageResult">
4127.             <summary>
4128.             Represents a client's response to a <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.SamplingCreateMessage"/> from the server.
4129.             </summary>
4130.             <remarks>
4131.             See the <see href="https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/specification/blob/main/schema/">schema</see> for details.
4132.             </remarks>
4133.         </member>
4134.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CreateMessageResult.Content">
4135.             <summary>
4136.             Gets or sets the content of the assistant's response.
4137.             </summary>
4138.             <remarks>
4139.             In the corresponding JSON, this might be a single content block or an array of content blocks.
4140.             </remarks>
4141.         </member>
4142.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CreateMessageResult.Model">
4143.             <summary>
4144.             Gets or sets the name of the model that generated the message.
4145.             </summary>
4146.             <remarks>
4147.             <para>
4148.             This value should contain the specific model identifier such as "claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022" or "o3-mini".
4149.             </para>
4150.             <para>
4151.             This property allows the server to know which model was used to generate the response,
4152.             enabling appropriate handling based on the model's capabilities and characteristics.
4153.             </para>
4154.             </remarks>
4155.         </member>
4156.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CreateMessageResult.StopReason">
4157.             <summary>
4158.             Gets or sets the reason why message generation (sampling) stopped, if known.
4159.             </summary>
4160.             <remarks>
4161.             Standard values include:
4162.             <list type="bullet">
4163.               <item><term>endTurn</term><description>The participant is yielding the conversation to the other party.</description></item>
4164.               <item><term>maxTokens</term><description>The response was truncated due to reaching token limits.</description></item>
4165.               <item><term>stopSequence</term><description>A specific stop sequence was encountered during generation.</description></item>
4166.               <item><term>toolUse</term><description>The model wants to use one or more tools.</description></item>
4167.             </list>
4168.             This field is an open string to allow for provider-specific stop reasons.
4169.             </remarks>
4170.         </member>
4171.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CreateMessageResult.Role">
4172.             <summary>
4173.             Gets or sets the role of the user who generated the message.
4174.             </summary>
4175.             <value>
4176.             The role of the user who generated the message. The default is <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Role.Assistant"/>.
4177.             </value>
4178.         </member>
4179.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CreateMessageResult.StopReasonEndTurn">
4180.             <summary>The stop reason "endTurn".</summary>
4181.         </member>
4182.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CreateMessageResult.StopReasonMaxTokens">
4183.             <summary>The stop reason "maxTokens".</summary>
4184.         </member>
4185.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CreateMessageResult.StopReasonStopSequence">
4186.             <summary>The stop reason "stopSequence".</summary>
4187.         </member>
4188.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CreateMessageResult.StopReasonToolUse">
4189.             <summary>The stop reason "toolUse".</summary>
4190.         </member>
4191.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CreateTaskResult">
4192.             <summary>
4193.             Represents the response to a task-augmented request.
4194.             </summary>
4195.             <remarks>
4196.             <para>
4197.             When a client sends a request with a <c>task</c> parameter, the server immediately returns
4198.             a <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CreateTaskResult"/> containing the created task information instead of the
4199.             normal result type. The actual result can be retrieved later via <c>tasks/result</c>.
4200.             </para>
4201.             <para>
4202.             This type is returned for any task-augmented request including <c>tools/call</c>,
4203.             <c>sampling/createMessage</c>, and <c>elicitation/create</c>.
4204.             </para>
4205.             </remarks>
4206.         </member>
4207.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CreateTaskResult.Task">
4208.             <summary>
4209.             Gets or sets the task data for the newly created task.
4210.             </summary>
4211.         </member>
4212.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.DebuggerDisplayHelper">
4213.             <summary>
4214.             Internal helper methods for DebuggerDisplay implementations.
4215.             </summary>
4216.         </member>
4217.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.DebuggerDisplayHelper.GetBase64LengthDisplay(System.String)">
4218.             <summary>
4219.             Gets the decoded length of base64 data for debugger display.
4220.             </summary>
4221.         </member>
4222.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.DebuggerDisplayHelper.GetBase64LengthDisplay(System.ReadOnlyMemory{System.Byte})">
4223.             <summary>
4224.             Gets the decoded length of base64 data encoded as UTF-8 bytes for debugger display.
4225.             </summary>
4226.         </member>
4227.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ElicitationCapability">
4228.             <summary>
4229.             Represents the capability for a client to provide server-requested additional information during interactions.
4230.             </summary>
4231.             <remarks>
4232.             <para>
4233.             This capability enables the MCP client to respond to elicitation requests from an MCP server.
4234.             Clients must support at least one elicitation mode: form (in-band) or url (out-of-band via URL).
4235.             </para>
4236.             <para>
4237.             When this capability is enabled, an MCP server can request the client to provide additional information
4238.             during interactions. The client must set a <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClientHandlers.ElicitationHandler"/> to process these requests.
4239.             </para>
4240.             <para>
4241.             Two modes of elicitation are supported:
4242.             <list type="bullet">
4243.               <item><description><b>form</b>: In-band elicitation where data is collected via a form and exposed to the client</description></item>
4244.               <item><description><b>url</b>: URL mode (out-of-band) elicitation via navigation where sensitive data is not exposed to the client</description></item>
4245.             </list>
4246.             </para>
4247.             </remarks>
4248.         </member>
4249.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ElicitationCapability.Form">
4250.             <summary>
4251.             Gets or sets the form mode elicitation (in-band) capability, indicating support for in-band elicitation.
4252.             </summary>
4253.             <remarks>
4254.             When present, indicates the client supports form mode elicitation where structured data
4255.             is collected through a form interface and returned to the server.
4256.             </remarks>
4257.         </member>
4258.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ElicitationCapability.Url">
4259.             <summary>
4260.             Gets or sets the URL mode (out-of-band) elicitation capability.
4261.             </summary>
4262.             <remarks>
4263.             When present, indicates the client supports URL mode elicitation for secure out-of-band
4264.             interactions such as OAuth flows, payments, or collecting sensitive credentials.
4265.             </remarks>
4266.         </member>
4267.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ElicitationCapability.Converter">
4268.             <summary>
4269.             Provides a converter that normalizes blank capability objects to imply form support.
4270.             </summary>
4271.         </member>
4272.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ElicitationCapability.Converter.Read(System.Text.Json.Utf8JsonReader@,System.Type,System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions)">
4273.             <inheritdoc />
4274.         </member>
4275.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ElicitationCapability.Converter.Write(System.Text.Json.Utf8JsonWriter,ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ElicitationCapability,System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions)">
4276.             <inheritdoc />
4277.         </member>
4278.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ElicitationCompleteNotificationParams">
4279.             <summary>
4280.             Represents the parameters used with a <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.NotificationMethods.ElicitationCompleteNotification"/>
4281.             notification emitted after a URL-mode elicitation finishes out-of-band.
4282.             </summary>
4283.             <remarks>
4284.             <para>
4285.             The payload references the original elicitation by ID so that clients can resume deferred
4286.             requests or update pending UI once the external flow completes.
4287.             </para>
4288.             </remarks>
4289.         </member>
4290.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ElicitationCompleteNotificationParams.ElicitationId">
4291.             <summary>
4292.             Gets or sets the unique identifier of the elicitation that completed.
4293.             </summary>
4294.             <remarks>
4295.             This matches <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ElicitRequestParams.ElicitationId"/> from the originating request and allows
4296.             clients to correlate the completion notification with previously issued prompts.
4297.             </remarks>
4298.         </member>
4299.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ElicitRequestParams">
4300.             <summary>
4301.             Represents a message issued from the server to elicit additional information from the user via the client.
4302.             </summary>
4303.         </member>
4304.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ElicitRequestParams.Mode">
4305.             <summary>
4306.             Gets or sets the elicitation mode: "form" for in-band data collection or "url" for out-of-band URL navigation.
4307.             </summary>
4308.             <remarks>
4309.             <list type="bullet">
4310.               <item><description><b>form</b>: Client collects structured data via a form interface. Data is exposed to the client.</description></item>
4311.               <item><description><b>url</b>: Client navigates user to a URL for out-of-band interaction. Sensitive data is not exposed to the client.</description></item>
4312.             </list>
4313.             </remarks>
4314.             <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentException">The value is not "form" or "url".</exception>
4315.         </member>
4316.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ElicitRequestParams.ElicitationId">
4317.             <summary>
4318.             Gets or sets a unique identifier for this elicitation request.
4319.             </summary>
4320.             <remarks>
4321.             <para>
4322.             Used to track and correlate the elicitation across multiple messages, especially for out-of-band flows
4323.             that may complete asynchronously.
4324.             </para>
4325.             <para>
4326.             Required for url mode elicitation to enable progress tracking and completion detection.
4327.             </para>
4328.             </remarks>
4329.         </member>
4330.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ElicitRequestParams.Url">
4331.             <summary>
4332.             Gets or sets the URL to navigate to for out-of-band elicitation.
4333.             </summary>
4334.             <remarks>
4335.             <para>
4336.             Required when <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ElicitRequestParams.Mode"/> is "url". The client should prompt the user for consent
4337.             and then navigate to this URL in a user-agent (browser) where the user completes
4338.             the required interaction.
4339.             </para>
4340.             <para>
4341.             URLs must not appear in any other field of the elicitation request for security reasons.
4342.             </para>
4343.             </remarks>
4344.         </member>
4345.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ElicitRequestParams.Message">
4346.             <summary>
4347.             Gets or sets the message to present to the user.
4348.             </summary>
4349.             <remarks>
4350.             For form mode, this describes what information is being requested.
4351.             For url mode, this explains why the user needs to navigate to the URL.
4352.             </remarks>
4353.         </member>
4354.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ElicitRequestParams.RequestedSchema">
4355.             <summary>
4356.             Gets or sets the requested schema for form mode elicitation.
4357.             </summary>
4358.             <remarks>
4359.             Only applicable when <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ElicitRequestParams.Mode"/> is "form".
4360.             </remarks>
4361.             <value>
4362.             Possible values are <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ElicitRequestParams.StringSchema"/>, <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ElicitRequestParams.NumberSchema"/>, <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ElicitRequestParams.BooleanSchema"/>,
4363.             <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ElicitRequestParams.UntitledSingleSelectEnumSchema"/>, <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ElicitRequestParams.TitledSingleSelectEnumSchema"/>,
4364.             <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ElicitRequestParams.UntitledMultiSelectEnumSchema"/>, <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ElicitRequestParams.TitledMultiSelectEnumSchema"/>,
4365.             and <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ElicitRequestParams.LegacyTitledEnumSchema"/> (deprecated).
4366.             </value>
4367.         </member>
4368.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ElicitRequestParams.Task">
4369.             <summary>
4370.             Gets or sets optional task metadata to augment this request with task execution.
4371.             </summary>
4372.             <remarks>
4373.             When present, indicates that the requestor wants this operation executed as a task.
4374.             The receiver must support task augmentation for this specific request type.
4375.             </remarks>
4376.         </member>
4377.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ElicitRequestParams.RequestSchema">
4378.             <summary>Represents a request schema used in a form mode elicitation request.</summary>
4379.         </member>
4380.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ElicitRequestParams.RequestSchema.Type">
4381.             <summary>Gets the type of the schema.</summary>
4382.             <remarks>This value is always "object".</remarks>
4383.         </member>
4384.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ElicitRequestParams.RequestSchema.Properties">
4385.             <summary>Gets or sets the properties of the schema.</summary>
4386.         </member>
4387.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ElicitRequestParams.RequestSchema.Required">
4388.             <summary>Gets or sets the required properties of the schema.</summary>
4389.         </member>

4394.             <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ElicitRequestParams.UntitledSingleSelectEnumSchema"/>, <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ElicitRequestParams.TitledSingleSelectEnumSchema"/>,
4395.             <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ElicitRequestParams.UntitledMultiSelectEnumSchema"/>, <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ElicitRequestParams.TitledMultiSelectEnumSchema"/>,
4396.             or <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ElicitRequestParams.LegacyTitledEnumSchema"/> (deprecated).
4397.             </summary>
4398.         </member>
4399.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ElicitRequestParams.PrimitiveSchemaDefinition.#ctor">

4402.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ElicitRequestParams.PrimitiveSchemaDefinition.GetDefaultAsJsonElement">
4403.             <summary>Gets the default value for this schema as a <see cref="T:System.Text.Json.JsonElement"/>, if one is defined.</summary>
4404.         </member>
4405.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ElicitRequestParams.PrimitiveSchemaDefinition.Type">
4406.             <summary>Gets or sets the type of the schema.</summary>
4407.         </member>
4408.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ElicitRequestParams.PrimitiveSchemaDefinition.Title">
4409.             <summary>Gets or sets a title for the schema.</summary>
4410.         </member>
4411.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ElicitRequestParams.PrimitiveSchemaDefinition.Description">
4412.             <summary>Gets or sets a description for the schema.</summary>
4413.         </member>
4414.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ElicitRequestParams.PrimitiveSchemaDefinition.Converter">
4415.             <summary>
4416.             Provides a <see cref="T:System.Text.Json.Serialization.JsonConverter"/> for <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ElicitRequestParams.PrimitiveSchemaDefinition"/>.

4594.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ElicitResult.Action">
4595.             <summary>
4596.             Gets or sets the user action in response to the elicitation.
4597.             </summary>
4598.             <value>
4599.             Defaults to "cancel" if not explicitly set.
4600.             </value>
4601.             <remarks>
4602.             <list type="bullet">
4603.               <item>
4604.                 <term>"accept"</term>
4605.                 <description>User submitted the form/confirmed the action</description>
4606.               </item>
4607.               <item>
4608.                 <term>"decline"</term>
4609.                 <description>User explicitly declined the action</description>
4610.               </item>
4611.               <item>
4612.                 <term>"cancel"</term>
4613.                 <description>User dismissed without making an explicit choice (default)</description>
4614.               </item>
4615.             </list>
4616.             </remarks>
4617.         </member>
4618.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ElicitResult.IsAccepted">
4619.             <summary>
4620.             Gets a value that indicates whether the elicitation was accepted by the user.
4621.             </summary>
4622.             <remarks>
4623.             If <see langword="true"/>, it indicates that the elicitation request completed successfully and the value of <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ElicitResult.Content"/> has been populated with a value.
4624.             </remarks>
4625.         </member>
4626.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ElicitResult.Content">

4629.             </summary>
4630.             <remarks>

4633.             </para>
4634.             <para>
4635.             Values in the dictionary should be of types <see cref="F:System.Text.Json.JsonValueKind.String"/>, <see cref="F:System.Text.Json.JsonValueKind.Number"/>,
4636.             <see cref="F:System.Text.Json.JsonValueKind.True"/>, <see cref="F:System.Text.Json.JsonValueKind.False"/>, or <see cref="F:System.Text.Json.JsonValueKind.Array"/> (for multi-select enums).
4637.             </para>
4638.             </remarks>
4639.         </member>

4766.             <remarks>
4767.             <para>
4768.             This request blocks until the task reaches a terminal status (<see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatus.Completed"/>,
4769.             <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatus.Failed"/>, or <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatus.Cancelled"/>).
4770.             </para>
4771.             <para>
4772.             The result structure matches the original request type (e.g., <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolResult"/> for tools/call).
4773.             This is distinct from the initial <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Result"/> response, which contains only task data.
4774.             </para>
