4860.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Icon">
4861.             <summary>
4862.             Represents an icon that can be used to visually identify an implementation, resource, tool, or prompt.
4863.             </summary>
4864.             <remarks>
4865.             <para>
4866.             Icons enhance user interfaces by providing visual context and improving the discoverability of available functionality.
4867.             Each icon includes a source URI pointing to the icon resource, and optional MIME type and size information.
4868.             </para>
4869.             <para>
4870.             Clients that support rendering icons MUST support at least the following MIME types:
4871.             </para>
4872.             <list type="bullet">
4873.             <item><description>image/png - PNG images (safe, universal compatibility)</description></item>
4874.             <item><description>image/jpeg (and image/jpg) - JPEG images (safe, universal compatibility)</description></item>
4875.             </list>
4876.             <para>
4877.             Clients that support rendering icons SHOULD also support:
4878.             </para>
4879.             <list type="bullet">
4880.             <item><description>image/svg+xml - SVG images (scalable but requires security precautions)</description></item>
4881.             <item><description>image/webp - WebP images (modern, efficient format)</description></item>
4882.             </list>
4883.             <para>
4884.             See the <see href="https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/specification/blob/main/schema/">schema</see> for details.
4885.             </para>
4886.             </remarks>
4887.         </member>
4888.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Icon.Source">
4889.             <summary>
4890.             Gets or sets the URI pointing to the icon resource.
4891.             </summary>
4892.             <remarks>
4893.             <para>
4894.             This value can be an HTTP/HTTPS URL pointing to an image file or a data URI with base64-encoded image data.
4895.             </para>
4896.             <para>
4897.             Consumers SHOULD take steps to ensure URLs serving icons are from the same domain as the client/server
4898.             or a trusted domain.
4899.             </para>
4900.             <para>
4901.             Consumers SHOULD take appropriate precautions when consuming SVGs as they can contain executable JavaScript.
4902.             </para>
4903.             </remarks>
4904.         </member>
4905.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Icon.MimeType">
4906.             <summary>
4907.             Gets or sets the optional MIME type of the icon.
4908.             </summary>
4909.             <remarks>
4910.             This value can be used to override the server's MIME type if it's missing or generic.
4911.             Common values include "image/png", "image/jpeg", "image/svg+xml", and "image/webp".
4912.             </remarks>
4913.         </member>
4914.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Icon.Sizes">
4915.             <summary>
4916.             Gets or sets the optional size specifications for the icon.
4917.             </summary>
4918.             <remarks>
4919.             <para>
4920.             This property can specify one or more sizes at which the icon file can be used.
4921.             Examples include "48x48", "any" for scalable formats like SVG.
4922.             </para>
4923.             <para>
4924.             If not provided, clients should assume that the icon can be used at any size.
4925.             </para>
4926.             </remarks>
4927.         </member>
4928.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Icon.Theme">
4929.             <summary>
4930.             Gets or sets the optional theme for this icon.
4931.             </summary>
4932.             <remarks>
4933.             <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Icon.Theme"/> may be "light" or "dark". "light" indicates the icon is designed to be used with a light
4934.             background, and "dark" indicates the icon is designed to be used with a dark background.
4935.             If not provided, clients should assume the icon can be used with any theme.
4936.             </remarks>
4937.         </member>
4938.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Implementation">
4939.             <summary>
4940.             Provides the name and version of an MCP implementation.
4941.             </summary>
4942.             <remarks>
4943.             <para>
4944.             The <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Implementation"/> class is used to identify MCP clients and servers during the initialization handshake.
4945.             It provides version and name information that can be used for compatibility checks, logging, and debugging.
4946.             </para>
4947.             <para>
4948.             Both clients and servers provide this information during connection establishment.
4949.             </para>
4950.             <para>
4951.             See the <see href="https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/specification/blob/main/schema/">schema</see> for details.
4952.             </para>
4953.             </remarks>
4954.         </member>
4955.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Implementation.Name">
4956.             <inheritdoc />
4957.         </member>
4958.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Implementation.Title">
4959.             <inheritdoc />
4960.         </member>
4961.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Implementation.Version">
4962.             <summary>
4963.             Gets or sets the version of the implementation.
4964.             </summary>
4965.             <remarks>
4966.             The version is used during client-server handshake to identify implementation versions,
4967.             which can be important for troubleshooting compatibility issues or when reporting bugs.
4968.             </remarks>
4969.         </member>
4970.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Implementation.Description">
4971.             <summary>
4972.             Gets or sets an optional description of the implementation.
4973.             </summary>
4974.             <remarks>
4975.             <para>
4976.             This description helps users and developers understand what the implementation provides
4977.             and its purpose. It should clearly explain the functionality and capabilities offered.
4978.             </para>
4979.             <para>
4980.             The description is typically used in documentation, UI displays, and for providing context
4981.             to users about the server or client they are interacting with.
4982.             </para>
4983.             </remarks>
4984.         </member>
4985.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Implementation.Icons">
4986.             <summary>
4987.             Gets or sets an optional list of icons for this implementation.
4988.             </summary>
4989.             <remarks>
4990.             This value can be used by clients to display the implementation's icon in a user interface.
4991.             </remarks>
4992.         </member>
4993.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Implementation.WebsiteUrl">
4994.             <summary>
4995.             Gets or sets an optional URL of the website for this implementation.
4996.             </summary>
4997.             <remarks>
4998.             <para>
4999.             This URL can be used by clients to link to documentation or more information about the implementation.
5000.             </para>
5001.             <para>
5002.             Consumers SHOULD take steps to ensure URLs are from the same domain as the client/server
5003.             or a trusted domain to prevent security issues.
5004.             </para>
5005.             </remarks>
5006.         </member>
5007.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.InitializedNotificationParams">
5008.             <summary>
5009.             Represents the parameters used with a <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.NotificationMethods.InitializedNotification"/>
5010.             sent from the client to the server after initialization has finished.
5011.             </summary>
5012.             <remarks>
5013.             See the <see href="https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/specification/blob/main/schema/">schema</see> for details.
5014.             </remarks>
5015.         </member>
5016.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.InitializeRequestParams">
5017.             <summary>
5018.             Represents the parameters used with a <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.Initialize"/> request sent by a client to a server during the protocol handshake.
5019.             </summary>
5020.             <remarks>
5021.             <para>
5022.             The <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.InitializeRequestParams"/> is the first message sent in the Model Context Protocol
5023.             communication flow. It establishes the connection between client and server, negotiates the protocol
5024.             version, and declares the client's capabilities.
5025.             </para>
5026.             <para>
5027.             After sending this request, the client should wait for an <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.InitializeResult"/> response
5028.             before sending an <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.NotificationMethods.InitializedNotification"/> notification to complete the handshake.
5029.             </para>
5030.             <para>
5031.             See the <see href="https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/specification/blob/main/schema/">schema</see> for details.
5032.             </para>
5033.             </remarks>
5034.         </member>
5035.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.InitializeRequestParams.ProtocolVersion">
5036.             <summary>
5037.             Gets or sets the version of the Model Context Protocol that the client wants to use.
5038.             </summary>
5039.             <remarks>
5040.             <para>
5041.             Protocol version is specified using a date-based versioning scheme in the format "YYYY-MM-DD".
5042.             The client and server must agree on a protocol version to communicate successfully.
5043.             </para>
5044.             <para>
5045.             During initialization, the server will check if it supports this requested version. If there's a
5046.             mismatch, the server will reject the connection with a version mismatch error.
5047.             </para>
5048.             <para>
5049.             See the <see href="https://spec.modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/">protocol specification</see> for version details.
5050.             </para>
5051.             </remarks>
5052.         </member>
5053.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.InitializeRequestParams.Capabilities">
5054.             <summary>
5055.             Gets or sets the client's capabilities.
5056.             </summary>
5057.             <remarks>
5058.             Capabilities define the features the client supports, such as "sampling" or "roots".
5059.             </remarks>
5060.         </member>
5061.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.InitializeRequestParams.ClientInfo">
5062.             <summary>
5063.             Gets or sets information about the client implementation, including its name and version.
5064.             </summary>
5065.             <remarks>
5066.             This information is required during the initialization handshake to identify the client.
5067.             Servers might use this information for logging, debugging, or compatibility checks.
5068.             </remarks>
5069.         </member>
5070.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.InitializeResult">
5071.             <summary>
5072.             Represents the result of a <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.Initialize"/> request sent to the server during connection establishment.
5073.             </summary>
5074.             <remarks>
5075.             <para>
5076.             The <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.InitializeResult"/> is sent by the server in response to an <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.InitializeRequestParams"/>
5077.             message from the client. It contains information about the server, its capabilities, and the protocol version
5078.             that will be used for the session.
5079.             </para>
5080.             <para>
5081.             After receiving this response, the client should send an <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.NotificationMethods.InitializedNotification"/>
5082.             notification to complete the handshake.
5083.             </para>
5084.             <para>
5085.             See the <see href="https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/specification/blob/main/schema/">schema</see> for details.
5086.             </para>
5087.             </remarks>
5088.         </member>
5089.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.InitializeResult.ProtocolVersion">
5090.             <summary>
5091.             Gets or sets the version of the Model Context Protocol that the server will use for this session.
5092.             </summary>
5093.             <remarks>
5094.             <para>
5095.             This is the protocol version the server has agreed to use, which should match the client's
5096.             requested version. If there's a mismatch, the client should throw an exception to prevent
5097.             communication issues due to incompatible protocol versions.
5098.             </para>
5099.             <para>
5100.             The protocol uses a date-based versioning scheme in the format "YYYY-MM-DD".
5101.             </para>
5102.             <para>
5103.             See the <see href="https://spec.modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/">protocol specification</see> for version details.
5104.             </para>
5105.             </remarks>
5106.         </member>
5107.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.InitializeResult.Capabilities">
5108.             <summary>
5109.             Gets or sets the server's capabilities.
5110.             </summary>
5111.             <remarks>
5112.             This property defines the features the server supports, such as "tools", "prompts", "resources", or "logging",
5113.             and other protocol-specific functionality.
5114.             </remarks>
5115.         </member>
5116.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.InitializeResult.ServerInfo">
5117.             <summary>
5118.             Gets or sets information about the server implementation, including its name and version.
5119.             </summary>
5120.             <remarks>
5121.             This information identifies the server during the initialization handshake.
5122.             Clients might use this information for logging, debugging, or compatibility checks.
5123.             </remarks>
5124.         </member>
5125.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.InitializeResult.Instructions">
5126.             <summary>
5127.             Gets or sets optional instructions for using the server and its features.
5128.             </summary>
5129.             <remarks>
5130.             <para>
5131.             These instructions should focus on guidance that helps a model use the server effectively,
5132.             such as workflow tips, capability relationships, and server-specific conventions.
5133.             They should avoid repeating tool descriptions, prompt descriptions, or resource descriptions
5134.             that are already available through other protocol responses.
5135.             </para>
5136.             <para>
5137.             Client applications often use these instructions as system messages for LLM interactions
5138.             to provide context about available functionality.
5139.             </para>
5140.             </remarks>
5141.         </member>
5142.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ITransport">
5143.             <summary>
5144.             Represents a transport mechanism for MCP (Model Context Protocol) communication between clients and servers.
5145.             </summary>
5146.             <remarks>
5147.             <para>
5148.             The <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ITransport"/> interface is the core abstraction for bidirectional communication.
5149.             It provides methods for sending and receiving messages, abstracting away the underlying transport mechanism,
5150.             and allowing protocol implementations to be decoupled from communication details.
5151.             </para>
5152.             <para>
5153.             Implementations of <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ITransport"/> handle the serialization, transmission, and reception of
5154.             messages over various channels like standard input/output streams and HTTP (Server-Sent Events).
5155.             </para>
5156.             <para>
5157.             While <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Client.IClientTransport"/> is responsible for establishing a client's connection,
5158.             <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ITransport"/> represents an established session. Client implementations typically obtain an
5159.             <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ITransport"/> instance by calling <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.Client.IClientTransport.ConnectAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>.
5160.             </para>
5161.             </remarks>
5162.         </member>
5163.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ITransport.SessionId">
5164.             <summary>Gets an identifier associated with the current MCP session.</summary>
5165.             <remarks>
5166.             The identifier is typically populated in transports supporting multiple sessions, such as Streamable HTTP or SSE.
5167.             This property can return <see langword="null"/> if the session hasn't initialized or if the transport doesn't
5168.             support multiple sessions (as is the case with STDIO).
5169.             </remarks>
5170.         </member>
5171.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ITransport.MessageReader">
5172.             <summary>
5173.             Gets a channel reader for receiving messages from the transport.
5174.             </summary>
5175.             <remarks>
5176.             <para>
5177.             The <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ITransport.MessageReader"/> provides access to incoming JSON-RPC messages received by the transport.
5178.             It returns a <see cref="T:System.Threading.Channels.ChannelReader`1"/> which allows messages to be consumed in a thread-safe manner.
5179.             </para>
5180.             <para>
5181.             The reader will continue to provide messages as long as the transport is connected. When the transport
5182.             is disconnected or disposed, the channel will be completed and no more messages will be available after
5183.             any already transmitted messages are consumed.
5184.             </para>
5185.             </remarks>
5186.         </member>
5187.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ITransport.SendMessageAsync(ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcMessage,System.Threading.CancellationToken)">
5188.             <summary>
5189.             Sends a JSON-RPC message through the transport.
5190.             </summary>
5191.             <param name="message">The JSON-RPC message to send.</param>
5192.             <param name="cancellationToken">The <see cref="T:System.Threading.CancellationToken"/> to monitor for cancellation requests. The default is <see cref="P:System.Threading.CancellationToken.None"/>.</param>
5193.             <returns>A task that represents the asynchronous send operation.</returns>
5194.             <exception cref="T:System.InvalidOperationException">The transport is not connected.</exception>
5195.             <remarks>
5196.             <para>
5197.             This method serializes and sends the provided JSON-RPC message through the transport connection.
5198.             </para>
5199.             <para>
5200.             This is a core method used by higher-level abstractions in the MCP protocol implementation.
5201.             Most client code should use the higher-level methods provided by <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.McpSession"/>,
5202.             <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClient"/>, or <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServer"/>,
5203.             rather than accessing this method directly.
5204.             </para>
5205.             </remarks>
5206.         </member>
5207.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcError">
5208.             <summary>
5209.             Represents an error response message in the JSON-RPC protocol.
5210.             </summary>
5211.             <remarks>
5212.             <para>
5213.             Error responses are sent when a request cannot be fulfilled or encounters an error during processing.
5214.             Like successful responses, error messages include the same ID as the original request, allowing the
5215.             sender to match errors with their corresponding requests.
5216.             </para>
5217.             <para>
5218.             Each error response contains a structured error detail object with a numeric code, descriptive message,
5219.             and optional additional data to provide more context about the error.
5220.             </para>
5221.             </remarks>
5222.         </member>
5223.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcError.Error">
5224.             <summary>
5225.             Gets or sets detailed error information for the failed request, containing an error code,
5226.             message, and optional additional data.
5227.             </summary>
5228.         </member>
5229.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcErrorDetail">
5230.             <summary>
5231.             Represents detailed error information for JSON-RPC error responses.
5232.             </summary>
5233.             <remarks>
5234.             This class is used as part of the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcError"/> message to provide structured
5235.             error information when a request cannot be fulfilled. The JSON-RPC 2.0 specification defines
5236.             a standard format for error responses that includes a numeric code, a human-readable message,
5237.             and optional additional data.
5238.             </remarks>
5239.         </member>
5240.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcErrorDetail.Code">
5241.             <summary>
5242.             Gets or sets an integer error code according to the JSON-RPC specification.
5243.             </summary>
5244.         </member>
5245.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcErrorDetail.Message">
5246.             <summary>
5247.             Gets or sets a short description of the error.
5248.             </summary>
5249.             <remarks>
5250.             This description is expected to be a brief, human-readable explanation of what went wrong.
5251.             For standard error codes, it's recommended to use the descriptions defined
5252.             in the JSON-RPC 2.0 specification.
5253.             </remarks>
5254.         </member>
5255.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcErrorDetail.Data">
5256.             <summary>
5257.             Gets or sets optional additional error data.
5258.             </summary>
5259.             <remarks>
5260.             This property can contain any additional information that might help the client
5261.             understand or resolve the error. Common examples include validation errors,
5262.             stack traces (in development environments), or contextual information about
5263.             the error condition.
5264.             </remarks>
5265.         </member>
5266.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcMessage">
5267.             <summary>
5268.             Represents any JSON-RPC message used in the Model Context Protocol (MCP).
5269.             </summary>
5270.             <remarks>
5271.             This interface serves as the foundation for all message types in the JSON-RPC 2.0 protocol
5272.             used by MCP, including requests, responses, notifications, and errors. JSON-RPC is a stateless,
5273.             lightweight remote procedure call (RPC) protocol that uses JSON as its data format.
5274.             </remarks>
5275.         </member>
5276.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcMessage.#ctor">
5277.             <summary>Prevent external derivations.</summary>
5278.         </member>
5279.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcMessage.JsonRpc">
5280.             <summary>
5281.             Gets or sets the JSON-RPC protocol version used.
5282.             </summary>
5283.             <inheritdoc />
5284.         </member>
5285.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcMessage.Context">
5286.             <summary>
5287.             Gets or sets the contextual information for this JSON-RPC message.
5288.             </summary>
5289.             <remarks>
5290.             <para>
5291.             This property contains transport-specific and runtime context information that accompanies
5292.             JSON-RPC messages but is not serialized as part of the JSON-RPC payload. This includes
5293.             transport references, execution context, and authenticated user information.
5294.             </para>
5295.             <para>
5296.             This property should only be set when implementing a custom <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ITransport"/>
5297.             that needs to pass additional per-message context or to pass a <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcMessageContext.User"/>
5298.             to <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.Server.StreamableHttpServerTransport.HandlePostRequestAsync(ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcMessage,System.IO.Stream,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>
5299.             or <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.Server.SseResponseStreamTransport.OnMessageReceivedAsync(ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcMessage,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>.
5300.             </para>
5301.             </remarks>
5302.         </member>
5303.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcMessage.Converter">
5304.             <summary>
5305.             Provides a <see cref="T:System.Text.Json.Serialization.JsonConverter"/> for <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcMessage"/> messages,
5306.             handling polymorphic deserialization of different message types.
5307.             </summary>
5308.             <remarks>
5309.             <para>
5310.             This converter is responsible for correctly deserializing JSON-RPC messages into their appropriate
5311.             concrete types based on the message structure. It analyzes the JSON payload and determines if it
5312.             represents a request, notification, successful response, or error response.
5313.             </para>
5314.             <para>
5315.             The type determination rules follow the JSON-RPC 2.0 specification:
5316.             <list type="bullet">
5317.             <item><description>Messages with "method" and "id" properties are deserialized as <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcRequest"/>.</description></item>
5318.             <item><description>Messages with "method" but no "id" property are deserialized as <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcNotification"/>.</description></item>
5319.             <item><description>Messages with "id" and "result" properties are deserialized as <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcResponse"/>.</description></item>
5320.             <item><description>Messages with "id" and "error" properties are deserialized as <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcError"/>.</description></item>
5321.             </list>
5322.             </para>
5323.             </remarks>
5324.         </member>
5325.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcMessage.Converter.Read(System.Text.Json.Utf8JsonReader@,System.Type,System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions)">
5326.             <inheritdoc/>
5327.         </member>
5328.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcMessage.Converter.Write(System.Text.Json.Utf8JsonWriter,ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcMessage,System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions)">
5329.             <inheritdoc/>
5330.         </member>
5331.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcMessageContext">
5332.             <summary>
5333.             Contains contextual information for JSON-RPC messages that is not part of the JSON-RPC protocol specification.
5334.             </summary>
5335.             <remarks>
5336.             This class holds transport-specific and runtime context information that accompanies JSON-RPC messages
5337.             but is not serialized as part of the JSON-RPC payload. This includes transport references, execution context,
5338.             and authenticated user information.
5339.             </remarks>
5340.         </member>
5341.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcMessageContext.RelatedTransport">
5342.             <summary>
5343.             Gets or sets the transport the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcMessage"/> was received on or should be sent over.
5344.             </summary>
5345.             <remarks>
5346.             This property is used to support the Streamable HTTP transport where the specification states that the server
5347.             SHOULD include JSON-RPC responses in the HTTP response body for the POST request containing
5348.             the corresponding JSON-RPC request. It can be <see langword="null"/> for other transports.
5349.             </remarks>
5350.         </member>
5351.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcMessageContext.ExecutionContext">
5352.             <summary>
5353.             Gets or sets the <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcMessageContext.ExecutionContext"/> that should be used to run any handlers.
5354.             </summary>
5355.             <remarks>
5356.             This property is used to support the Streamable HTTP transport in its default stateful mode. In this mode,
5357.             the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServer"/> outlives the initial HTTP request context it was created on, and new
5358.             JSON-RPC messages can originate from future HTTP requests. This behavior allows the transport to flow the
5359.             context with the JSON-RPC message. This is particularly useful for enabling IHttpContextAccessor
5360.             in tool calls.
5361.             </remarks>
5362.         </member>
5363.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcMessageContext.User">
5364.             <summary>
5365.             Gets or sets the authenticated user associated with this JSON-RPC message.
5366.             </summary>
5367.             <remarks>
5368.             <para>
5369.             This property contains the <see cref="T:System.Security.Claims.ClaimsPrincipal"/> representing the authenticated user
5370.             who initiated this JSON-RPC message. This enables request handlers to access user identity
5371.             and authorization information without requiring dependency on HTTP context accessors
5372.             or other HTTP-specific abstractions.
5373.             </para>
5374.             <para>
5375.             The user information is automatically populated by the transport layer when processing
5376.             incoming HTTP requests in ASP.NET Core scenarios. For other transport types or scenarios
5377.             where user authentication is not applicable, this property can be <see langword="null"/>.
5378.             </para>
5379.             <para>
5380.             This property is particularly useful in the Streamable HTTP transport where JSON-RPC messages
5381.             might outlive the original HTTP request context, allowing user identity to be preserved
5382.             throughout the message processing pipeline.
5383.             </para>
5384.             </remarks>
5385.         </member>
5386.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcMessageContext.Items">
5387.             <summary>
5388.             Gets or sets a key/value collection that can be used to share data within the scope of this message.
5389.             </summary>
5390.             <remarks>
5391.             <para>
5392.             This property allows data to be flowed throughout the message processing pipeline,
5393.             including from incoming message filters to request-specific filters and handlers.
5394.             </para>
5395.             <para>
5396.             When creating a <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.MessageContext"/> or <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.RequestContext`1"/> for server-side
5397.             processing, the Items dictionary from this context will be used, ensuring data set in message filters
5398.             is available in request filters and handlers.
5399.             </para>
5400.             </remarks>
5401.         </member>
5402.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcMessageWithId">
5403.             <summary>
5404.             Represents a JSON-RPC message used in the Model Context Protocol (MCP) and that includes an ID.
5405.             </summary>
5406.             <remarks>
5407.             In the JSON-RPC protocol, messages with an ID require a response from the receiver.
5408.             This includes request messages (which expect a matching response) and response messages
5409.             (which include the ID of the original request they're responding to).
5410.             The ID is used to correlate requests with their responses, allowing asynchronous
5411.             communication where multiple requests can be sent without waiting for responses.
5412.             </remarks>
5413.         </member>
5414.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcMessageWithId.#ctor">
5415.             <summary>Prevent external derivations.</summary>
5416.         </member>
5417.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcMessageWithId.Id">
5418.             <summary>
5419.             Gets or sets the message identifier.
5420.             </summary>
5421.             <remarks>
5422.             Each ID is expected to be unique within the context of a given session.
5423.             </remarks>
5424.         </member>
5425.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcNotification">
5426.             <summary>
5427.             Represents a notification message in the JSON-RPC protocol.
5428.             </summary>
5429.             <remarks>
5430.             Notifications are messages that do not require a response and are not matched with a response message.
5431.             They are useful for one-way communication, such as log notifications and progress updates.
5432.             Unlike requests, notifications do not include an ID field, since there will be no response to match with it.
5433.             </remarks>
5434.         </member>
5435.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcNotification.Method">
5436.             <summary>
5437.             Gets or sets the name of the notification method.
5438.             </summary>
5439.         </member>
5440.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcNotification.Params">
5441.             <summary>
5442.             Gets or sets optional parameters for the notification.
5443.             </summary>
5444.         </member>
5445.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcRequest">
5446.              <summary>
5447.              Represents a request message in the JSON-RPC protocol.
5448.              </summary>
5449.              <remarks>
5450.              Requests are messages that require a response from the receiver. Each request includes a unique ID
5451.              that will be included in the corresponding response message (either a success response or an error).
5452.             
5453.              The receiver of a request message is expected to execute the specified method with the provided parameters
5454.              and return either a <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcResponse"/> with the result, or a <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcError"/>
5455.              if the method execution fails.
5456.              </remarks>
5457.         </member>
5458.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcRequest.Method">
5459.             <summary>
5460.             Gets or sets the name of the method to invoke.
5461.             </summary>
5462.         </member>
5463.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcRequest.Params">
5464.             <summary>
5465.             Gets or sets optional parameters for the method.
5466.             </summary>
5467.         </member>
5468.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcResponse">
5469.             <summary>
5470.             Represents a successful response message in the JSON-RPC protocol.
5471.             </summary>
5472.             <remarks>
5473.             <para>
5474.             Response messages are sent in reply to a request message and contain the result of the method execution.
5475.             Each response includes the same ID as the original request, allowing the sender to match responses
5476.             with their corresponding requests.
5477.             </para>
5478.             <para>
5479.             This class represents a successful response with a result. For error responses, see <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcError"/>.
5480.             </para>
5481.             </remarks>
5482.         </member>
5483.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcResponse.Result">
5484.             <summary>
5485.             Gets or sets the result of the method invocation.
5486.             </summary>
5487.             <remarks>
5488.             This property contains the result data returned by the server in response to the JSON-RPC method request.
5489.             </remarks>
5490.         </member>

5687.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.LoggingCapability">
5688.             <summary>
5689.             Represents the logging capability configuration for a Model Context Protocol server.
5690.             </summary>
5691.             <remarks>
5692.             <para>
5693.             This capability allows clients to set the logging level and receive log messages from the server.
5694.             See the <see href="https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/specification/blob/main/schema/">schema</see> for details.
5695.             </para>
5696.             <para>
5697.             This class is intentionally empty as the Model Context Protocol specification does not
5698.             currently define additional properties for logging capabilities. Future versions of the
5699.             specification may extend this capability with additional configuration options.
5700.             </para>
5701.             </remarks>
5702.         </member>
5703.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.LoggingLevel">
5704.             <summary>
5705.             Indicates the severity of a log message.
5706.             </summary>
5707.             <remarks>
5708.             These values map to syslog message severities, as specified in <see href="https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc5424#section-6.2.1">RFC-5424</see>.
5709.             </remarks>
5710.         </member>
5711.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.LoggingLevel.Debug">
5712.             <summary>Detailed debug information, typically only valuable to developers.</summary>
5713.         </member>
5714.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.LoggingLevel.Info">
5715.             <summary>Normal operational messages that require no action.</summary>
5716.         </member>
5717.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.LoggingLevel.Notice">
5718.             <summary>Normal but significant events that might deserve attention.</summary>
5719.         </member>
5720.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.LoggingLevel.Warning">
5721.             <summary>Warning conditions that don't represent an error but indicate potential issues.</summary>
5722.         </member>
5723.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.LoggingLevel.Error">
5724.             <summary>Error conditions that should be addressed but don't require immediate action.</summary>
5725.         </member>
5726.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.LoggingLevel.Critical">
5727.             <summary>Critical conditions that require immediate attention.</summary>
5728.         </member>
5729.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.LoggingLevel.Alert">
5730.             <summary>Action must be taken immediately to address the condition.</summary>
5731.         </member>
5732.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.LoggingLevel.Emergency">
5733.             <summary>System is unusable and requires immediate attention.</summary>
5734.         </member>
5735.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.LoggingMessageNotificationParams">
5736.             <summary>
5737.             Represents the parameters used with a <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.NotificationMethods.LoggingMessageNotification"/>
5738.             notification sent whenever a log message is generated.
5739.             </summary>
5740.             <remarks>
5741.             <para>
5742.             Logging notifications allow servers to communicate diagnostic information to clients with varying severity levels.
5743.             Clients can filter these messages based on the <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.LoggingMessageNotificationParams.Level"/> and <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.LoggingMessageNotificationParams.Logger"/> properties.
5744.             </para>
5745.             <para>
5746.             If no <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.LoggingSetLevel"/> request has been sent from the client, the server can decide which
5747.             messages to send automatically.
5748.             </para>
5749.             <para>
5750.             See the <see href="https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/specification/blob/main/schema/">schema</see> for details.
5751.             </para>
5752.             </remarks>
5753.         </member>
5754.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.LoggingMessageNotificationParams.Level">
5755.             <summary>
5756.             Gets or sets the severity of this log message.
5757.             </summary>
5758.         </member>
5759.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.LoggingMessageNotificationParams.Logger">
5760.             <summary>
5761.             Gets or sets an optional name of the logger issuing this message.
5762.             </summary>
5763.             <remarks>
5764.             <para>
5765.             <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.LoggingMessageNotificationParams.Logger"/> typically represents a category or component in the server's logging system.
5766.             The logger name is useful for filtering and routing log messages in client applications.
5767.             </para>
5768.             <para>
5769.             When implementing custom servers, choose clear, hierarchical logger names to help
5770.             clients understand the source of log messages.
5771.             </para>
5772.             </remarks>
5773.         </member>
5774.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.LoggingMessageNotificationParams.Data">

5778.             </summary>
5779.         </member>
5780.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTask">
5781.             <summary>
5782.             Represents an MCP task, which is a durable state machine carrying information
5783.             about the underlying execution state of a request.
5784.             </summary>
5785.             <remarks>
5786.             <para>
5787.             Tasks are useful for representing expensive computations and batch processing requests.
5788.             Each task is uniquely identifiable by a receiver-generated task ID.
5789.             </para>
5790.             <para>
5791.             Tasks follow a defined lifecycle through the <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTask.Status"/> property. They begin
5792.             in the <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatus.Working"/> status and may transition through various states
5793.             before reaching a terminal status (<see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatus.Completed"/>, <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatus.Failed"/>,
5794.             or <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatus.Cancelled"/>).
5795.             </para>
5796.             <para>
5797.             See the <see href="https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/modelcontextprotocol/blob/main/docs/specification/draft/basic/utilities/tasks.mdx">tasks specification</see> for details.
5798.             </para>
5799.             </remarks>
5800.         </member>
5801.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTask.TaskId">
5802.             <summary>
5803.             Gets or sets the unique identifier for the task.
5804.             </summary>
5805.             <remarks>
5806.             Task IDs are generated by the receiver when creating a task and must be unique
5807.             among all tasks controlled by that receiver.
5808.             </remarks>
5809.         </member>
5810.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTask.Status">
5811.             <summary>
5812.             Gets or sets the current state of the task execution.
5813.             </summary>
5814.         </member>
5815.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTask.StatusMessage">
5816.             <summary>
5817.             Gets or sets an optional human-readable message describing the current state.
5818.             </summary>
5819.             <remarks>
5820.             This message can be present for any status, including error details for failed tasks.
5821.             </remarks>
5822.         </member>
5823.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTask.CreatedAt">
5824.             <summary>
5825.             Gets or sets the ISO 8601 timestamp when the task was created.
5826.             </summary>
5827.             <remarks>
5828.             Receivers must include this timestamp in all task responses to indicate when
5829.             the task was created.
5830.             </remarks>
5831.         </member>
5832.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTask.LastUpdatedAt">
5833.             <summary>
5834.             Gets or sets the ISO 8601 timestamp when the task status was last updated.
5835.             </summary>
5836.             <remarks>
5837.             Receivers must include this timestamp in all task responses to indicate when
5838.             the task was last updated.
5839.             </remarks>
5840.         </member>
5841.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTask.TimeToLive">
5842.             <summary>
5843.             Gets or sets the time to live (retention duration) from creation before the task may be deleted.
5844.             </summary>
5845.             <remarks>
5846.             <para>
5847.             A null value indicates unlimited lifetime. After a task's TTL lifetime has elapsed,
5848.             receivers may delete the task and its results, regardless of the task status.
5849.             </para>
5850.             <para>
5851.             Receivers may override the requested TTL duration and must include the actual TTL
5852.             duration (or null for unlimited) in task responses.
5853.             </para>
5854.             </remarks>
5855.         </member>
5856.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTask.PollInterval">
5857.             <summary>
5858.             Gets or sets the suggested time between status checks.
5859.             </summary>
5860.             <remarks>
5861.             Requestors should respect this value when provided to avoid excessive polling.
5862.             This value is optional and may not be present in all task responses.
5863.             </remarks>
5864.         </member>
5865.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskMetadata">
5866.             <summary>
5867.             Represents metadata for augmenting a request with task execution.
5868.             </summary>
5869.             <remarks>
5870.             <para>
5871.             When included in a request's params, this metadata signals that the requestor
5872.             wants the receiver to execute the request as a task rather than synchronously.
5873.             The receiver will return a <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Result"/> containing task data
5874.             instead of the actual operation result.
5875.             </para>
5876.             <para>
5877.             Requestors can specify a desired TTL (time-to-live) duration for the task,
5878.             though receivers may override this value based on their resource management policies.
5879.             </para>
5880.             </remarks>
5881.         </member>
5882.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskMetadata.TimeToLive">
5883.             <summary>
5884.             Gets or sets the requested time to live (retention duration) to retain the task from creation.
5885.             </summary>
5886.             <remarks>
5887.             <para>
5888.             This is a hint to the receiver about how long the requestor expects to need access
5889.             to the task data. Receivers may override this value based on their resource constraints
5890.             and policies.
5891.             </para>
5892.             <para>
5893.             A null value indicates no specific retention requirement. The actual TTL used by the
5894.             receiver will be returned in the <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTask.TimeToLive"/> property.
5895.             </para>
5896.             </remarks>
5897.         </member>
5898.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTasksCapability">
5899.             <summary>
5900.             Represents the tasks capability configuration for servers and clients.
5901.             </summary>
5902.             <remarks>
5903.             <para>
5904.             The tasks capability enables requestors (clients or servers) to augment their requests with
5905.             tasks for long-running operations. Tasks are durable state machines that carry information
5906.             about the underlying execution state of requests.
5907.             </para>
5908.             <para>
5909.             During initialization, both parties exchange their tasks capabilities to establish which
5910.             operations support task-based execution. Requestors should only augment requests with a
5911.             task if the corresponding capability has been declared by the receiver.
5912.             </para>
5913.             </remarks>
5914.         </member>
5915.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTasksCapability.List">
5916.             <summary>
5917.             Gets or sets whether this party supports the tasks/list operation.
5918.             </summary>
5919.             <remarks>
5920.             When present, indicates support for listing all tasks.
5921.             </remarks>
5922.         </member>
5923.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTasksCapability.Cancel">
5924.             <summary>
5925.             Gets or sets whether this party supports the tasks/cancel operation.
5926.             </summary>
5927.             <remarks>
5928.             When present, indicates support for cancelling tasks.
5929.             </remarks>
5930.         </member>
5931.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTasksCapability.Requests">
5932.             <summary>
5933.             Gets or sets which request types support task augmentation.
5934.             </summary>
5935.             <remarks>
5936.             <para>
5937.             The set of capabilities in this property is exhaustive. If a request type is not present,
5938.             it does not support task augmentation.
5939.             </para>
5940.             <para>
5941.             For servers, this typically includes tools/call. For clients, this typically includes
5942.             sampling/createMessage and elicitation/create.
5943.             </para>
5944.             </remarks>
5945.         </member>
5946.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMcpTasksCapability">
5947.             <summary>
5948.             Represents task support for tool-specific requests.
5949.             </summary>
5950.         </member>
5951.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMcpTasksCapability.Tools">

5953.             Gets or sets task support for tool-related requests.
5954.             </summary>
5955.         </member>
5956.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMcpTasksCapability.Sampling">

5958.             Gets or sets task support for sampling-related requests.
5959.             </summary>
5960.         </member>
5961.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMcpTasksCapability.Elicitation">

5965.         </member>
5966.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ToolsMcpTasksCapability">

5969.             </summary>
5970.         </member>
5971.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ToolsMcpTasksCapability.Call">

5975.             <remarks>

5977.             </remarks>
5978.         </member>
5979.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.SamplingMcpTasksCapability">
5980.             <summary>
5981.             Represents task support for sampling-related requests.
5982.             </summary>
5983.         </member>
5984.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.SamplingMcpTasksCapability.CreateMessage">

5986.             Gets or sets whether sampling/createMessage requests support task augmentation.
5987.             </summary>
5988.             <remarks>
5989.             When present, indicates that the client supports task-augmented sampling/createMessage requests.
5990.             </remarks>
5991.         </member>
5992.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ElicitationMcpTasksCapability">
