6030.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatus">
6031.             <summary>
6032.             Represents the status of an MCP task.
6033.             </summary>
6034.             <remarks>
6035.             <para>
6036.             Tasks progress through a defined lifecycle:
6037.             <list type="bullet">
6038.             <item><description><see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatus.Working"/>: The request is currently being processed.</description></item>
6039.             <item><description><see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatus.InputRequired"/>: The receiver needs input from the requestor. 
6040.             The requestor should call tasks/result to receive input requests.</description></item>
6041.             <item><description><see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatus.Completed"/>: The request completed successfully and results are available.</description></item>
6042.             <item><description><see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatus.Failed"/>: The request did not complete successfully.</description></item>
6043.             <item><description><see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatus.Cancelled"/>: The request was cancelled before completion.</description></item>
6044.             </list>
6045.             </para>
6046.             <para>
6047.             Terminal states are <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatus.Completed"/>, <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatus.Failed"/>, and <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatus.Cancelled"/>.
6048.             Once a task reaches a terminal state, it cannot transition to any other status.
6049.             </para>
6050.             </remarks>
6051.         </member>
6052.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatus.Working">
6053.             <summary>
6054.             The request is currently being processed.
6055.             </summary>
6056.             <remarks>
6057.             Tasks begin in this status when created. From <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatus.Working"/>, tasks may transition
6058.             to <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatus.InputRequired"/>, <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatus.Completed"/>, <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatus.Failed"/>, or <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatus.Cancelled"/>.
6059.             </remarks>
6060.         </member>
6061.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatus.InputRequired">
6062.             <summary>
6063.             The receiver needs input from the requestor.
6064.             </summary>
6065.             <remarks>
6066.             The requestor should call tasks/result to receive input requests, even though the task
6067.             has not reached a terminal state. From <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatus.InputRequired"/>, tasks may transition
6068.             to <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatus.Working"/>, <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatus.Completed"/>, <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatus.Failed"/>, or <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatus.Cancelled"/>.
6069.             </remarks>
6070.         </member>
6071.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatus.Completed">
6072.             <summary>
6073.             The request completed successfully and results are available.
6074.             </summary>
6075.             <remarks>
6076.             This is a terminal status. Tasks in this status cannot transition to any other status.
6077.             </remarks>
6078.         </member>
6079.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatus.Failed">
6080.             <summary>
6081.             The associated request did not complete successfully.
6082.             </summary>
6083.             <remarks>
6084.             This is a terminal status. For tool calls specifically, this includes cases where
6085.             the tool call result has isError set to true. Tasks in this status cannot transition
6086.             to any other status.
6087.             </remarks>
6088.         </member>
6089.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatus.Cancelled">
6090.             <summary>
6091.             The request was cancelled before completion.
6092.             </summary>
6093.             <remarks>
6094.             This is a terminal status. Tasks in this status cannot transition to any other status.
6095.             </remarks>
6096.         </member>
6097.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatusNotificationParams">
6098.             <summary>
6099.             Represents the parameters for a notifications/tasks/status notification.
6100.             </summary>
6101.             <remarks>
6102.             <para>
6103.             When a task status changes, receivers may send this notification to inform the
6104.             requestor of the change. This notification includes the full task state.
6105.             </para>
6106.             <para>
6107.             Requestors must not rely on receiving this notification, as it is optional. Receivers
6108.             are not required to send status notifications and may choose to only send them for
6109.             certain status transitions. Requestors should continue to poll via tasks/get to ensure
6110.             they receive status updates.
6111.             </para>
6112.             </remarks>
6113.         </member>
6114.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatusNotificationParams.TaskId">
6115.             <summary>
6116.             Gets or sets the task ID.
6117.             </summary>
6118.         </member>
6119.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatusNotificationParams.Status">
6120.             <summary>
6121.             Gets or sets the current status of the task.
6122.             </summary>
6123.         </member>
6124.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatusNotificationParams.StatusMessage">
6125.             <summary>
6126.             Gets or sets an optional human-readable message describing the current state.
6127.             </summary>
6128.         </member>
6129.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatusNotificationParams.CreatedAt">
6130.             <summary>
6131.             Gets or sets the ISO 8601 timestamp when the task was created.
6132.             </summary>
6133.         </member>
6134.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatusNotificationParams.LastUpdatedAt">
6135.             <summary>
6136.             Gets or sets the ISO 8601 timestamp when the task status was last updated.
6137.             </summary>
6138.         </member>
6139.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatusNotificationParams.TimeToLive">
6140.             <summary>
6141.             Gets or sets the time to live (retention duration) from creation before the task may be deleted.
6142.             </summary>
6143.         </member>
6144.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatusNotificationParams.PollInterval">
6145.             <summary>
6146.             Gets or sets the suggested time between status checks.
6147.             </summary>
6148.         </member>
6149.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ModelHint">
6150.             <summary>
6151.             Provides hints to use for model selection.
6152.             </summary>
6153.             <remarks>
6154.             <para>
6155.             When multiple hints are specified in <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ModelPreferences.Hints"/>, they are evaluated in order,
6156.             with the first match taking precedence. Clients should prioritize these hints over numeric priorities.
6157.             </para>
6158.             <para>
6159.             See the <see href="https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/specification/blob/main/schema/">schema</see> for details.
6160.             </para>
6161.             </remarks>
6162.         </member>
6163.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ModelHint.Name">
6164.             <summary>
6165.             Gets or sets a hint for a model name.
6166.             </summary>
6167.             <remarks>
6168.             The specified string can be a partial or full model name. Clients can also
6169.             map hints to equivalent models from different providers. Clients make the final model
6170.             selection based on these preferences and their available models.
6171.             </remarks>
6172.         </member>
6173.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ModelPreferences">
6174.             <summary>
6175.             Represents a server's preferences for model selection, requested of the client during sampling.
6176.             </summary>
6177.             <remarks>
6178.             <para>
6179.             Because LLMs can vary along multiple dimensions, choosing the "best" model is
6180.             rarely straightforward.  Different models excel in different areas—some are
6181.             faster but less capable, others are more capable but more expensive, and so
6182.             on. This class allows servers to express their priorities across multiple
6183.             dimensions to help clients make an appropriate selection for their use case.
6184.             </para>
6185.             <para>
6186.             These preferences are always advisory. The client may ignore them. It is also
6187.             up to the client to decide how to interpret these preferences and how to
6188.             balance them against other considerations.
6189.             </para>
6190.             <para>
6191.             See the <see href="https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/specification/blob/main/schema/">schema</see> for details.
6192.             </para>
6193.             </remarks>
6194.         </member>
6195.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ModelPreferences.CostPriority">
6196.             <summary>
6197.             Gets or sets a value that indicates how much to prioritize cost when selecting a model.
6198.             </summary>
6199.             <remarks>
6200.             A value of 0 means cost is not important, while a value of 1 means cost is the most important factor.
6201.             </remarks>
6202.         </member>
6203.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ModelPreferences.Hints">
6204.             <summary>
6205.             Gets or sets optional hints to use for model selection.
6206.             </summary>
6207.         </member>
6208.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ModelPreferences.SpeedPriority">
6209.             <summary>
6210.             Gets or sets a value that indicates how much to prioritize sampling speed (latency) when selecting a model.
6211.             </summary>
6212.             <remarks>
6213.             A value of 0 means speed is not important, while a value of 1 means speed is the most important factor.
6214.             </remarks>
6215.         </member>
6216.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ModelPreferences.IntelligencePriority">
6217.             <summary>
6218.             Gets or sets a value that indicates how much to prioritize intelligence and capabilities when selecting a model.
6219.             </summary>
6220.             <remarks>
6221.             A value of 0 means intelligence is not important, while a value of 1 means intelligence is the most important factor.
6222.             </remarks>
6223.         </member>
6224.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.NotificationMethods">
6225.             <summary>
6226.             Provides constants with the names of common notification methods used in the MCP protocol.
6227.             </summary>
6228.         </member>
6229.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.NotificationMethods.ToolListChangedNotification">
6230.             <summary>
6231.             The name of the notification sent by a server when the list of available tools changes.
6232.             </summary>
6233.             <remarks>
6234.             This notification informs clients that the set of available tools has been modified.
6235.             Changes may include tools being added, removed, or updated. Upon receiving this
6236.             notification, clients may refresh their tool list by calling the appropriate
6237.             method to get the updated list of tools.
6238.             </remarks>
6239.         </member>
6240.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.NotificationMethods.PromptListChangedNotification">
6241.             <summary>
6242.             The name of the notification sent by the server when the list of available prompts changes.
6243.             </summary>
6244.             <remarks>
6245.             This notification informs clients that the set of available prompts has been modified.
6246.             Changes may include prompts being added, removed, or updated. Upon receiving this
6247.             notification, clients may refresh their prompt list by calling the appropriate
6248.             method to get the updated list of prompts.
6249.             </remarks>
6250.         </member>
6251.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.NotificationMethods.ResourceListChangedNotification">
6252.             <summary>
6253.             The name of the notification sent by the server when the list of available resources changes.
6254.             </summary>
6255.             <remarks>
6256.             This notification informs clients that the set of available resources has been modified.
6257.             Changes may include resources being added, removed, or updated. Upon receiving this
6258.             notification, clients may refresh their resource list by calling the appropriate
6259.             method to get the updated list of resources.
6260.             </remarks>
6261.         </member>
6262.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.NotificationMethods.ResourceUpdatedNotification">
6263.             <summary>
6264.             The name of the notification sent by the server when a resource is updated.
6265.             </summary>
6266.             <remarks>
6267.             This notification is used to inform clients about changes to a specific resource they have subscribed to.
6268.             When a resource is updated, the server sends this notification to all clients that have subscribed to that resource.
6269.             </remarks>
6270.         </member>
6271.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.NotificationMethods.RootsListChangedNotification">
6272.             <summary>
6273.             The name of the notification sent by the client when roots have been updated.
6274.             </summary>
6275.             <remarks>
6276.             <para>
6277.             This notification informs the server that the client's "roots" have changed.
6278.             Roots inform servers about the directories and files the client considers relevant,
6279.             so that servers can focus their operations accordingly. They are informational guidance
6280.             rather than an access-control mechanism; the protocol does not enforce that servers stay within roots.
6281.             Servers can request the list of roots from supporting clients and receive notifications when that list changes.
6282.             </para>
6283.             <para>
6284.             After receiving this notification, servers can refresh their knowledge of roots by calling the appropriate
6285.             method to get the updated list of roots from the client.
6286.             </para>
6287.             </remarks>
6288.         </member>
6289.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.NotificationMethods.LoggingMessageNotification">
6290.             <summary>
6291.             The name of the notification sent by the server when a log message is generated.
6292.             </summary>
6293.             <remarks>
6294.             <para>
6295.             This notification is used by the server to send log messages to clients. Log messages can include
6296.             different severity levels, such as debug, info, warning, or error, and an optional logger name to
6297.             identify the source component.
6298.             </para>
6299.             <para>
6300.             The minimum logging level that triggers notifications can be controlled by clients using the
6301.             <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.LoggingSetLevel"/> request. If no level has been set by a client,
6302.             the server can determine which messages to send based on its own configuration.
6303.             </para>
6304.             </remarks>
6305.         </member>
6306.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.NotificationMethods.ElicitationCompleteNotification">
6307.             <summary>
6308.             The name of the notification sent by the server when a URL-mode elicitation flow completes.
6309.             </summary>
6310.             <remarks>
6311.             This notification references the original elicitation by ID, allowing clients to retry blocked requests
6312.             or update their UI state once the out-of-band interaction finishes.
6313.             </remarks>
6314.         </member>
6315.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.NotificationMethods.InitializedNotification">
6316.             <summary>
6317.             The name of the notification sent from the client to the server after initialization has finished.
6318.             </summary>
6319.             <remarks>
6320.             <para>
6321.             This notification is sent by the client after it has received and processed the server's response to the
6322.             <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.Initialize"/> request. It signals that the client is ready to begin normal operation
6323.             and that the initialization phase is complete.
6324.             </para>
6325.             <para>
6326.             After receiving this notification, the server can begin sending notifications and processing
6327.             further requests from the client.
6328.             </para>
6329.             </remarks>
6330.         </member>
6331.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.NotificationMethods.ProgressNotification">
6332.             <summary>
6333.             The name of the notification sent to inform the receiver of a progress update for a long-running request.
6334.             </summary>
6335.             <remarks>
6336.             <para>
6337.             This notification provides updates on the progress of long-running operations. It includes
6338.             a progress token that associates the notification with a specific request, the current progress value,
6339.             and optionally, a total value and a descriptive message.
6340.             </para>
6341.             <para>
6342.             Progress notifications can be sent by either the client or the server, depending on the context.
6343.             Progress notifications enable clients to display progress indicators for operations that might take
6344.             significant time to complete, such as large file uploads, complex computations, or resource-intensive
6345.             processing tasks.
6346.             </para>
6347.             </remarks>
6348.         </member>
6349.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.NotificationMethods.CancelledNotification">
6350.             <summary>
6351.             The name of the notification sent to indicate that a previously issued request should be canceled.
6352.             </summary>
6353.             <remarks>
6354.             <para>
6355.             From the issuer's perspective, the request should still be in-flight. However, due to communication latency,
6356.             it is always possible that this notification might arrive after the request has already finished.
6357.             </para>
6358.             <para>
6359.             This notification indicates that the result will be unused, so any associated processing SHOULD cease.
6360.             </para>
6361.             <para>
6362.             A client must not attempt to cancel its `initialize` request.
6363.             </para>
6364.             </remarks>
6365.         </member>
6366.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.NotificationMethods.TaskStatusNotification">
6367.             <summary>
6368.             The name of the notification sent when a task status changes.
6369.             </summary>
6370.             <remarks>
6371.             <para>
6372.             When a task status changes, receivers may send this notification to inform the requestor
6373.             of the change. This notification includes the full task state.
6374.             </para>
6375.             <para>
6376.             Requestors must not rely on receiving this notification, as it is optional. Receivers
6377.             are not required to send status notifications and may choose to only send them for
6378.             certain status transitions. Requestors should continue to poll via tasks/get to ensure
6379.             they receive status updates.
6380.             </para>
6381.             </remarks>
6382.         </member>
6383.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.NotificationMethods.RelatedTaskMetaKey">
6384.             <summary>
6385.             The metadata key used to associate requests, responses, and notifications with a task.
6386.             </summary>
6387.             <remarks>
6388.             <para>
6389.             This constant defines the key <c>"io.modelcontextprotocol/related-task"</c> used in the
6390.             <c>_meta</c> field to associate messages with their originating task across the entire
6391.             request lifecycle.
6392.             </para>
6393.             <para>
6394.             For example, an elicitation that a task-augmented tool call depends on must share the
6395.             same related task ID with that tool call's task.
6396.             </para>
6397.             <para>
6398.             For <c>tasks/get</c>, <c>tasks/list</c>, and <c>tasks/cancel</c> operations, this
6399.             metadata should not be included as the taskId is already present in the message structure.
6400.             </para>
6401.             </remarks>
6402.         </member>

6419.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PaginatedRequestParams">
6420.             <summary>
6421.             Provides a base class for paginated requests.
6422.             </summary>
6423.             <remarks>
6424.             <see href="https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/specification/blob/main/schema/">See the schema for details</see>
6425.             </remarks>
6426.         </member>
6427.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PaginatedRequestParams.#ctor">
6428.             <summary>Prevent external derivations.</summary>
6429.         </member>
6430.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PaginatedRequestParams.Cursor">
6431.             <summary>
6432.             Gets or sets an opaque token representing the current pagination position.
6433.             </summary>
6434.             <remarks>
6435.             If provided, the server should return results starting after this cursor.
6436.             This value should be obtained from the <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PaginatedResult.NextCursor"/>
6437.             property of a previous request's response.
6438.             </remarks>
6439.         </member>
6440.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PaginatedResult">
6441.             <summary>
6442.             Provides a base class for result payloads that support cursor-based pagination.
6443.             </summary>
6444.             <remarks>
6445.             <para>
6446.             Pagination allows API responses to be broken into smaller, manageable chunks when
6447.             there are potentially many results to return or when dynamically computed results
6448.             might incur measurable latency.
6449.             </para>
6450.             <para>
6451.             Classes that inherit from <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PaginatedResult"/> implement cursor-based pagination,
6452.             where the <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PaginatedResult.NextCursor"/> property serves as an opaque token pointing to the next
6453.             set of results.
6454.             </para>
6455.             </remarks>
6456.         </member>
6457.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PaginatedResult.NextCursor">
6458.             <summary>
6459.             Gets or sets an opaque token representing the pagination position after the last returned result.
6460.             </summary>
6461.             <remarks>
6462.             When a paginated result has more data available, the <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PaginatedResult.NextCursor"/>
6463.             property will contain a non-<see langword="null"/> token that can be used in subsequent requests
6464.             to fetch the next page. When there are no more results to return, the <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PaginatedResult.NextCursor"/> property
6465.             will be <see langword="null"/>.
6466.             </remarks>
6467.         </member>
6468.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PingRequestParams">
6469.             <summary>
6470.             Represents the parameters used with a <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.Ping"/> request to verify
6471.             server connectivity.
6472.             </summary>
6473.             <remarks>
6474.             The server responds with a <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PingResult"/>.
6475.             See the <see href="https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/specification/blob/main/schema/">schema</see> for details.
6476.             </remarks>
6477.         </member>
6478.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PingResult">
6479.             <summary>
6480.             Represents the result of a <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.Ping"/> request in the Model Context Protocol.
6481.             </summary>
6482.             <remarks>
6483.             <para>
6484.             The <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PingResult"/> is returned in response to a <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.Ping"/> request, 
6485.             which is used to verify that the connection between client and server is still alive and responsive. 
6486.             Since this is a simple connectivity check, the result is an empty object containing no data.
6487.             </para>
6488.             <para>
6489.             Ping requests can be initiated by either the client or the server to check if the other party
6490.             is still responsive.
6491.             </para>
6492.             </remarks>
6493.         </member>
6494.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ProgressNotificationParams">
6495.             <summary>
6496.             Represents an out-of-band notification used to inform the receiver of a progress update for a long-running request.
6497.             </summary>
6498.             <remarks>

6500.             </remarks>

6502.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ProgressNotificationParams.ProgressToken">
6503.             <summary>
6504.             Gets or sets the progress token that was given in the initial request that's used to associate this notification with
6505.             the corresponding request.
6506.             </summary>
6507.             <remarks>
6508.             <para>
6509.             This token acts as a correlation identifier that links progress updates to their corresponding request.
6510.             </para>
6511.             <para>
6512.             When an endpoint initiates a request with a <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ProgressNotificationParams.ProgressToken"/> in its metadata,
6513.             the receiver can send progress notifications using this same token. This allows both sides to
6514.             correlate the notifications with the original request.
6515.             </para>
6516.             </remarks>
6517.         </member>
6518.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ProgressNotificationParams.Progress">
6519.             <summary>
6520.             Gets or sets the progress thus far.
6521.             </summary>
6522.             <remarks>

6524.             </remarks>
6525.         </member>

6584.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Prompt">
6585.             <summary>
6586.             Represents a prompt that the server offers.
6587.             </summary>
6588.             <remarks>
6589.             See the <see href="https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/specification/blob/main/schema/">schema</see> for details.
6590.             </remarks>
6591.         </member>
6592.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Prompt.Name">
6593.             <inheritdoc />
6594.         </member>
6595.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Prompt.Title">
6596.             <inheritdoc />
6597.         </member>
6598.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Prompt.Description">
6599.             <summary>
6600.             Gets or sets an optional description of what this prompt provides.
6601.             </summary>
6602.             <remarks>
6603.             <para>
6604.             This description helps developers understand the purpose and use cases for the prompt.
6605.             It should explain what the prompt is designed to accomplish and any important context.
6606.             </para>
6607.             <para>
6608.             The description is typically used in documentation, UI displays, and for providing context
6609.             to client applications that may need to choose between multiple available prompts.
6610.             </para>
6611.             </remarks>
6612.         </member>
6613.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Prompt.Arguments">
6614.             <summary>
6615.             Gets or sets a list of arguments that this prompt accepts for templating and customization.
6616.             </summary>
6617.             <remarks>
6618.             <para>
6619.             This list defines the arguments that can be provided when requesting the prompt.
6620.             Each argument specifies metadata like name, description, and whether it's required.
6621.             </para>
6622.             <para>
6623.             When a client makes a <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.PromptsGet"/> request, it can provide values for these arguments
6624.             that will be substituted into the prompt template or otherwise used to render the prompt.
6625.             </para>
6626.             </remarks>
6627.         </member>
6628.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Prompt.Icons">
6629.             <summary>
6630.             Gets or sets an optional list of icons for this prompt.
6631.             </summary>
6632.             <remarks>
6633.             This list can be used by clients to display the prompt's icon in a user interface.
6634.             </remarks>
6635.         </member>
6636.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Prompt.Meta">
6637.             <summary>
6638.             Gets or sets metadata reserved by MCP for protocol-level metadata.
6639.             </summary>
6640.             <remarks>
6641.             Implementations must not make assumptions about its contents.
6642.             </remarks>
6643.         </member>
6644.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PromptArgument">
6645.             <summary>
6646.             Represents an argument that a prompt can accept for templating and customization.
6647.             </summary>
6648.             <remarks>
6649.             <para>
6650.             The <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PromptArgument"/> class defines metadata for arguments that can be provided
6651.             to a prompt. These arguments are used to customize or parameterize prompts when they are
6652.             retrieved using <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.PromptsGet"/> requests.
6653.             </para>
6654.             <para>
6655.             See the <see href="https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/specification/blob/main/schema/">schema</see> for details.
6656.             </para>
6657.             </remarks>
6658.         </member>
6659.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PromptArgument.Name">
6660.             <inheritdoc />
6661.         </member>
6662.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PromptArgument.Title">
6663.             <inheritdoc />
6664.         </member>
6665.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PromptArgument.Description">
6666.             <summary>
6667.             Gets or sets a human-readable description of the argument's purpose and expected values.
6668.             </summary>
6669.             <remarks>
6670.             This description helps developers understand what information should be provided
6671.             for this argument and how it will affect the generated prompt.
6672.             </remarks>
6673.         </member>
6674.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PromptArgument.Required">
6675.             <summary>
6676.             Gets or sets a value that indicates whether this argument must be provided when requesting the prompt.
6677.             </summary>
6678.             <remarks>
6679.             When set to <see langword="true"/>, the client must include this argument when making a <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.PromptsGet"/> request.
6680.             If a required argument is missing, the server should respond with an error.
6681.             </remarks>
6682.         </member>
6683.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PromptListChangedNotificationParams">
6684.             <summary>
6685.             Represents the parameters used with a <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.NotificationMethods.PromptListChangedNotification"/>
6686.             notification from the server to the client, informing it that the list of prompts it offers has changed.
6687.             </summary>
6688.             <remarks>
6689.             <para>
6690.             This notification can be issued by servers without any previous subscription from the client.
6691.             </para>
6692.             <para>
6693.             See the <see href="https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/specification/blob/main/schema/">schema</see> for details.
6694.             </para>
6695.             </remarks>
6696.         </member>
6697.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PromptMessage">
6698.             <summary>
6699.             Represents a message within the Model Context Protocol (MCP) system, used for communication between clients and AI models.
6700.             </summary>
6701.             <remarks>
6702.             <para>
6703.             A <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PromptMessage"/> encapsulates content sent to or received from AI models in the Model Context Protocol.
6704.             Each message has a specific role (<see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Role.User"/> or <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Role.Assistant"/>) and contains content, which can be
6705.             text, images, audio, or embedded resources.
6706.             </para>
6707.             <para>
6708.             This class is similar to <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.SamplingMessage"/>, but with enhanced support for embedding resources from the MCP server.
6709.             It serves as a core data structure in the MCP message exchange flow, particularly in prompt formation and model responses.
6710.             </para>
6711.             <para>
6712.             <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PromptMessage"/> objects are typically used in collections within <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.GetPromptResult"/>
6713.             to represent complete conversations or prompt sequences. They can be converted to and from <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatMessage"/>
6714.             objects using the extension methods <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.AIContentExtensions.ToChatMessage(ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PromptMessage,System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions)"/> and
6715.             <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.AIContentExtensions.ToPromptMessages(Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatMessage)"/>.
6716.             </para>
6717.             <para>
6718.             See the <see href="https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/specification/blob/main/schema/">schema</see> for details.
6719.             </para>
6720.             </remarks>
6721.         </member>
6722.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PromptMessage.Content">
6723.             <summary>
6724.             Gets or sets the content of the message, which can be text, image, audio, or an embedded resource.
6725.             </summary>
6726.             <remarks>
6727.             The <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PromptMessage.Content"/> object contains all the message payload, whether it's simple text,
6728.             base64-encoded binary data (for images/audio), or a reference to an embedded resource.
6729.             The <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ContentBlock.Type"/> property indicates the specific content type.
6730.             </remarks>
6731.         </member>
6732.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PromptMessage.Role">
6733.             <summary>
6734.             Gets or sets the role of the message sender, specifying whether it's from a "user" or an "assistant".
6735.             </summary>
6736.             <remarks>
6737.             In the Model Context Protocol, each message must have a clear role assignment to maintain
6738.             the conversation flow. User messages represent queries or inputs from users, while assistant
6739.             messages represent responses generated by AI models.
6740.             </remarks>
6741.         </member>
6742.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PromptsCapability">
6743.             <summary>
6744.             Represents the server's capability to provide predefined prompt templates that clients can use.
6745.             </summary>
6746.             <remarks>
6747.             <para>
6748.             The prompts capability allows a server to expose a collection of predefined prompt templates that clients
6749.             can discover and use. These prompts can be static (defined in the <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerOptions.PromptCollection"/>) or
6750.             dynamically generated through handlers.
6751.             </para>
6752.             <para>
6753.             See the <see href="https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/specification/blob/main/schema/">schema</see> for details.
6754.             </para>
6755.             </remarks>
6756.         </member>
6757.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PromptsCapability.ListChanged">
6758.             <summary>
6759.             Gets or sets a value that indicates whether this server supports notifications for changes to the prompt list.
6760.             </summary>
6761.             <remarks>
6762.             When set to <see langword="true"/>, the server will send notifications using
6763.             <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.NotificationMethods.PromptListChangedNotification"/> when prompts are added,
6764.             removed, or modified. Clients can register handlers for these notifications to
6765.             refresh their prompt cache. This capability enables clients to stay synchronized with server-side changes
6766.             to available prompts.
6767.             </remarks>
6768.         </member>
6769.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ReadResourceRequestParams">
6770.             <summary>
6771.             Represents the parameters used with a <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.ResourcesRead"/> request from a client to get a resource provided by a server.
6772.             </summary>
6773.             <remarks>
6774.             <para>
6775.             The server will respond with a <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ReadResourceResult"/> containing the resulting resource data.
6776.             </para>
6777.             <para>
6778.             Alternatively, if the resource URI uses the <c>https://</c> scheme, clients may fetch the resource
6779.             directly from the web instead of using <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.ResourcesRead"/>.
6780.             Servers should only use the <c>https://</c> scheme when the client is able to fetch and load the
6781.             resource directly on its own.
6782.             </para>
6783.             <para>
6784.             See the <see href="https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/specification/blob/main/schema/">schema</see> for details.
6785.             </para>
6786.             </remarks>
6787.         </member>
6788.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ReadResourceRequestParams.Uri">
6789.             <summary>
6790.             Gets or sets the URI of the resource to read. The URI can use any protocol; it is up to the server how to interpret it.
6791.             </summary>
6792.         </member>
6793.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ReadResourceResult">
6794.             <summary>
6795.             Represents a server's response to a <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.ResourcesRead"/> request from the client.
6796.             </summary>
6797.             <remarks>
6798.             See the <see href="https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/specification/blob/main/schema/">schema</see> for details.
6799.             </remarks>
6800.         </member>
6801.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ReadResourceResult.Contents">
6802.             <summary>
6803.             Gets or sets a list of <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ResourceContents"/> objects that this resource contains.
6804.             </summary>
6805.             <remarks>
6806.             This property contains the actual content of the requested resource, which can be
6807.             either text-based (<see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.TextResourceContents"/>) or binary (<see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.BlobResourceContents"/>).
6808.             The type of content included depends on the resource being accessed.
6809.             </remarks>
6810.         </member>

6817.             References are commonly used with <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClient.CompleteAsync(ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Reference,System.String,System.String,ModelContextProtocol.RequestOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>
6818.             to request completion suggestions for arguments, and with other methods that need to reference resources or prompts.
6819.             </para>
6820.             <para>

6822.             </para>
6823.             </remarks>

6931.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods">
6932.             <summary>
6933.             Provides constants with the names of common request methods used in the MCP protocol.
6934.             </summary>
6935.         </member>
6936.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.ToolsList">
6937.             <summary>
6938.             The name of the request method sent from the client to request a list of the server's tools.
6939.             </summary>
6940.         </member>
6941.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.ToolsCall">
6942.             <summary>
6943.             The name of the request method sent from the client to request that the server invoke a specific tool.
6944.             </summary>
6945.         </member>
6946.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.PromptsList">
6947.             <summary>
6948.             The name of the request method sent from the client to request a list of the server's prompts.
6949.             </summary>
6950.         </member>
6951.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.PromptsGet">
6952.             <summary>
6953.             The name of the request method sent by the client to get a prompt provided by the server.
6954.             </summary>
6955.         </member>
6956.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.ResourcesList">
6957.             <summary>
6958.             The name of the request method sent from the client to request a list of the server's resources.
6959.             </summary>
6960.         </member>
6961.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.ResourcesRead">
6962.             <summary>
6963.             The name of the request method sent from the client to read a specific server resource.
6964.             </summary>
6965.         </member>
6966.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.ResourcesTemplatesList">
6967.             <summary>
6968.             The name of the request method sent from the client to request a list of the server's resource templates.
6969.             </summary>
6970.         </member>
6971.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.ResourcesSubscribe">
6972.             <summary>
6973.             The name of the request method sent from the client to request <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.NotificationMethods.ResourceUpdatedNotification"/>
6974.             notifications from the server whenever a particular resource changes.
6975.             </summary>
6976.         </member>
6977.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.ResourcesUnsubscribe">
6978.             <summary>
6979.             The name of the request method sent from the client to request unsubscribing from <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.NotificationMethods.ResourceUpdatedNotification"/>
6980.             notifications from the server.
6981.             </summary>
6982.         </member>
6983.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.RootsList">
6984.             <summary>
6985.             The name of the request method sent from the server to request a list of the client's roots.
6986.             </summary>
6987.         </member>
6988.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.Ping">
6989.             <summary>
6990.             The name of the request method sent by either endpoint to check that the connected endpoint is still alive.
6991.             </summary>
6992.         </member>
6993.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.LoggingSetLevel">
6994.             <summary>
6995.             The name of the request method sent from the client to the server to adjust the logging level.
6996.             </summary>
6997.             <remarks>
6998.             This request allows clients to control which log messages they receive from the server
6999.             by setting a minimum severity threshold. After processing this request, the server will
7000.             send log messages with severity at or above the specified level to the client as
7001.             <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.NotificationMethods.LoggingMessageNotification"/> notifications.
7002.             </remarks>
7003.         </member>
7004.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.CompletionComplete">
7005.             <summary>
7006.             The name of the request method sent from the client to the server to ask for completion suggestions.
7007.             </summary>
7008.             <remarks>
7009.             This is used to provide autocompletion-like functionality for arguments in a resource reference or a prompt template.
7010.             The client provides a reference (resource or prompt), argument name, and partial value, and the server
7011.             responds with matching completion options.
7012.             </remarks>
7013.         </member>
7014.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.SamplingCreateMessage">
7015.             <summary>
7016.             The name of the request method sent from the server to sample a large language model (LLM) via the client.
7017.             </summary>
7018.             <remarks>
7019.             This request allows servers to utilize an LLM available on the client side to generate text or image responses
7020.             based on provided messages. It is part of the sampling capability in the Model Context Protocol and enables servers to access
7021.             client-side AI models without needing direct API access to those models.
7022.             </remarks>
7023.         </member>
7024.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.ElicitationCreate">
7025.             <summary>
7026.             The name of the request method sent from the server to elicit additional information from the user via the client.
7027.             </summary>
7028.             <remarks>
7029.             <para>
7030.             This request is used when the server needs more information from the client to proceed with a task or interaction.
7031.             Servers can request structured data from users, with optional JSON schemas to validate responses (form mode),
7032.             or request URL mode (out-of-band) user interaction via navigation for sensitive operations.
7033.             </para>
7034.             <para>
7035.             Two modes are supported:
7036.             <list type="bullet">
7037.               <item><description><b>form</b>: In-band elicitation where structured data is collected and returned to the server</description></item>
7038.               <item><description><b>url</b>: URL mode (out-of-band) elicitation for sensitive operations like OAuth or payments</description></item>
7039.             </list>
7040.             </para>
7041.             </remarks>
7042.         </member>
7043.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.Initialize">
7044.             <summary>
7045.             The name of the request method sent from the client to the server when it first connects, asking it to initialize.
7046.             </summary>
7047.             <remarks>
7048.             The initialize request is the first request sent by the client to the server. It provides client information
7049.             and capabilities to the server during connection establishment. The server responds with its own capabilities
7050.             and information, establishing the protocol version and available features for the session.
7051.             </remarks>
7052.         </member>
7053.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.TasksGet">
7054.             <summary>
7055.             The name of the request method to retrieve task status.
7056.             </summary>
7057.             <remarks>
7058.             Requestors poll for task completion by sending tasks/get requests. They should respect
7059.             the pollInterval provided in responses when determining polling frequency.
7060.             </remarks>
7061.         </member>
7062.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.TasksResult">
7063.             <summary>
7064.             The name of the request method to retrieve the result of a completed task.
7065.             </summary>
7066.             <remarks>
7067.             This request blocks until the task reaches a terminal status (completed, failed, or cancelled).
7068.             The result structure matches the original request type (e.g., CallToolResult for tools/call).
7069.             </remarks>
7070.         </member>
7071.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.TasksList">
7072.             <summary>
7073.             The name of the request method to retrieve a list of tasks with pagination support.
7074.             </summary>
7075.         </member>
7076.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.TasksCancel">
7077.             <summary>
7078.             The name of the request method to explicitly cancel a task.
7079.             </summary>
7080.         </member>

7122.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Resource">
7123.             <summary>
7124.             Represents a known resource that the server is capable of reading.
7125.             </summary>
7126.             <remarks>
7127.             See the <see href="https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/specification/blob/main/schema/">schema</see> for details.
7128.             </remarks>
7129.         </member>

7131.             <inheritdoc />

7134.             <inheritdoc />
7135.         </member>
7136.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Resource.Uri">
7137.             <summary>
7138.             Gets or sets the URI of this resource.
7139.             </summary>
7140.         </member>
7141.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Resource.Description">
7142.             <summary>
7143.             Gets or sets a description of what this resource represents.
7144.             </summary>
7145.             <remarks>
7146.             <para>
7147.             This can be used by clients to improve the LLM's understanding of available resources. It can be thought of like a \"hint\" to the model.
7148.             </para>
7149.             <para>
7150.             The description should provide clear context about the resource's content, format, and purpose.
7151.             This helps AI models make better decisions about when to access or reference the resource.
7152.             </para>
7153.             <para>
7154.             Client applications can also use this description for display purposes in user interfaces
7155.             or to help users understand the available resources.
7156.             </para>
7157.             </remarks>
7158.         </member>
7159.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Resource.MimeType">
7160.             <summary>
7161.             Gets or sets the MIME type of this resource.
7162.             </summary>
7163.             <remarks>
7164.             <para>
7165.             <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Resource.MimeType"/> specifies the format of the resource content, helping clients to properly interpret and display the data.
7166.             Common MIME types include "text/plain" for plain text, "application/pdf" for PDF documents,
7167.             "image/png" for PNG images, and "application/json" for JSON data.
7168.             </para>
7169.             <para>
7170.             This property can be <see langword="null"/> if the MIME type is unknown or not applicable for the resource.
7171.             </para>
7172.             </remarks>
7173.         </member>
7174.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Resource.Annotations">
7175.             <summary>
7176.             Gets or sets optional annotations for the resource.
7177.             </summary>
7178.             <remarks>
7179.             These annotations can be used to specify the intended audience (<see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Role.User"/>, <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Role.Assistant"/>, or both)
7180.             and the priority level of the resource. Clients can use this information to filter or prioritize resources for different roles.
7181.             </remarks>
7182.         </member>
7183.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Resource.Size">
7184.             <summary>
7185.             Gets or sets the size of the raw resource content (before base64 encoding), in bytes, if known.
7186.             </summary>
7187.             <remarks>
7188.             This can be used by applications to display file sizes and estimate context window usage.
7189.             </remarks>
7190.         </member>
7191.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Resource.Icons">
7192.             <summary>
7193.             Gets or sets an optional list of icons for this resource.
7194.             </summary>
7195.             <remarks>
7196.             This can be used by clients to display the resource's icon in a user interface.
7197.             </remarks>
7198.         </member>
7199.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Resource.Meta">
7200.             <summary>
7201.             Gets or sets metadata reserved by MCP for protocol-level metadata.
7202.             </summary>
7203.             <remarks>
7204.             Implementations must not make assumptions about its contents.
7205.             </remarks>
7206.         </member>
7207.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ResourceContents">
7208.             <summary>
7209.             Provides a base class representing contents of a resource in the Model Context Protocol.
7210.             </summary>
7211.             <remarks>
7212.             <para>
7213.             <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ResourceContents"/> serves as the base class for different types of resources that can be 
7214.             exchanged through the Model Context Protocol. Resources are identified by URIs and can contain
7215.             different types of data.
7216.             </para>
7217.             <para>
7218.             This class is abstract and has two concrete implementations:
7219.             <list type="bullet">
7220.               <item><description><see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.TextResourceContents"/> - For text-based resources</description></item>
7221.               <item><description><see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.BlobResourceContents"/> - For binary data resources</description></item>
7222.             </list>
7223.             </para>
7224.             <para>
7225.             See the <see href="https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/specification/blob/main/schema/">schema</see> for more details.
7226.             </para>
7227.             </remarks>
7228.         </member>

7231.         </member>

7234.             Gets or sets the URI of the resource.
7235.             </summary>
7236.         </member>
7237.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ResourceContents.MimeType">

7239.             Gets or sets the MIME type of the resource content.
7240.             </summary>
7241.         </member>

7246.             <remarks>

7299.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ResourceTemplate">
7300.             <summary>
7301.             Represents a known resource template that the server is capable of reading.

7303.             <remarks>

7306.             </remarks>

7317.             </summary>

7319.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ResourceTemplate.Description">
7320.             <summary>
7321.             Gets or sets a description of what this resource template represents.
7322.             </summary>
7323.             <remarks>
7324.             <para>
7325.             This description helps clients understand the purpose and content of resources
7326.             that can be generated from this template. It can be used by client applications
7327.             to provide context about available resource types or to display in user interfaces.
7328.             </para>
7329.             <para>
7330.             For AI models, this description can serve as a hint about when and how to use
7331.             the resource template, enhancing the model's ability to generate appropriate URIs.
7332.             </para>
7333.             </remarks>
7334.         </member>
7335.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ResourceTemplate.MimeType">
7336.             <summary>
7337.             Gets or sets the MIME type of this resource template, if known.
7338.             </summary>
7339.             <remarks>
7340.             <para>
7341.             Specifies the expected format of resources that can be generated from this template.
7342.             This helps clients understand what type of content to expect when accessing resources
7343.             created using this template.
7344.             </para>
7345.             <para>
7346.             Common MIME types include "text/plain" for plain text, "application/pdf" for PDF documents,
7347.             "image/png" for PNG images, or "application/json" for JSON data.
7348.             </para>
7349.             </remarks>
7350.         </member>
7351.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ResourceTemplate.Annotations">

7354.             </summary>
7355.             <remarks>

7358.             or prioritize resource templates for different roles.
7359.             </remarks>
7360.         </member>

7380.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ResourceTemplate.AsResource">
7381.             <summary>Converts the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ResourceTemplate"/> into a <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Resource"/>.</summary>
7382.             <returns>A <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Resource"/> if <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ResourceTemplate.IsTemplated"/> is <see langword="false"/>; otherwise, <see langword="null"/>.</returns>
7383.         </member>
7384.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ResourceUpdatedNotificationParams">
7385.             <summary>
7386.             Represents the parameters used with a <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.NotificationMethods.ResourceUpdatedNotification"/>
7387.             notification sent whenever a subscribed resource changes.
7388.             </summary>
7389.             <remarks>
7390.             <para>
7391.             When a client subscribes to resource updates using <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.SubscribeRequestParams"/>, the server will
7392.             send notifications with this payload whenever the subscribed resource is modified. These notifications
7393.             allow clients to maintain synchronized state without needing to poll the server for changes.
7394.             </para>
7395.             <para>
7396.             See the <see href="https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/specification/blob/main/schema/">schema</see> for details.
7397.             </para>
7398.             </remarks>
7399.         </member>
7400.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ResourceUpdatedNotificationParams.Uri">
7401.             <summary>
7402.             Gets or sets the URI of the resource that was updated.
7403.             </summary>
7404.             <remarks>
7405.             The URI can use any protocol; it is up to the server how to interpret it.
7406.             </remarks>
7407.         </member>
7408.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Result">
7409.             <summary>
7410.             Provides a base class for result payloads.
7411.             </summary>
7412.         </member>
7413.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Result.#ctor">
7414.             <summary>Prevent external derivations.</summary>
7415.         </member>
7416.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Result.Meta">
7417.             <summary>
7418.             Gets or sets metadata reserved by MCP for protocol-level metadata.
7419.             </summary>
7420.             <remarks>
