7423.         </member>
7424.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Role">
7425.             <summary>
7426.             Represents the type of role in the Model Context Protocol conversation.
7427.             </summary>
7428.         </member>
7429.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Role.User">
7430.             <summary>
7431.             Corresponds to a human user in the conversation.
7432.             </summary>
7433.         </member>
7434.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Role.Assistant">
7435.             <summary>
7436.             Corresponds to the AI assistant in the conversation.
7437.             </summary>
7438.         </member>
7439.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Root">
7440.             <summary>
7441.             Represents a root URI and its metadata in the Model Context Protocol.
7442.             </summary>
7443.             <remarks>
7444.             Root URIs serve as entry points for resource navigation, typically representing
7445.             top-level directories or container resources that are relevant to the current session.
7446.             Roots inform servers which locations the client considers important, providing informational
7447.             guidance rather than an access-control mechanism. Each root has a URI that uniquely identifies
7448.             it and optional metadata like a human-readable name.
7449.             </remarks>
7450.         </member>
7451.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Root.Uri">
7452.             <summary>
7453.             Gets or sets the URI of the root.
7454.             </summary>
7455.         </member>
7456.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Root.Name">
7457.             <summary>
7458.             Gets or sets a human-readable name for the root.
7459.             </summary>
7460.         </member>
7461.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Root.Meta">
7462.             <summary>
7463.             Gets or sets additional metadata for the root.
7464.             </summary>
7465.             <remarks>
7466.             This is reserved by the protocol for future use.
7467.             </remarks>
7468.         </member>
7469.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RootsCapability">
7470.             <summary>
7471.             Represents a client capability that enables root resource discovery in the Model Context Protocol.
7472.             </summary>
7473.             <remarks>
7474.             <para>
7475.             When present in <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ClientCapabilities"/>, it indicates that the client supports listing
7476.             root URIs that serve as entry points for resource navigation.
7477.             </para>
7478.             <para>
7479.             The roots capability establishes a mechanism for servers to discover the directories and files
7480.             the client considers relevant. Root URIs represent top-level entry points that inform the server
7481.             about the working context, providing informational guidance rather than enforcing access control.
7482.             </para>
7483.             <para>
7484.             See the <see href="https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/specification/blob/main/schema/">schema</see> for details.
7485.             </para>
7486.             </remarks>
7487.         </member>
7488.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RootsCapability.ListChanged">
7489.             <summary>
7490.             Gets or sets a value that indicates whether the client supports notifications for changes to the roots list.
7491.             </summary>
7492.             <remarks>
7493.             When set to <see langword="true"/>, the client can notify servers when roots are added,
7494.             removed, or modified, allowing servers to refresh their roots cache accordingly.
7495.             This enables servers to stay synchronized with client-side changes to available roots.
7496.             </remarks>
7497.         </member>
7498.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RootsListChangedNotificationParams">
7499.             <summary>
7500.             Represents the parameters used with a <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.NotificationMethods.RootsListChangedNotification"/>
7501.             notification from the client to the server, informing it that the list of roots has changed.
7502.             </summary>
7503.             <remarks>
7504.             <para>
7505.             This notification can be issued by clients without any previous subscription from the server.
7506.             </para>
7507.             <para>
7508.             See the <see href="https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/specification/blob/main/schema/">schema</see> for details.
7509.             </para>
7510.             </remarks>
7511.         </member>
7512.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.SamplingCapability">
7513.             <summary>
7514.             Represents the capability for a client to generate text or other content using an AI model.
7515.             </summary>
7516.             <remarks>
7517.             <para>
7518.             This capability enables the MCP client to respond to sampling requests from an MCP server.
7519.             </para>
7520.             <para>
7521.             When this capability is enabled, an MCP server can request the client to generate content
7522.             using an AI model. The client must set a <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClientHandlers.SamplingHandler"/> to process these requests.
7523.             </para>
7524.             </remarks>
7525.         </member>
7526.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.SamplingCapability.Context">
7527.             <summary>
7528.             Gets or sets whether the client supports context inclusion via includeContext parameter.
7529.             </summary>
7530.             <remarks>
7531.             If not declared, servers should only use includeContext: "none".
7532.             </remarks>
7533.         </member>
7534.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.SamplingCapability.Tools">
7535.             <summary>
7536.             Gets or sets whether the client supports tool use via tools and toolChoice parameters.
7537.             </summary>
7538.         </member>
7539.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.SamplingContextCapability">
7540.             <summary>
7541.             Represents the sampling context capability.
7542.             </summary>
7543.         </member>
7544.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.SamplingMessage">
7545.             <summary>
7546.             Represents a message issued to or received from an LLM API within the Model Context Protocol.
7547.             </summary>
7548.             <remarks>
7549.             <para>
7550.             A <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.SamplingMessage"/> encapsulates content sent to or received from AI models in the Model Context Protocol.
7551.             The message has a role (<see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Role.User"/> or <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Role.Assistant"/>) and content which can be text, images,
7552.             audio, tool uses, or tool results.
7553.             </para>
7554.             <para>
7555.             <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.SamplingMessage"/> objects are typically used in collections within <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CreateMessageRequestParams"/>
7556.             to represent prompts or queries for LLM sampling. They form the core data structure for text generation requests
7557.             within the Model Context Protocol.
7558.             </para>
7559.             <para>
7560.             If content contains any <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ToolResultContentBlock"/>, then all content items
7561.             must be <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ToolResultContentBlock"/>. Tool results cannot be mixed with text, image, or
7562.             audio content in the same message.
7563.             </para>
7564.             <para>
7565.             See the <see href="https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/specification/blob/main/schema/">schema</see> for details.
7566.             </para>
7567.             </remarks>
7568.         </member>
7569.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.SamplingMessage.Content">
7570.             <summary>
7571.             Gets or sets the content of the message.
7572.             </summary>
7573.         </member>
7574.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.SamplingMessage.Role">
7575.             <summary>
7576.             Gets or sets the role of the message sender.
7577.             </summary>
7578.         </member>
7579.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.SamplingMessage.Meta">
7580.             <summary>
7581.             Gets or sets metadata reserved by MCP for protocol-level metadata.
7582.             </summary>
7583.             <remarks>
7584.             Implementations must not make assumptions about its contents.
7585.             </remarks>
7586.         </member>
7587.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.SamplingToolsCapability">
7588.             <summary>
7589.             Represents the sampling tools capability.
7590.             </summary>
7591.         </member>
7592.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ServerCapabilities">
7593.             <summary>
7594.             Represents the capabilities that a server supports.
7595.             </summary>
7596.             <remarks>
7597.             <para>
7598.             Server capabilities define the features and functionality available when clients connect.
7599.             These capabilities are advertised to clients during the initialize handshake.
7600.             </para>
7601.             <para>
7602.             See the <see href="https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/specification/blob/main/schema/">schema</see> for details.
7603.             </para>
7604.             </remarks>
7605.         </member>
7606.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ServerCapabilities.Experimental">
7607.             <summary>
7608.             Gets or sets experimental, non-standard capabilities that the server supports.
7609.             </summary>
7610.             <remarks>
7611.             <para>
7612.             The <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ServerCapabilities.Experimental"/> dictionary allows servers to advertise support for features that are not yet
7613.             standardized in the Model Context Protocol specification. This extension mechanism enables
7614.             future protocol enhancements while maintaining backward compatibility.
7615.             </para>
7616.             <para>
7617.             Values in this dictionary are implementation-specific and should be coordinated between client
7618.             and server implementations. Clients should not assume the presence of any experimental capability
7619.             without checking for it first.
7620.             </para>
7621.             </remarks>
7622.         </member>
7623.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ServerCapabilities.Logging">
7624.             <summary>
7625.             Gets or sets a server's logging capability for sending log messages to the client.
7626.             </summary>
7627.         </member>
7628.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ServerCapabilities.Prompts">
7629.             <summary>
7630.             Gets or sets a server's prompts capability for serving predefined prompt templates that clients can discover and use.
7631.             </summary>
7632.         </member>
7633.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ServerCapabilities.Resources">
7634.             <summary>
7635.             Gets or sets a server's resources capability for serving predefined resources that clients can discover and use.
7636.             </summary>
7637.         </member>
7638.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ServerCapabilities.Tools">
7639.             <summary>
7640.             Gets or sets a server's tools capability for listing tools that a client is able to invoke.
7641.             </summary>
7642.         </member>
7643.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ServerCapabilities.Completions">
7644.             <summary>
7645.             Gets or sets a server's completions capability for supporting argument auto-completion suggestions.
7646.             </summary>
7647.         </member>
7648.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ServerCapabilities.Tasks">
7649.             <summary>
7650.             Gets or sets a server's tasks capability for supporting task-augmented requests.
7651.             </summary>
7652.             <remarks>
7653.             <para>
7654.             The tasks capability enables clients to augment their requests with tasks for long-running
7655.             operations. When present, clients can request that certain operations (like tool calls)
7656.             execute asynchronously, with the ability to poll for status and retrieve results later.
7657.             </para>
7658.             <para>
7659.             See <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTasksCapability"/> for details on configuring which operations support tasks.
7660.             </para>
7661.             </remarks>
7662.         </member>
7663.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ServerCapabilities.Extensions">
7664.             <summary>
7665.             Gets or sets optional MCP extensions that the server supports.
7666.             </summary>
7667.             <remarks>
7668.             <para>
7669.             Keys are extension identifiers in reverse domain notation with an extension name
7670.             (e.g., <c>"io.modelcontextprotocol/apps"</c>), and values are per-extension settings
7671.             objects. An empty object indicates support with no additional settings.
7672.             </para>
7673.             <para>
7674.             Extensions provide a framework for extending the Model Context Protocol while maintaining
7675.             interoperability. Servers advertise extension support via this field during the initialization handshake.
7676.             </para>
7677.             </remarks>
7678.         </member>
7679.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.SetLevelRequestParams">
7680.             <summary>
7681.             Represents the parameters used with a <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.LoggingSetLevel"/> request from a client
7682.             to enable or adjust logging.
7683.             </summary>
7684.             <remarks>
7685.             This request allows clients to configure the level of logging information they want to receive from the server.
7686.             The server will send notifications for log events at the specified level and all higher (more severe) levels.
7687.             </remarks>
7688.         </member>
7689.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.SetLevelRequestParams.Level">
7690.             <summary>
7691.             Gets or sets the level of logging that the client wants to receive from the server. 
7692.             </summary>
7693.         </member>
7694.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.SingleItemOrListConverter`1">
7695.             <summary>
7696.             Provides a JSON converter for <see cref="T:System.Collections.Generic.IList`1"/> that handles both array and single object representations.
7697.             </summary>
7698.         </member>
7699.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.SingleItemOrListConverter`1.Read(System.Text.Json.Utf8JsonReader@,System.Type,System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions)">
7700.             <inheritdoc />
7701.         </member>
7702.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.SingleItemOrListConverter`1.Write(System.Text.Json.Utf8JsonWriter,System.Collections.Generic.IList{`0},System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions)">
7703.             <inheritdoc />
7704.         </member>
7705.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.SubscribeRequestParams">
7706.             <summary>
7707.             Represents the parameters used with a <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.ResourcesSubscribe"/> request from a client
7708.             to request real-time notifications from the server whenever a particular resource changes.
7709.             </summary>
7710.             <remarks>
7711.             <para>
7712.             The subscription mechanism allows clients to be notified about changes to specific resources
7713.             identified by their URI. When a subscribed resource changes, the server sends a notification
7714.             to the client with the updated resource information.
7715.             </para>
7716.             <para>
7717.             Subscriptions remain active until explicitly canceled using <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.UnsubscribeRequestParams"/>
7718.             or until the connection is terminated.
7719.             </para>
7720.             <para>
7721.             The server might refuse or limit subscriptions based on its capabilities or resource constraints.
7722.             </para>
7723.             </remarks>
7724.         </member>
7725.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.SubscribeRequestParams.Uri">
7726.             <summary>
7727.             Gets or sets the URI of the resource to subscribe to.
7728.             </summary>
7729.             <remarks>
7730.             The URI can use any protocol; it is up to the server how to interpret it.
7731.             </remarks>
7732.         </member>
7733.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.TextResourceContents">
7734.             <summary>
7735.             Represents text-based contents of a resource in the Model Context Protocol.
7736.             </summary>
7737.             <remarks>
7738.             <para>
7739.             <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.TextResourceContents"/> is used when textual data needs to be exchanged through
7740.             the Model Context Protocol. The text is stored directly in the <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.TextResourceContents.Text"/> property.
7741.             </para>
7742.             <para>
7743.             This class inherits from <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ResourceContents"/>, which also has a sibling implementation
7744.             <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.BlobResourceContents"/> for binary resources. When working with resources, the
7745.             appropriate type is chosen based on the nature of the content.
7746.             </para>
7747.             <para>
7748.             See the <see href="https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/specification/blob/main/schema/">schema</see> for more details.
7749.             </para>
7750.             </remarks>
7751.         </member>
7752.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.TextResourceContents.Text">
7753.             <summary>
7754.             Gets or sets the text of the item.
7755.             </summary>
7756.         </member>
7757.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.TextResourceContents.ToString">
7758.             <inheritdoc/>
7759.         </member>
7760.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.TimeSpanMillisecondsConverter">
7761.             <summary>
7762.             Provides a JSON converter for <see cref="T:System.TimeSpan"/> that serializes as integer milliseconds.
7763.             </summary>
7764.             <remarks>
7765.             This converter serializes TimeSpan values as the total number of milliseconds (as an integer),
7766.             and deserializes integer millisecond values back to TimeSpan. System.Text.Json automatically
7767.             handles nullable TimeSpan properties using this converter.
7768.             </remarks>
7769.         </member>

7771.             <inheritdoc />
7772.         </member>

7774.             <inheritdoc />
7775.         </member>
7776.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Tool">
7777.             <summary>
7778.             Represents a tool that the server is capable of calling.
7779.             </summary>
7780.         </member>
7781.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Tool.Name">
7782.             <inheritdoc />
7783.         </member>
7784.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Tool.Title">
7785.             <inheritdoc />
7786.         </member>
7787.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Tool.Description">
7788.             <summary>
7789.             Gets or sets a human-readable description of the tool.
7790.             </summary>
7791.             <remarks>
7792.             <para>
7793.             This description helps the AI model understand what the tool does and when to use it.
7794.             It should be clear, concise, and accurately describe the tool's purpose and functionality.
7795.             </para>
7796.             <para>
7797.             The description is typically presented to AI models to help them determine when
7798.             and how to use the tool based on user requests. A well-written description significantly
7799.             reduces incorrect tool invocations. Include information about what the tool does, any
7800.             constraints or prerequisites, and what it returns.
7801.             </para>
7802.             <para>
7803.             Similarly, individual parameter descriptions (provided via <see cref="T:System.ComponentModel.DescriptionAttribute"/>
7804.             on tool method parameters) are important for guiding the model to supply correct argument values.
7805.             Descriptions should document expected formats, valid value ranges, and any other constraints
7806.             the model should be aware of.
7807.             </para>
7808.             </remarks>
7809.         </member>
7810.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Tool.InputSchema">
7811.             <summary>
7812.             Gets or sets a JSON Schema object defining the expected parameters for the tool.
7813.             </summary>
7814.             <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentException">The value is not a valid MCP tool JSON schema.</exception>
7815.             <remarks>
7816.             <para>
7817.             The schema must be a valid JSON Schema object with the "type" property set to "object".
7818.             This is enforced by validation in the setter which will throw an <see cref="T:System.ArgumentException"/>
7819.             if an invalid schema is provided.
7820.             </para>
7821.             <para>
7822.             The schema typically defines the properties (parameters) that the tool accepts,
7823.             their types, and which ones are required. This helps AI models understand
7824.             how to structure their calls to the tool.
7825.             </para>
7826.             <para>
7827.             If not explicitly set, a default minimal schema of <c>{"type":"object"}</c> is used.
7828.             </para>
7829.             </remarks>
7830.         </member>
7831.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Tool.OutputSchema">
7832.             <summary>
7833.             Gets or sets a JSON Schema object defining the expected structured outputs for the tool.
7834.             </summary>
7835.             <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentException">The value is not a valid MCP tool JSON schema.</exception>
7836.             <remarks>
7837.             <para>
7838.             The schema must be a valid JSON Schema object with the "type" property set to "object".
7839.             This is enforced by validation in the setter which will throw an <see cref="T:System.ArgumentException"/>
7840.             if an invalid schema is provided.
7841.             </para>
7842.             <para>
7843.             The schema should describe the shape of the data as returned in <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolResult.StructuredContent"/>.
7844.             </para>
7845.             </remarks>
7846.         </member>
7847.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Tool.Annotations">
7848.             <summary>
7849.             Gets or sets optional additional tool information and behavior hints.
7850.             </summary>
7851.             <remarks>
7852.             These annotations provide metadata about the tool's behavior, such as whether it's read-only,
7853.             destructive, idempotent, or operates in an open world. They also can include a human-readable title.
7854.             Note that these are hints and should not be relied upon for security decisions.
7855.             </remarks>
7856.         </member>
7857.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Tool.Execution">
7858.             <summary>
7859.             Gets or sets execution-related metadata for this tool.
7860.             </summary>
7861.             <remarks>
7862.             This property provides hints about how the tool should be executed, particularly
7863.             regarding task augmentation support. See <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ToolExecution"/> for details.
7864.             </remarks>
7865.         </member>
7866.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Tool.Icons">
7867.             <summary>
7868.             Gets or sets an optional list of icons for this tool.
7869.             </summary>
7870.             <remarks>
7871.             This can be used by clients to display the tool's icon in a user interface.
7872.             </remarks>
7873.         </member>
7874.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Tool.Meta">
7875.             <summary>
7876.             Gets or sets metadata reserved by MCP for protocol-level metadata.
7877.             </summary>
7878.             <remarks>
7879.             Implementations must not make assumptions about its contents.
7880.             </remarks>
7881.         </member>
7882.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ToolAnnotations">
7883.             <summary>
7884.             Represents additional properties describing a <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Tool"/> to clients.
7885.             </summary>
7886.             <remarks>
7887.             All properties in <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ToolAnnotations"/> are hints.
7888.             They are not guaranteed to provide a faithful description of tool behavior (including descriptive properties like `title`).
7889.             Clients should never make tool use decisions based on <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ToolAnnotations"/> received from untrusted servers.
7890.             </remarks>
7891.         </member>
7892.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ToolAnnotations.Title">
7893.             <summary>
7894.             Gets or sets a human-readable title for the tool that can be displayed to users.
7895.             </summary>
7896.             <remarks>
7897.             <para>
7898.             The title provides a more descriptive, user-friendly name for the tool than the tool's
7899.             programmatic name. It is intended for display purposes and to help users understand
7900.             the tool's purpose at a glance.
7901.             </para>
7902.             <para>
7903.             Unlike the tool name (which follows programmatic naming conventions), the title can
7904.             include spaces, special characters, and be phrased in a more natural language style.
7905.             </para>
7906.             </remarks>
7907.         </member>
7908.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ToolAnnotations.DestructiveHint">
7909.             <summary>
7910.             Gets or sets a value that indicates whether the tool can perform destructive updates to its environment.
7911.             </summary>
7912.             <value>
7913.             <see langword="true"/> if the tool can perform destructive updates to its environment;
7914.             <see langword="false"/> if the tool performs only additive updates;
7915.             <see langword="null"/> if unspecified, in which case clients should assume <see langword="true"/>.
7916.             </value>
7917.             <remarks>
7918.             This property is most relevant when the tool modifies its environment (ReadOnly = false).
7919.             </remarks>
7920.         </member>
7921.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ToolAnnotations.IdempotentHint">
7922.             <summary>
7923.             Gets or sets a value that indicates whether calling the tool repeatedly with the same arguments
7924.             has no additional effect on its environment.
7925.             </summary>
7926.             <value>
7927.             <see langword="true"/> if calling the tool repeatedly with the same arguments
7928.             has no additional effect on the environment; <see langword="false"/> if it does;
7929.             <see langword="null"/> if unspecified, in which case clients should assume <see langword="false"/>.
7930.             </value>
7931.             <remarks>
7932.             This property is most relevant when the tool modifies its environment (ReadOnly = false).
7933.             </remarks>
7934.         </member>
7935.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ToolAnnotations.OpenWorldHint">
7936.             <summary>
7937.             Gets or sets a value that indicates whether this tool can interact with an "open world" of external entities.
7938.             </summary>
7939.             <value>
7940.             <see langword="true"/> if the tool can interact with an unpredictable or dynamic set of entities (like web search);
7941.             <see langword="false"/> if the tool's domain of interaction is closed and well-defined (like memory access);
7942.             <see langword="null"/> if unspecified, in which case clients should assume <see langword="true"/>.
7943.             </value>
7944.         </member>
7945.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ToolAnnotations.ReadOnlyHint">
7946.             <summary>
7947.             Gets or sets a value that indicates whether this tool modifies its environment.
7948.             </summary>
7949.             <value>
7950.             <see langword="true"/> if the tool only performs read operations without changing state;
7951.             <see langword="false"/> if the tool can make modifications to its environment;
7952.             <see langword="null"/> if unspecified, in which case clients should assume <see langword="false"/>.
7953.             </value>
7954.             <remarks>
7955.             <para>
7956.             Read-only tools do not have side effects beyond computational resource usage.
7957.             They don't create, update, or delete data in any system.
7958.             </para>
7959.             </remarks>
7960.         </member>
7961.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ToolChoice">
7962.             <summary>
7963.             Controls tool selection behavior for sampling requests.
7964.             </summary>
7965.         </member>
7966.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ToolChoice.Mode">
7967.             <summary>
7968.             Gets or sets the mode that controls which tools the model can call.
7969.             </summary>
7970.             <remarks>
7971.             <list type="bullet">
7972.             <item><term>"auto"</term><description>Model decides whether to call tools (default)</description></item>
7973.             <item><term>"required"</term><description>Model must call at least one tool</description></item>
7974.             <item><term>"none"</term><description>Model must not call any tools</description></item>
7975.             </list>
7976.             </remarks>
7977.         </member>
7978.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ToolChoice.ModeAuto">
7979.             <summary>The mode value "auto".</summary>
7980.         </member>
7981.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ToolChoice.ModeRequired">
7982.             <summary>The mode value "required".</summary>
7983.         </member>
7984.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ToolChoice.ModeNone">
7985.             <summary>The mode value "none".</summary>
7986.         </member>
7987.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ToolExecution">
7988.             <summary>
7989.             Represents execution-related metadata for a tool.
7990.             </summary>
7991.             <remarks>
7992.             This type provides hints about how a tool should be executed, particularly
7993.             regarding task augmentation support.
7994.             </remarks>
7995.         </member>
7996.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ToolExecution.TaskSupport">
7997.             <summary>
7998.             Gets or sets the level of task augmentation support for this tool.
7999.             </summary>
8000.             <remarks>
8001.             <para>
8002.             This property declares whether a tool supports task-augmented execution:
8003.             <list type="bullet">
8004.             <item><description><see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ToolTaskSupport.Forbidden"/>: Clients must not attempt to invoke 
8005.             the tool as a task. This is the default behavior.</description></item>
8006.             <item><description><see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ToolTaskSupport.Optional"/>: Clients may invoke the tool as a task 
8007.             or as a normal request.</description></item>
8008.             <item><description><see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ToolTaskSupport.Required"/>: Clients must invoke the tool as a task.
8009.             </description></item>
8010.             </list>
8011.             </para>
8012.             <para>
8013.             This is a fine-grained layer in addition to server capabilities. Even if a server's capabilities
8014.             include tasks.requests.tools.call, this property controls whether each specific tool supports tasks.
8015.             </para>
8016.             </remarks>
8017.         </member>
8018.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ToolTaskSupport">
8019.             <summary>
8020.             Represents the level of task augmentation support for a tool.
8021.             </summary>
8022.             <remarks>
8023.             <para>
8024.             This enum defines how a tool interacts with the task augmentation system:
8025.             <list type="bullet">
8026.             <item><description><see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ToolTaskSupport.Forbidden"/>: Task augmentation is not allowed (default)</description></item>
8027.             <item><description><see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ToolTaskSupport.Optional"/>: Task augmentation is supported but not required</description></item>
8028.             <item><description><see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ToolTaskSupport.Required"/>: Task augmentation is mandatory</description></item>
8029.             </list>
8030.             </para>
8031.             </remarks>
8032.         </member>
8033.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ToolTaskSupport.Forbidden">
8034.             <summary>
8035.             Clients must not attempt to invoke the tool as a task.
8036.             </summary>
8037.             <remarks>
8038.             This is the default behavior. Servers should return a -32601 (Method not found) error
8039.             if a client attempts to invoke the tool as a task when this is set.
8040.             </remarks>
8041.         </member>
8042.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ToolTaskSupport.Optional">
8043.             <summary>
8044.             Clients may invoke the tool as a task or as a normal request.
8045.             </summary>
8046.             <remarks>
8047.             When this is set, clients can choose whether to use task augmentation based on their needs.
8048.             </remarks>
8049.         </member>
8050.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ToolTaskSupport.Required">
8051.             <summary>
8052.             Clients must invoke the tool as a task.
8053.             </summary>
8054.             <remarks>
8055.             Servers must return a -32601 (Method not found) error if a client does not attempt
8056.             to invoke the tool as a task when this is set.
8057.             </remarks>
8058.         </member>
8059.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ToolListChangedNotificationParams">
8060.             <summary>
8061.             Represents the parameters used with a <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.NotificationMethods.ToolListChangedNotification"/>
8062.             notification from the server to the client, informing it that the list of tools it offers has changed.
8063.             </summary>
8064.             <remarks>
8065.             <para>
8066.             This notification can be issued by servers without any previous subscription from the client.
8067.             </para>
8068.             <para>
8069.             See the <see href="https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/specification/blob/main/schema/">schema</see> for details.
8070.             </para>
8071.             </remarks>
8072.         </member>
8073.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ToolsCapability">
8074.             <summary>
8075.             Represents the tools capability configuration.
8076.             See the <see href="https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/specification/blob/main/schema/">schema</see> for details.
8077.             </summary>
8078.         </member>
8079.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ToolsCapability.ListChanged">
8080.             <summary>
8081.             Gets or sets a value that indicates whether this server supports notifications for changes to the tool list.
8082.             </summary>
8083.             <remarks>
8084.             When set to <see langword="true"/>, the server sends notifications using
8085.             <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.NotificationMethods.ToolListChangedNotification"/> when tools are added,
8086.             removed, or modified. Clients can register handlers for these notifications to
8087.             refresh their tool cache. This capability enables clients to stay synchronized with server-side
8088.             changes to available tools.
8089.             </remarks>
8090.         </member>
8091.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.TransportBase">
8092.             <summary>

8101.             Custom transport implementations should inherit from this class and implement the abstract
8102.             <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.TransportBase.SendMessageAsync(ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcMessage,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/> and <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.TransportBase.DisposeAsync"/> methods
8103.             to handle the specific transport mechanism being used.
8104.             </para>
8105.             </remarks>
8106.         </member>

8176.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.UnsubscribeRequestParams">
8177.             <summary>
8178.             Represents a message sent from the client to cancel resource update notifications from the server for a specific resource.
8179.             </summary>
8180.             <remarks>
8181.             <para>
8182.             After a client has subscribed to resource updates using <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.SubscribeRequestParams"/>,
8183.             this message can be sent to stop receiving notifications for a specific resource.
8184.             This action is useful for conserving resources and network bandwidth when
8185.             the client no longer needs to track changes to a particular resource.
8186.             </para>
8187.             <para>
8188.             The unsubscribe operation is idempotent, meaning it can be called multiple times
8189.             for the same resource without causing errors, even if there is no active subscription.
8190.             </para>
8191.             </remarks>
8192.         </member>
8193.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.UnsubscribeRequestParams.Uri">
8194.             <summary>
8195.             Gets or sets the URI of the resource to unsubscribe from. The URI can use any protocol; it is up to the server how to interpret it.
8196.             </summary>
8197.         </member>
8198.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.UrlElicitationCapability">
8199.             <summary>
8200.             Represents the capability for URL mode (out-of-band) elicitation.
8201.             </summary>
8202.             <remarks>
8203.             <para>
8204.             This capability enables secure out-of-band interactions where the user is directed to a URL
8205.             (typically opened in a browser) to complete sensitive operations like OAuth authorization,
8206.             payments, or credential entry.
8207.             </para>
8208.             <para>
8209.             Unlike form mode, sensitive data in URL mode is never exposed to the MCP client, providing
8210.             better security for sensitive interactions.
8211.             </para>
8212.             </remarks>
8213.         </member>
8214.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.UrlElicitationRequiredErrorData">
8215.             <summary>
8216.             Represents the payload for the <c>URL_ELICITATION_REQUIRED</c> JSON-RPC error.
8217.             </summary>
8218.         </member>
8219.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.UrlElicitationRequiredErrorData.Elicitations">
8220.             <summary>
8221.             Gets or sets the elicitations that must be completed before retrying the original request.
8222.             </summary>
8223.         </member>
8224.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Diagnostics.ExplicitBucketBoundaries">
8225.             <summary>
8226.             ExplicitBucketBoundaries specified in MCP semantic conventions for all MCP metrics.
8227.             See https://github.com/open-telemetry/semantic-conventions/blob/main/docs/gen-ai/mcp.md#metrics
8228.             </summary>
8229.         </member>
8230.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.Diagnostics.TryGetOuterToolExecutionActivity(System.Diagnostics.Activity@)">
8231.             <summary>
8232.             If outer GenAI instrumentation is already tracing the tool execution,
8233.             MCP instrumentation SHOULD add MCP-specific attributes to the existing tool execution span instead
8234.             of creating a new one.
8235.             </summary>
8236.             <param name="activity">The outer activity for tool execution, if found.</param>
8237.             <returns>true if an outer tool execution activity was found and can be reused; false otherwise.</returns>
8238.         </member>
8239.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.JsonRpcMessageFilter">
8240.             <summary>
8241.             Represents a filter that wraps the processing of incoming JSON-RPC messages.
8242.             </summary>
8243.             <param name="next">The next handler in the pipeline.</param>
8244.             <returns>A wrapped handler that processes messages and optionally delegates to the next handler.</returns>
8245.         </member>
8246.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.McpErrorCode">
8247.             <summary>
8248.             Represents standard JSON-RPC error codes as defined in the MCP specification.
8249.             </summary>
8250.         </member>
8251.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.McpErrorCode.ResourceNotFound">
8252.             <summary>
8253.             Indicates that the requested resource could not be found.
8254.             </summary>
8255.             <remarks>
8256.             This error should be used when a resource URI does not match any available resource on the server.
8257.             It allows clients to distinguish between missing resources and other types of errors.
8258.             </remarks>
8259.         </member>
8260.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.McpErrorCode.UrlElicitationRequired">
8261.             <summary>
8262.             Indicates that URL-mode elicitation is required to complete the requested operation.
8263.             </summary>
8264.             <remarks>
8265.             <para>
8266.             This error is returned when a server operation requires additional user input through URL-mode elicitation
8267.             before it can proceed. The error data must include the `data.elicitations` payload describing the pending
8268.             elicitation(s) for the client to present to the user.
8269.             </para>
8270.             <para>
8271.             Common scenarios include OAuth authorization and other out-of-band flows that cannot be completed inside
8272.             the MCP client.
8273.             </para>
8274.             </remarks>
8275.         </member>
8276.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.McpErrorCode.InvalidRequest">
8277.             <summary>
8278.             Indicates that the JSON payload does not conform to the expected Request object structure.
8279.             </summary>
8280.             <remarks>
8281.             The request is considered invalid if it lacks required fields or fails to follow the JSON-RPC protocol.
8282.             </remarks>
8283.         </member>
8284.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.McpErrorCode.MethodNotFound">
8285.             <summary>
8286.             Indicates that the requested method does not exist or is not available.
8287.             </summary>
8288.             <remarks>
8289.             <para>
8290.             In MCP, this error is returned when a request is made for a method that requires a capability
8291.             that has not been declared. This can occur in either direction:
8292.             </para>
8293.             <list type="bullet">
8294.             <item><description>A server returning this error when the client requests a capability it doesn't support
8295.             (for example, requesting completions when the <c>completions</c> capability was not advertised).</description></item>
8296.             <item><description>A client returning this error when the server requests a capability it doesn't support
8297.             (for example, requesting roots when the client did not declare the <c>roots</c> capability).</description></item>
8298.             </list>
8299.             </remarks>
8300.         </member>
8301.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.McpErrorCode.InvalidParams">
8302.             <summary>
8303.             Indicates that the request parameters are invalid at the protocol level.
8304.             </summary>
8305.             <remarks>
8306.             <para>
8307.             In MCP, this error is returned for protocol-level parameter validation failures in various contexts:
8308.             </para>
8309.             <list type="bullet">
8310.             <item><description><b>Tools</b>: Unknown tool name or invalid protocol-level tool arguments.</description></item>
8311.             <item><description><b>Prompts</b>: Unknown prompt name or missing required protocol-level arguments.</description></item>
8312.             <item><description><b>Pagination</b>: Invalid or expired cursor values.</description></item>
8313.             <item><description><b>Logging</b>: Invalid log level.</description></item>
8314.             <item><description><b>Tasks</b>: Invalid or nonexistent task ID or invalid cursor.</description></item>
8315.             <item><description><b>Elicitation</b>: Server requests an elicitation mode not declared in client capabilities.</description></item>
8316.             <item><description><b>Sampling</b>: Missing tool result or tool results mixed with other content.</description></item>
8317.             </list>
8318.             <para>
8319.             Note: Application-layer validation errors within tool/prompt/resource arguments should be reported as execution errors
8320.             (for example, via <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolResult.IsError"/>) rather than as protocol errors, allowing language
8321.             models to receive error feedback and self-correct.
8322.             </para>
8323.             </remarks>
8324.         </member>
8325.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.McpErrorCode.InternalError">
8326.             <summary>
8327.             Indicates that an internal error occurred while processing the request.
8328.             </summary>
8329.             <remarks>
8330.             This error is used when the endpoint encounters an unexpected condition that prevents it from fulfilling the request.
8331.             </remarks>
8332.         </member>
8333.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.McpErrorCode.ParseError">
8334.             <summary>
8335.             Indicates that the JSON received could not be parsed.
8336.             </summary>
8337.             <remarks>
8338.             This error occurs when the input contains malformed JSON or incorrect syntax.
8339.             </remarks>
8340.         </member>
8341.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.McpException">
8342.              <summary>
8343.              Represents an exception that is thrown when a Model Context Protocol (MCP) error occurs.
8344.              </summary>
8345.              <remarks>
8346.              The <see cref="P:System.Exception.Message"/> from a <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.McpException"/> might be propagated to the remote
8347.              endpoint; sensitive information should not be included. If sensitive details need to be included,
8348.              a different exception type should be used.
8349.             
8350.              This exception type can be thrown by MCP tools or tool call filters to propagate detailed error messages
8351.              from <see cref="P:System.Exception.Message"/> when a tool execution fails via a <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolResult"/>.
8352.              This includes input validation errors, business logic errors, or any other failure that the model should
8353.              be informed about. For example, if a required field is missing or a value is out of range, throwing an
8354.              <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.McpException"/> with a descriptive message allows the model to understand the issue and
8355.              potentially self-correct in a subsequent request.
8356.             
8357.              For non-tool calls, this exception controls the message propagated via a <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcError"/>.
8358.             
8359.              <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.McpProtocolException"/> is a derived type that can be used to also specify the
8360.              <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.McpErrorCode"/> that should be used for the resulting <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcError"/>.
8361.              </remarks>
8362.         </member>
8363.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.McpException.#ctor">
8364.             <summary>
8365.             Initializes a new instance of the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.McpException"/> class.
8366.             </summary>
8367.         </member>
8368.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.McpException.#ctor(System.String)">
8369.             <summary>
8370.             Initializes a new instance of the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.McpException"/> class with a specified error message.
8371.             </summary>
8372.             <param name="message">The message that describes the error.</param>
8373.         </member>
8374.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.McpException.#ctor(System.String,System.Exception)">
8375.             <summary>
8376.             Initializes a new instance of the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.McpException"/> class with a specified error message and
8377.             a reference to the inner exception that is the cause of this exception.
8378.             </summary>
8379.             <param name="message">The message that describes the error.</param>

8383.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.McpJsonUtilities">
8384.             <summary>Provides a collection of utility methods for working with JSON data in the context of MCP.</summary>

8386.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.McpJsonUtilities.DefaultOptions">
8387.             <summary>
8388.             Gets the <see cref="T:System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions"/> singleton used as the default in JSON serialization operations.
8389.             </summary>
8390.             <remarks>
8391.             <para>
8392.             For Native AOT or applications disabling <see cref="P:System.Text.Json.JsonSerializer.IsReflectionEnabledByDefault"/>, this instance
8393.             includes source generated contracts for all common exchange types contained in the ModelContextProtocol library.
8394.             </para>
8395.             <para>
8396.             It additionally turns on the following settings:
8397.             <list type="number">
8398.             <item>Enables <see cref="F:System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerDefaults.Web"/> defaults.</item>
8399.             <item>Enables <see cref="F:System.Text.Json.Serialization.JsonIgnoreCondition.WhenWritingNull"/> as the default ignore condition for properties.</item>
8400.             <item>Enables <see cref="F:System.Text.Json.Serialization.JsonNumberHandling.AllowReadingFromString"/> as the default number handling for number types.</item>
8401.             </list>
8402.             </para>
8403.             </remarks>
8404.         </member>

9485.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.McpProtocolException">
9486.             <summary>
9487.             Represents an exception that is thrown when a Model Context Protocol (MCP) protocol-level error occurs.
9488.             </summary>
9489.             <remarks>
9490.             <para>
9491.             This exception is used to represent failures related to protocol-level concerns, such as malformed
9492.             JSON-RPC requests, unknown methods, unknown primitive names (tools/prompts/resources), or internal
9493.             server errors. It is not intended to be used for tool execution errors, including input validation failures.
9494.             </para>
9495.             <para>
9496.             Tool execution errors (including input validation errors, API failures, and business logic errors)
9497.             should be returned in the result object with <c>IsError</c> set to <see langword="true"/>, allowing
9498.             language models to see error details and self-correct. Only protocol-level issues should throw
9499.             <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.McpProtocolException"/>.
9500.             </para>
9501.             <para>
9502.             <see cref="P:System.Exception.Message"/> or <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.McpProtocolException.ErrorCode"/> from a <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.McpProtocolException"/> may be
9503.             propagated to the remote endpoint; sensitive information should not be included. If sensitive details need
9504.             to be included, a different exception type should be used.
9505.             </para>
9506.             </remarks>
9507.         </member>
9508.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.McpProtocolException.#ctor">
9509.             <summary>
9510.             Initializes a new instance of the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.McpProtocolException"/> class.
9511.             </summary>
9512.         </member>
9513.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.McpProtocolException.#ctor(System.String)">
9514.             <summary>
9515.             Initializes a new instance of the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.McpProtocolException"/> class with a specified error message.
9516.             </summary>
9517.             <param name="message">The message that describes the error.</param>
9518.         </member>
9519.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.McpProtocolException.#ctor(System.String,System.Exception)">
9520.             <summary>
9521.             Initializes a new instance of the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.McpProtocolException"/> class with a specified error message and a reference to the inner exception that is the cause of this exception.
9522.             </summary>
9523.             <param name="message">The message that describes the error.</param>
9524.             <param name="innerException">The exception that is the cause of the current exception, or a null reference if no inner exception is specified.</param>
9525.         </member>
9526.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.McpProtocolException.#ctor(System.String,ModelContextProtocol.McpErrorCode)">
9527.             <summary>
9528.             Initializes a new instance of the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.McpProtocolException"/> class with a specified error message and JSON-RPC error code.
9529.             </summary>
9530.             <param name="message">The message that describes the error.</param>
9531.             <param name="errorCode">An <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.McpErrorCode"/>.</param>
9532.         </member>
9533.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.McpProtocolException.#ctor(System.String,System.Exception,ModelContextProtocol.McpErrorCode)">
9534.             <summary>
9535.             Initializes a new instance of the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.McpProtocolException"/> class with a specified error message, inner exception, and JSON-RPC error code.
9536.             </summary>
9537.             <param name="message">The message that describes the error.</param>
9538.             <param name="innerException">The exception that is the cause of the current exception, or a null reference if no inner exception is specified.</param>
9539.             <param name="errorCode">An <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.McpErrorCode"/>.</param>
9540.         </member>
9541.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.McpProtocolException.ErrorCode">
9542.             <summary>
9543.             Gets the error code associated with this exception.
9544.             </summary>
9545.             <remarks>
9546.             This property contains a standard JSON-RPC error code as defined in the MCP specification. Common error codes include:
9547.             <list type="bullet">
9548.             <item><description>-32700: Parse error - Invalid JSON received</description></item>
9549.             <item><description>-32600: Invalid request - The JSON is not a valid Request object</description></item>
9550.             <item><description>-32601: Method not found - The method does not exist or is not available</description></item>
9551.             <item><description>-32602: Invalid params - Malformed request or unknown primitive name (tool/prompt/resource)</description></item>
9552.             <item><description>-32603: Internal error - Internal JSON-RPC error</description></item>
9553.             </list>
9554.             </remarks>
9555.         </member>
9556.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.McpSession">
9557.             <summary>
9558.             Represents a client or server Model Context Protocol (MCP) session.
9559.             </summary>
9560.             <remarks>
9561.             <para>
9562.             The MCP session provides the core communication functionality used by both clients and servers:
9563.             <list type="bullet">
9564.               <item>Sending JSON-RPC requests and receiving responses.</item>
9565.               <item>Sending notifications to the connected session.</item>
9566.               <item>Registering handlers for receiving notifications.</item>
9567.             </list>
9568.             </para>
9569.             <para>
9570.             <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.McpSession"/> serves as the base class for both <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClient"/> and
9571.             <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServer"/>, providing the common functionality needed for MCP protocol
9572.             communication. Most applications will use these more specific interfaces rather than working with
9573.             <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.McpSession"/> directly.
9574.             </para>
9575.             <para>
9576.             All MCP sessions should be properly disposed after use as they implement <see cref="T:System.IAsyncDisposable"/>.
9577.             </para>
9578.             </remarks>
9579.         </member>
9580.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.McpSession.SessionId">
9581.             <summary>Gets an identifier associated with the current MCP session.</summary>
9582.             <remarks>
9583.             Typically populated in transports supporting multiple sessions, such as Streamable HTTP or SSE.
9584.             Can return <see langword="null"/> if the session hasn't initialized or if the transport doesn't
9585.             support multiple sessions (as is the case with STDIO).
9586.             </remarks>
9587.         </member>
9588.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.McpSession.NegotiatedProtocolVersion">
9589.             <summary>
9590.             Gets the negotiated protocol version for the current MCP session.
9591.             </summary>
9592.             <remarks>
9593.             Returns the protocol version negotiated during session initialization,
9594.             or <see langword="null"/> if initialization hasn't yet occurred.
9595.             </remarks>
9596.         </member>
9597.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.McpSession.SendRequestAsync(ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcRequest,System.Threading.CancellationToken)">
9598.             <summary>
9599.             Sends a JSON-RPC request to the connected session and waits for a response.
9600.             </summary>
9601.             <param name="request">The JSON-RPC request to send.</param>
9602.             <param name="cancellationToken">The <see cref="T:System.Threading.CancellationToken"/> to monitor for cancellation requests. The default is <see cref="P:System.Threading.CancellationToken.None"/>.</param>
9603.             <returns>A task containing the session's response.</returns>
9604.             <exception cref="T:System.InvalidOperationException">The transport is not connected, or another error occurred during request processing.</exception>
9605.             <exception cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.McpException">An error occurred during request processing.</exception>
9606.             <remarks>
9607.             This method provides low-level access to send raw JSON-RPC requests. For most use cases,
9608.             consider using the strongly-typed methods that provide a more convenient API.
9609.             </remarks>
9610.         </member>
9611.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.McpSession.SendMessageAsync(ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcMessage,System.Threading.CancellationToken)">
9612.             <summary>
9613.             Sends a JSON-RPC message to the connected session.
9614.             </summary>
9615.             <param name="message">
9616.             The JSON-RPC message to send. This can be any type that implements JsonRpcMessage, such as
9617.             JsonRpcRequest, JsonRpcResponse, JsonRpcNotification, or JsonRpcError.
9618.             </param>
9619.             <param name="cancellationToken">The <see cref="T:System.Threading.CancellationToken"/> to monitor for cancellation requests. The default is <see cref="P:System.Threading.CancellationToken.None"/>.</param>
9620.             <returns>A task that represents the asynchronous send operation.</returns>
9621.             <exception cref="T:System.InvalidOperationException">The transport is not connected.</exception>
9622.             <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="message"/> is <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
9623.             <remarks>
9624.             <para>
9625.             This method provides low-level access to send any JSON-RPC message. For specific message types,
9626.             consider using the higher-level methods such as <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.McpSession.SendRequestAsync(ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcRequest,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/> or methods
9627.             on this class that provide a simpler API.
9628.             </para>
9629.             <para>
9630.             The method serializes the message and transmits it using the underlying transport mechanism.
9631.             </para>
9632.             </remarks>
9633.         </member>
9634.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.McpSession.RegisterNotificationHandler(System.String,System.Func{ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcNotification,System.Threading.CancellationToken,System.Threading.Tasks.ValueTask})">

9640.             <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentException"><paramref name="method"/> is empty or composed entirely of whitespace.</exception>

9675.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.McpSession.SendNotificationAsync(System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)">
9676.             <summary>
9677.             Sends a parameterless notification to the connected session.
9678.             </summary>
9679.             <param name="method">The notification method name.</param>
9680.             <param name="cancellationToken">The <see cref="T:System.Threading.CancellationToken"/> to monitor for cancellation requests. The default is <see cref="P:System.Threading.CancellationToken.None"/>.</param>
9681.             <returns>A task that represents the asynchronous send operation.</returns>
9682.             <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="method"/> is <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
9683.             <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentException"><paramref name="method"/> is empty or composed entirely of whitespace.</exception>
9684.             <remarks>
9685.             <para>
9686.             This method sends a notification without any parameters. Notifications are one-way messages
9687.             that don't expect a response. They are commonly used for events, status updates, or to signal
9688.             changes in state.
9689.             </para>
9690.             </remarks>
9691.         </member>
9692.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.McpSession.SendNotificationAsync``1(System.String,``0,System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)">
9693.             <summary>
9694.             Sends a notification with parameters to the connected session.
9695.             </summary>
9696.             <typeparam name="TParameters">The type of the notification parameters to serialize.</typeparam>
9697.             <param name="method">The JSON-RPC method name for the notification.</param>
9698.             <param name="parameters">The notification parameters.</param>
9699.             <param name="serializerOptions">The options governing parameter serialization. If null, default options are used.</param>
9700.             <param name="cancellationToken">The <see cref="T:System.Threading.CancellationToken"/> to monitor for cancellation requests. The default is <see cref="P:System.Threading.CancellationToken.None"/>.</param>
9701.             <returns>A task that represents the asynchronous send operation.</returns>
9702.             <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="method"/> is <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
9703.             <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentException"><paramref name="method"/> is empty or composed entirely of whitespace.</exception>
9704.             <remarks>
9705.             <para>
9706.             This method sends a notification with parameters to the connected session. Notifications are one-way
9707.             messages that don't expect a response, commonly used for events, status updates, or signaling changes.
9708.             </para>
9709.             <para>
9710.             The parameters object is serialized to JSON according to the provided serializer options or the default
9711.             options if none are specified.
9712.             </para>
9713.             <para>
9714.             The Model Context Protocol defines several standard notification methods in <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.NotificationMethods"/>,
9715.             but custom methods can also be used for application-specific notifications.
9716.             </para>
9717.             </remarks>
9718.         </member>
9719.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.McpSession.SendNotificationAsync``1(System.String,``0,System.Text.Json.Serialization.Metadata.JsonTypeInfo{``0},System.Threading.CancellationToken)">

9722.             </summary>

9726.             <param name="cancellationToken">The <see cref="T:System.Threading.CancellationToken"/> to monitor for cancellation requests. The default is <see cref="P:System.Threading.CancellationToken.None"/>.</param>
9727.         </member>
9728.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.McpSession.NotifyProgressAsync(ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ProgressToken,ModelContextProtocol.ProgressNotificationValue,ModelContextProtocol.RequestOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)">
9729.             <summary>
9730.             Notifies the connected session of progress for a long-running operation.
9731.             </summary>
9732.             <param name="progressToken">The token that identifies the operation for which progress is being reported.</param>
9733.             <param name="progress">The progress update to send, containing information such as percentage complete or status message.</param>
9734.             <param name="options">Optional request options including metadata, serialization settings, and progress tracking.</param>
9735.             <param name="cancellationToken">The <see cref="T:System.Threading.CancellationToken"/> to monitor for cancellation requests. The default is <see cref="P:System.Threading.CancellationToken.None"/>.</param>
9736.             <returns>A task representing the completion of the notification operation (not the operation being tracked).</returns>
9737.             <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="progress"/> is <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
9738.             <remarks>
9739.             <para>
9740.             This method sends a progress notification to the connected session using the Model Context Protocol's
9741.             standardized progress notification format. Progress updates are identified by a <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ProgressToken"/>
9742.             that allows the recipient to correlate multiple updates with a specific long-running operation.
9743.             </para>
9744.             <para>
9745.             Progress notifications are sent asynchronously and don't block the operation from continuing.
9746.             </para>
9747.             </remarks>
9748.         </member>
9749.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.McpSession.NotifyProgressAsync(ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ProgressNotificationParams,System.Threading.CancellationToken)">

9755.             <returns>A task representing the completion of the notification operation (not the operation being tracked).</returns>

9757.             <remarks>

9761.             that allows the recipient to correlate multiple updates with a specific long-running operation.

9764.             Progress notifications are sent asynchronously and don't block the operation from continuing.

9826.             <summary>
9827.             Gets a task that completes when the client session has completed, providing details about the closure.

9885.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.McpTaskCancellationTokenProvider">
9886.             <summary>
9887.             Provides cancellation tokens for running MCP tasks, enabling TTL-based
9888.             automatic cancellation and explicit task cancellation.
9889.             </summary>
9890.             <remarks>
9891.             <para>
9892.             This class provides lifecycle management for <see cref="T:System.Threading.CancellationTokenSource"/> instances
9893.             associated with running tasks. Each task gets its own CTS that can be:
9894.             </para>
9895.             <list type="bullet">
9896.             <item><description>Automatically cancelled when the task's TTL expires</description></item>
9897.             <item><description>Explicitly cancelled via the <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.McpTaskCancellationTokenProvider.Cancel(System.String)"/> method</description></item>
9898.             <item><description>Cleaned up when the task completes via <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.McpTaskCancellationTokenProvider.Complete(System.String)"/></description></item>
9899.             </list>
9900.             <para>
9901.             Both <c>McpClient</c> and <c>McpServer</c> use this class to manage task cancellation
9902.             independently of request cancellation tokens.
9903.             </para>
9904.             </remarks>
9905.         </member>
9906.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.McpTaskCancellationTokenProvider.RequestToken(System.String,System.Nullable{System.TimeSpan})">
9907.             <summary>
9908.             Registers a new task and returns a cancellation token for use during execution.
9909.             </summary>
9910.             <param name="taskId">The unique identifier of the task.</param>
9911.             <param name="timeToLive">
9912.             Optional TTL duration. If specified, the returned token will be automatically
9913.             cancelled when the TTL expires.
9914.             </param>
9915.             <returns>
9916.             A <see cref="T:System.Threading.CancellationToken"/> that will be cancelled when the TTL expires,
9917.             when <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.McpTaskCancellationTokenProvider.Cancel(System.String)"/> is called, or when this provider is disposed.
9918.             </returns>
9919.             <exception cref="T:System.ObjectDisposedException">The provider has been disposed.</exception>
9920.             <exception cref="T:System.InvalidOperationException">A task with the same ID is already registered.</exception>
9921.         </member>
9922.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.McpTaskCancellationTokenProvider.Cancel(System.String)">
9923.             <summary>
9924.             Attempts to cancel a running task.
9925.             </summary>
9926.             <param name="taskId">The unique identifier of the task to cancel.</param>
9927.             <remarks>
9928.             This method signals cancellation but does not remove the task from tracking.
9929.             The task executor should call <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.McpTaskCancellationTokenProvider.Complete(System.String)"/> when it observes
9930.             the cancellation and finishes cleanup.
9931.             </remarks>
9932.         </member>
9933.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.McpTaskCancellationTokenProvider.Complete(System.String)">

9938.             <remarks>
9939.             This method should be called from a <c>finally</c> block in the task execution
9940.             to ensure proper cleanup regardless of success, failure, or cancellation.
9941.             </remarks>
9942.         </member>
9943.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.McpTaskCancellationTokenProvider.Dispose">
9944.             <summary>

9946.             </summary>
9947.         </member>
