9972.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.NotificationHandlers.RegisterRange(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{System.Collections.Generic.KeyValuePair{System.String,System.Func{ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcNotification,System.Threading.CancellationToken,System.Threading.Tasks.ValueTask}}})">
9973.             <summary>
9974.             Registers a collection of notification handlers at once.
9975.             </summary>
9976.             <param name="handlers">
9977.             A collection of notification method names paired with their corresponding handler functions.
9978.             Each key in the collection is a notification method name, and each value is a handler function
9979.             that will be invoked when a notification with that method name is received.
9980.             </param>
9981.             <remarks>
9982.             <para>
9983.             This method is typically used during client or server initialization to register
9984.             all notification handlers provided in capabilities.
9985.             </para>
9986.             <para>
9987.             Registrations completed with this method are permanent and non-removable.
9988.             This differs from handlers registered with <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.NotificationHandlers.Register(System.String,System.Func{ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcNotification,System.Threading.CancellationToken,System.Threading.Tasks.ValueTask},System.Boolean)"/> which can be temporary.
9989.             </para>
9990.             <para>
9991.             When multiple handlers are registered for the same method, all handlers will be invoked
9992.             in reverse order of registration (newest first) when a notification is received.
9993.             </para>
9994.             <para>
9995.             The registered handlers will be invoked by <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.NotificationHandlers.InvokeHandlers(System.String,ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcNotification,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/> when a notification
9996.             with the corresponding method name is received.
9997.             </para>
9998.             </remarks>
9999.         </member>
10000.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.NotificationHandlers.Register(System.String,System.Func{ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcNotification,System.Threading.CancellationToken,System.Threading.Tasks.ValueTask},System.Boolean)">
10001.             <summary>
10002.             Adds a notification handler as part of configuring the endpoint.
10003.             </summary>
10004.             <param name="method">The notification method for which the handler is being registered.</param>
10005.             <param name="handler">The handler being registered.</param>
10006.             <param name="temporary">
10007.             <see langword="true"/> if the registration can be removed later; <see langword="false"/> if it cannot.
10008.             If <see langword="false"/>, the registration will be permanent: calling <see cref="M:System.IAsyncDisposable.DisposeAsync"/>
10009.             on the returned instance will not unregister the handler.
10010.             </param>
10011.             <returns>
10012.             An <see cref="T:System.IAsyncDisposable"/> that when disposed will unregister the handler if <paramref name="temporary"/> is <see langword="true"/>.
10013.             </returns>
10014.             <remarks>
10015.             Multiple handlers can be registered for the same method. When a notification for that method is received,
10016.             all registered handlers will be invoked in reverse order of registration (newest first).
10017.             </remarks>
10018.         </member>
10019.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.NotificationHandlers.InvokeHandlers(System.String,ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcNotification,System.Threading.CancellationToken)">
10020.             <summary>
10021.             Invokes all registered handlers for the specified notification method.
10022.             </summary>
10023.             <param name="method">The notification method name to invoke handlers for.</param>
10024.             <param name="notification">The notification object to pass to each handler.</param>
10025.             <param name="cancellationToken">The <see cref="T:System.Threading.CancellationToken"/> to monitor for cancellation requests. The default is <see cref="P:System.Threading.CancellationToken.None"/>.</param>
10026.             <remarks>
10027.             Handlers are invoked in reverse order of registration (newest first).
10028.             If any handler throws an exception, all handlers will still be invoked, and an <see cref="T:System.AggregateException"/> 
10029.             containing all exceptions will be thrown after all handlers have been invoked.
10030.             </remarks>
10031.         </member>
10032.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.NotificationHandlers.Registration">
10033.             <summary>Provides storage for a handler registration.</summary>
10034.         </member>
10035.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.NotificationHandlers.Registration.#ctor(ModelContextProtocol.NotificationHandlers,System.String,System.Func{ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcNotification,System.Threading.CancellationToken,System.Threading.Tasks.ValueTask},System.Boolean)">
10036.             <summary>Provides storage for a handler registration.</summary>
10037.         </member>
10038.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.NotificationHandlers.Registration.s_invokingAncestor">
10039.             <summary>Used to prevent deadlocks during disposal.</summary>
10040.             <remarks>
10041.             The task returned from <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.NotificationHandlers.Registration.DisposeAsync"/> does not complete until all invocations of the handler
10042.             have completed and no more will be performed, so that the consumer can then trust that any resources accessed
10043.             by that handler are no longer in use and may be cleaned up. If <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.NotificationHandlers.Registration.DisposeAsync"/> were to be invoked
10044.             and its task awaited from within the invocation of the handler, however, that would result in deadlock, since
10045.             the task wouldn't complete until the invocation completed, and the invocation wouldn't complete until the task
10046.             completed. To circumvent that, we track via an <see cref="T:System.Threading.AsyncLocal`1"/> in-flight invocations. If
10047.             <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.NotificationHandlers.Registration.DisposeAsync"/> detects it's being invoked from within an invocation, it will avoid waiting. For
10048.             simplicity, we don't require that it's the same handler.
10049.             </remarks>
10050.         </member>
10051.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.NotificationHandlers.Registration._handlers">
10052.             <summary>The parent <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.NotificationHandlers"/> to which this registration belongs.</summary>
10053.         </member>
10054.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.NotificationHandlers.Registration._method">
10055.             <summary>The method with which this registration is associated.</summary>
10056.         </member>
10057.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.NotificationHandlers.Registration._handler">
10058.             <summary>The handler this registration represents.</summary>
10059.         </member>
10060.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.NotificationHandlers.Registration._temporary">
10061.             <summary>true if this instance is temporary; false if it's permanent</summary>
10062.         </member>
10063.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.NotificationHandlers.Registration._disposeTcs">
10064.             <summary>Provides a task that <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.NotificationHandlers.Registration.DisposeAsync"/> can await to know when all in-flight invocations have completed.</summary>
10065.             <remarks>
10066.             This will only be initialized if <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.NotificationHandlers.Registration.DisposeAsync"/> sees in-flight invocations, in which case it'll initialize
10067.             this <see cref="T:System.Threading.Tasks.TaskCompletionSource`1"/> and then await its task. The task will be completed when the last
10068.             in-flight notification completes.
10069.             </remarks>
10070.         </member>
10071.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.NotificationHandlers.Registration._refCount">
10072.             <summary>The number of remaining references to this registration.</summary>
10073.             <remarks>
10074.             The ref count starts life at 1 to represent the whole registration; that ref count will be subtracted when
10075.             the instance is disposed. Every invocation then temporarily increases the ref count before invocation and
10076.             decrements it after. When <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.NotificationHandlers.Registration.DisposeAsync"/> is called, it decrements the ref count. In the common
10077.             case, that'll bring the count down to 0, in which case the instance will never be subsequently invoked.
10078.             If, however, after that decrement the count is still positive, then there are in-flight invocations; the last
10079.             one of those to complete will end up decrementing the ref count to 0.
10080.             </remarks>
10081.         </member>
10082.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.NotificationHandlers.Registration._disposedCalled">
10083.             <summary>Tracks whether <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.NotificationHandlers.Registration.DisposeAsync"/> has ever been invoked.</summary>
10084.             <remarks>
10085.             It's rare but possible <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.NotificationHandlers.Registration.DisposeAsync"/> is called multiple times. Only the first
10086.             should decrement the initial ref count, but they all must wait until all invocations have quiesced.
10087.             </remarks>
10088.         </member>
10089.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.NotificationHandlers.Registration.Next">
10090.             <summary>The next registration in the linked list.</summary>
10091.         </member>
10092.         <member name="F:ModelContextProtocol.NotificationHandlers.Registration.Prev">
10093.             <summary>
10094.             The previous registration in the linked list of handlers for a specific notification method.
10095.             Used to maintain the bidirectional linked list when handlers are added or removed.
10096.             </summary>
10097.         </member>
10098.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.NotificationHandlers.Registration.DisposeAsync">
10099.             <summary>Removes the registration.</summary>
10100.         </member>
10101.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.NotificationHandlers.Registration.InvokeAsync(ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcNotification,System.Threading.CancellationToken)">
10102.             <summary>Invoke the handler associated with the registration.</summary>
10103.         </member>

10105.             <summary>Invoke the handler associated with the temporary registration.</summary>

10112.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.ProcessHelper.KillTree(System.Diagnostics.Process,System.TimeSpan)">
10113.             <summary>

10115.             </summary>

10152.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.RequestHandlers.Set``2(System.String,System.Func{``0,ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcRequest,System.Threading.CancellationToken,System.Threading.Tasks.ValueTask{``1}},System.Text.Json.Serialization.Metadata.JsonTypeInfo{``0},System.Text.Json.Serialization.Metadata.JsonTypeInfo{``1})">
10153.             <summary>
10154.             Registers a handler for incoming requests of a specific method in the MCP protocol.
10155.             </summary>
10156.             <typeparam name="TParams">Type of request payload that will be deserialized from incoming JSON</typeparam>
10157.             <typeparam name="TResult">Type of response payload that will be serialized to JSON (not full RPC response)</typeparam>

10160.             <param name="requestTypeInfo">The JSON contract governing request parameter deserialization</param>
10161.             <param name="responseTypeInfo">The JSON contract governing response serialization</param>
10162.             <remarks>
10163.             <para>
10164.             This method is used internally by the MCP infrastructure to register handlers for various protocol methods.
10165.             When an incoming request matches the specified method, the registered handler will be invoked with the
10166.             deserialized request parameters.

10195.             does not affect <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.RequestOptions.ProgressToken"/>, and the object returned from <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.RequestOptions.Meta"/>

10207.             property and the <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.RequestOptions.Meta"/> property do not interact (setting <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.RequestOptions.ProgressToken"/>
10208.             does not affect <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.RequestOptions.Meta"/>, and getting <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.RequestOptions.ProgressToken"/> does not read from
10209.             <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.RequestOptions.Meta"/>). To get the actual <see cref="T:System.Text.Json.Nodes.JsonObject"/> that contains state from both
10210.             <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.RequestOptions.Meta"/> and <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.RequestOptions.ProgressToken"/>, use the <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.RequestOptions.GetMetaForRequest"/> method.

10221.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.RequestOptions.GetMetaForRequest">
10222.             <summary>
10223.             Gets a <see cref="T:System.Text.Json.Nodes.JsonObject"/> to use in requests for the "_meta" property.

10226.             A <see cref="T:System.Text.Json.Nodes.JsonObject"/> suitable for use in requests for the "_meta" property.

10228.             <remarks>
10229.             Progress tokens are part of MCP's _meta property. As such, if <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.RequestOptions.ProgressToken"/>
10230.             is non-<see langword="null"/> but <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.RequestOptions.Meta"/> is <see langword="null"/>, <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.RequestOptions.GetMetaForRequest"/> will 
10231.             manufacture and return a new <see cref="T:System.Text.Json.Nodes.JsonObject"/> instance containing the token. If both <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.RequestOptions.ProgressToken"/>
10232.             and <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.RequestOptions.Meta"/> are non-<see langword="null"/>, a new clone of <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.RequestOptions.Meta"/> will be created and its
10233.             "progressToken" property overwritten with <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.RequestOptions.ProgressToken"/>. Otherwise, <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.RequestOptions.GetMetaForRequest"/>
10234.             will just return <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.RequestOptions.Meta"/>.
10235.             </remarks>
10236.         </member>
10237.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.SynchronizationExtensions.LockAsync(System.Threading.SemaphoreSlim,System.Threading.CancellationToken)">
10238.             <summary>
10239.             Asynchronously acquires a lock on the semaphore and returns a disposable object that releases the lock when disposed.
10240.             </summary>
10241.             <param name="semaphore">The semaphore to acquire a lock on.</param>
10242.             <param name="cancellationToken">A cancellation token to observe while waiting for the semaphore.</param>
10243.             <returns>A disposable <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.SynchronizationExtensions.Releaser"/> that releases the semaphore when disposed.</returns>
10244.             <remarks>
10245.             This extension method provides a convenient pattern for using a semaphore in asynchronous code,
10246.             similar to how the `lock` statement is used in synchronous code.
10247.             </remarks>
10248.             <exception cref="T:System.OperationCanceledException">The <paramref name="cancellationToken"/> was canceled.</exception>
10249.         </member>
10250.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.SynchronizationExtensions.Releaser">
10251.             <summary>
10252.             A disposable struct that releases a semaphore when disposed.
10253.             </summary>
10254.             <remarks>
10255.             This struct is used with the <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.SynchronizationExtensions.LockAsync(System.Threading.SemaphoreSlim,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/> extension method to provide
10256.             a using-pattern for semaphore locking, similar to lock statements.
10257.             </remarks>

10259.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.SynchronizationExtensions.Releaser.#ctor(System.Threading.SemaphoreSlim)">

10265.             a using-pattern for semaphore locking, similar to lock statements.
10266.             </remarks>
10267.         </member>

10615.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpMessageFilter">
10616.             <summary>
10617.             Delegate type for applying filters to JSON-RPC messages.
10618.             </summary>
10619.             <param name="next">The next message handler in the pipeline.</param>
10620.             <returns>The next message handler wrapped with the filter.</returns>
10621.             <remarks>
10622.             <para>
10623.             Message filters allow you to intercept and process JSON-RPC messages before they reach
10624.             their respective handlers (incoming) or before they are sent (outgoing). This is useful for implementing
10625.             cross-cutting concerns that need to apply to all message types, such as logging, authentication, rate limiting,
10626.             redaction, or request tracing.
10627.             </para>
10628.             <para>
10629.             Filters are applied in the order they are registered, with the first registered filter being the outermost.
10630.             Each filter receives the next handler in the pipeline and can choose to:
10631.             <list type="bullet">
10632.             <item><description>Call the next handler to continue processing (await next(context, cancellationToken))</description></item>
10633.             <item><description>Skip the default handlers entirely by not calling next</description></item>
10634.             <item><description>Perform operations before and/or after calling next</description></item>
10635.             <item><description>Catch and handle exceptions from inner handlers</description></item>
10636.             </list>
10637.             </para>
10638.             <para>
10639.             For request-specific filters, use <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpRequestFilter`2"/> instead.
10640.             </para>
10641.             </remarks>
10642.         </member>
10643.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpMessageFilters">
10644.             <summary>
10645.             Provides grouped message filter collections.
10646.             </summary>
10647.         </member>
10648.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpMessageFilters.IncomingFilters">
10649.             <summary>
10650.             Gets or sets the filters for all incoming JSON-RPC messages.
10651.             </summary>
10652.             <remarks>
10653.             <para>
10654.             These filters intercept all incoming JSON-RPC messages before they are processed by the server,
10655.             including requests, notifications, responses, and errors. The filters can perform logging,
10656.             authentication, rate limiting, or other cross-cutting concerns that apply to all message types.
10657.             </para>
10658.             <para>
10659.             Message filters are applied before request-specific filters. If a message filter does not call
10660.             the next handler in the pipeline, the default handlers will not be executed.
10661.             </para>
10662.             </remarks>
10663.         </member>
10664.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpMessageFilters.OutgoingFilters">
10665.             <summary>
10666.             Gets or sets the filters for all outgoing JSON-RPC messages.
10667.             </summary>
10668.             <remarks>
10669.             <para>
10670.             These filters intercept all outgoing JSON-RPC messages before they are sent to the client,
10671.             including responses, notifications, and errors. The filters can perform logging,
10672.             redaction, auditing, or other cross-cutting concerns that apply to all message types.
10673.             </para>
10674.             <para>
10675.             If a message filter does not call the next handler in the pipeline, the message will not be sent.
10676.             Filters may also call the next handler multiple times with different messages to emit additional
10677.             server-to-client messages.
10678.             </para>
10679.             </remarks>
10680.         </member>
10681.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpMessageHandler">
10682.             <summary>
10683.             Delegate type for handling incoming JSON-RPC messages.
10684.             </summary>
10685.             <param name="context">The message context containing the JSON-RPC message and other metadata.</param>
10686.             <param name="cancellationToken">A cancellation token to cancel the operation.</param>
10687.             <returns>A task representing the asynchronous operation.</returns>
10688.             <remarks>
10689.             <para>
10690.             This delegate can handle any type of JSON-RPC message, including requests, notifications, responses, and errors.
10691.             Use this for implementing cross-cutting concerns that need to intercept all message types,
10692.             such as logging, authentication, rate limiting, or request tracing.
10693.             </para>
10694.             <para>
10695.             For request-specific handling, use <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpRequestHandler`2"/> instead.
10696.             </para>
10697.             </remarks>
10698.         </member>
10699.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpMetaAttribute">
10700.             <summary>
10701.             Specifies metadata for an MCP server primitive (tool, prompt, or resource).
10702.             </summary>
10703.             <remarks>
10704.             <para>
10705.             The metadata is used to populate the <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Tool.Meta"/>, <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Prompt.Meta"/>,
10706.             or <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Resource.Meta"/> property of the corresponding primitive. This metadata is
10707.             included in the responses to listing operations (<c>tools/list</c>, <c>prompts/list</c>,
10708.             <c>resources/list</c>).
10709.             </para>
10710.             <para>
10711.             This metadata is <b>not</b> propagated to the results of invocation operations such as
10712.             <c>tools/call</c>, <c>prompts/get</c>, or <c>resources/read</c>. To include metadata in
10713.             those results, set the <c>Meta</c> property on the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolResult"/>,
10714.             <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.GetPromptResult"/>, or <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ReadResourceResult"/> directly in your method implementation.
10715.             </para>
10716.             <para>
10717.             This attribute can be applied multiple times to a method to specify multiple key/value pairs
10718.             of metadata. However, the same key should not be used more than once; doing so will result
10719.             in undefined behavior.
10720.             </para>
10721.             <para>
10722.             Metadata can be used to attach additional information to primitives, such as model preferences,
10723.             version information, or other custom data that should be communicated to MCP clients.
10724.             </para>
10725.             </remarks>
10726.             <example>
10727.             <code language="csharp">
10728.             [McpServerTool]
10729.             [McpMeta("model", "gpt-4o")]
10730.             [McpMeta("version", "1.0")]
10731.             [McpMeta("priority", 5.0)]
10732.             [McpMeta("isBeta", true)]
10733.             [McpMeta("tags", JsonValue = """["a","b"]""")]
10734.             public string MyTool(string input) => $"Processed: {input}";
10735.             </code>
10736.             </example>
10737.         </member>
10738.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpMetaAttribute.#ctor(System.String,System.String)">
10739.             <summary>
10740.             Initializes a new instance of the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpMetaAttribute"/> class with a string value.
10741.             </summary>
10742.             <param name="name">The name (key) of the metadata entry.</param>
10743.             <param name="value">The string value of the metadata entry. If null, the value will be serialized as JSON null.</param>
10744.         </member>
10745.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpMetaAttribute.#ctor(System.String,System.Double)">
10746.             <summary>
10747.             Initializes a new instance of the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpMetaAttribute"/> class with a double value.
10748.             </summary>
10749.             <param name="name">The name (key) of the metadata entry.</param>
10750.             <param name="value">The double value of the metadata entry.</param>
10751.         </member>
10752.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpMetaAttribute.#ctor(System.String,System.Boolean)">
10753.             <summary>
10754.             Initializes a new instance of the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpMetaAttribute"/> class with a Boolean value.
10755.             </summary>
10756.             <param name="name">The name (key) of the metadata entry.</param>
10757.             <param name="value">The Boolean value of the metadata entry.</param>
10758.         </member>
10759.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpMetaAttribute.Name">
10760.             <summary>
10761.             Gets the name (key) of the metadata entry.
10762.             </summary>
10763.             <remarks>
10764.             This value is used as the key in the metadata object. It should be a unique identifier
10765.             for this piece of metadata within the context of the primitive.
10766.             </remarks>
10767.         </member>
10768.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpMetaAttribute.JsonValue">
10769.             <summary>
10770.             Gets or sets the value of the metadata entry as a JSON string.
10771.             </summary>
10772.             <remarks>
10773.             <para>
10774.             This value must be well-formed JSON. It will be parsed and added to the metadata <see cref="T:System.Text.Json.Nodes.JsonObject"/>.
10775.             Simple values can be represented as JSON literals like <c>"\"my-string\""</c>, <c>"123"</c>,
10776.             or <c>"true"</c>. Complex structures can be represented as JSON objects or arrays.
10777.             </para>
10778.             <para>
10779.             Setting this property overrides any value provided via the constructor.
10780.             </para>
10781.             <para>
10782.             For programmatic scenarios where you want to construct complex metadata without dealing with
10783.             JSON strings, use the <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerToolCreateOptions.Meta"/>,
10784.             <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPromptCreateOptions.Meta"/>, or <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerResourceCreateOptions.Meta"/>
10785.             property to provide a JsonObject directly.
10786.             </para>
10787.             </remarks>
10788.         </member>

10803.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpRequestFilters.ListToolsFilters">
10804.             <summary>
10805.             Gets or sets the filters for the list-tools handler pipeline.
10806.             </summary>
10807.             <remarks>
10808.             <para>
10809.             These filters wrap handlers that return a list of available tools when requested by a client.
10810.             The filters can modify, log, or perform additional operations on requests and responses for
10811.             <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.ToolsList"/> requests. It supports pagination through the cursor mechanism,
10812.             where the client can make repeated calls with the cursor returned by the previous call to retrieve more tools.
10813.             </para>
10814.             <para>
10815.             These filters work alongside any tools defined in the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerTool"/> collection.
10816.             Tools from both sources will be combined when returning results to clients.
10817.             </para>
10818.             </remarks>
10819.         </member>
10820.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpRequestFilters.CallToolFilters">
10821.             <summary>
10822.             Gets or sets the filters for the call-tool handler pipeline.
10823.             </summary>
10824.             <remarks>
10825.             These filters wrap handlers that are invoked when a client makes a call to a tool that isn't found in the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerTool"/> collection.
10826.             The filters can modify, log, or perform additional operations on requests and responses for
10827.             <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.ToolsCall"/> requests. The handler should implement logic to execute the requested tool and return appropriate results.
10828.             </remarks>
10829.         </member>
10830.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpRequestFilters.ListPromptsFilters">
10831.             <summary>
10832.             Gets or sets the filters for the list-prompts handler pipeline.
10833.             </summary>
10834.             <remarks>
10835.             <para>
10836.             These filters wrap handlers that return a list of available prompts when requested by a client.
10837.             The filters can modify, log, or perform additional operations on requests and responses for
10838.             <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.PromptsList"/> requests. It supports pagination through the cursor mechanism,
10839.             where the client can make repeated calls with the cursor returned by the previous call to retrieve more prompts.
10840.             </para>
10841.             <para>
10842.             These filters work alongside any prompts defined in the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPrompt"/> collection.
10843.             Prompts from both sources will be combined when returning results to clients.
10844.             </para>
10845.             </remarks>
10846.         </member>
10847.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpRequestFilters.GetPromptFilters">
10848.             <summary>
10849.             Gets or sets the filters for the get-prompt handler pipeline.
10850.             </summary>
10851.             <remarks>
10852.             These filters wrap handlers that are invoked when a client requests details for a specific prompt that isn't found in the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPrompt"/> collection.
10853.             The filters can modify, log, or perform additional operations on requests and responses for
10854.             <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.PromptsGet"/> requests. The handler should implement logic to fetch or generate the requested prompt and return appropriate results.
10855.             </remarks>
10856.         </member>
10857.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpRequestFilters.ListResourceTemplatesFilters">
10858.             <summary>
10859.             Gets or sets the filters for the list-resource-templates handler pipeline.
10860.             </summary>
10861.             <remarks>
10862.             These filters wrap handlers that return a list of available resource templates when requested by a client.
10863.             The filters can modify, log, or perform additional operations on requests and responses for
10864.             <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.ResourcesTemplatesList"/> requests. It supports pagination through the cursor mechanism,
10865.             where the client can make repeated calls with the cursor returned by the previous call to retrieve more resource templates.
10866.             </remarks>
10867.         </member>
10868.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpRequestFilters.ListResourcesFilters">
10869.             <summary>
10870.             Gets or sets the filters for the list-resources handler pipeline.
10871.             </summary>
10872.             <remarks>
10873.             These filters wrap handlers that return a list of available resources when requested by a client.
10874.             The filters can modify, log, or perform additional operations on requests and responses for
10875.             <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.ResourcesList"/> requests. It supports pagination through the cursor mechanism,
10876.             where the client can make repeated calls with the cursor returned by the previous call to retrieve more resources.
10877.             </remarks>
10878.         </member>
10879.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpRequestFilters.ReadResourceFilters">
10880.             <summary>
10881.             Gets or sets the filters for the read-resource handler pipeline.
10882.             </summary>
10883.             <remarks>
10884.             These filters wrap handlers that are invoked when a client requests the content of a specific resource identified by its URI.
10885.             The filters can modify, log, or perform additional operations on requests and responses for
10886.             <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.ResourcesRead"/> requests. The handler should implement logic to locate and retrieve the requested resource.
10887.             </remarks>
10888.         </member>
10889.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpRequestFilters.CompleteFilters">
10890.             <summary>
10891.             Gets or sets the filters for the complete-handler pipeline.
10892.             </summary>
10893.             <remarks>
10894.             These filters wrap handlers that provide auto-completion suggestions for prompt arguments or resource references in the Model Context Protocol.
10895.             The filters can modify, log, or perform additional operations on requests and responses for
10896.             <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.CompletionComplete"/> requests. The handler processes auto-completion requests, returning a list of suggestions based on the
10897.             reference type and current argument value.
10898.             </remarks>
10899.         </member>
10900.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpRequestFilters.SubscribeToResourcesFilters">
10901.             <summary>
10902.             Gets or sets the filters for the subscribe-to-resources handler pipeline.
10903.             </summary>
10904.             <remarks>
10905.             <para>
10906.             These filters wrap handlers that are invoked when a client wants to receive notifications about changes to specific resources or resource patterns.
10907.             The filters can modify, log, or perform additional operations on requests and responses for
10908.             <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.ResourcesSubscribe"/> requests. The handler should implement logic to register the client's interest in the specified resources
10909.             and set up the necessary infrastructure to send notifications when those resources change.
10910.             </para>
10911.             <para>
10912.             After a successful subscription, the server should send resource change notifications to the client
10913.             whenever a relevant resource is created, updated, or deleted.
10914.             </para>
10915.             </remarks>
10916.         </member>
10917.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpRequestFilters.UnsubscribeFromResourcesFilters">
10918.             <summary>
10919.             Gets or sets the filters for the unsubscribe-from-resources handler pipeline.
10920.             </summary>
10921.             <remarks>
10922.             <para>
10923.             These filters wrap handlers that are invoked when a client wants to stop receiving notifications about previously subscribed resources.
10924.             The filters can modify, log, or perform additional operations on requests and responses for
10925.             <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.ResourcesUnsubscribe"/> requests. The handler should implement logic to remove the client's subscriptions to the specified resources
10926.             and clean up any associated resources.
10927.             </para>
10928.             <para>
10929.             After a successful unsubscription, the server should no longer send resource change notifications
10930.             to the client for the specified resources.
10931.             </para>
10932.             </remarks>
10933.         </member>
10934.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpRequestFilters.SetLoggingLevelFilters">
10935.             <summary>
10936.             Gets or sets the filters for the set-logging-level handler pipeline.
10937.             </summary>
10938.             <remarks>
10939.             <para>
10940.             These filters wrap handlers that process <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.LoggingSetLevel"/> requests from clients. When set, it enables
10941.             clients to control which log messages they receive by specifying a minimum severity threshold.
10942.             The filters can modify, log, or perform additional operations on requests and responses for
10943.             <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.LoggingSetLevel"/> requests.
10944.             </para>
10945.             <para>
10946.             After handling a level change request, the server typically begins sending log messages
10947.             at or above the specified level to the client as notifications/message notifications.
10948.             </para>
10949.             </remarks>
10950.         </member>
10951.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpRequestHandler`2">
10952.             <summary>
10953.             Delegate type for handling incoming MCP requests with specific parameter and result types.

10955.             <typeparam name="TParams">The type of the parameters sent with the request.</typeparam>

11032.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServer.Create(ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ITransport,ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerOptions,Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILoggerFactory,System.IServiceProvider)">
11033.             <summary>

11036.             <param name="transport">The transport to use for the server representing an already-established MCP session.</param>

11155.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServer.GetTaskResultAsync``1(System.String,System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)">
11156.             <summary>
11157.             Retrieves the result of a completed task from the client, blocking until the task reaches a terminal state.
11158.             </summary>
11159.             <typeparam name="TResult">The type to deserialize the task result into.</typeparam>
11160.             <param name="taskId">The unique identifier of the task whose result to retrieve.</param>
11161.             <param name="jsonSerializerOptions">Optional serializer options for deserializing the result.</param>
11162.             <param name="cancellationToken">The <see cref="T:System.Threading.CancellationToken"/> to monitor for cancellation requests. The default is <see cref="P:System.Threading.CancellationToken.None"/>.</param>
11163.             <returns>The result of the task, deserialized into type <typeparamref name="TResult"/>.</returns>
11164.             <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="taskId"/> is <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
11165.             <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentException"><paramref name="taskId"/> is empty or composed entirely of whitespace.</exception>
11166.             <exception cref="T:System.InvalidOperationException">The client does not support tasks.</exception>
11167.             <exception cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.McpException">The request failed or the client returned an error response.</exception>
11168.             <remarks>
11169.             <para>
11170.             This method sends a tasks/result request to the client, which will block until the task completes if it hasn't already.
11171.             The client handles all polling logic internally.
11172.             </para>
11173.             <para>
11174.             For sampling tasks, use <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CreateMessageResult"/> as <typeparamref name="TResult"/>.
11175.             For elicitation tasks, use <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ElicitResult"/> as <typeparamref name="TResult"/>.
11176.             </para>
11177.             </remarks>
11178.         </member>

11195.             <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="requestParams"/> is <see langword="null"/>.</exception>

11198.             <remarks>
11199.             The <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServer.ListTasksAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/> overload retrieves all tasks by automatically handling pagination.
11200.             This overload works with the lower-level <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ListTasksRequestParams"/> and <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ListTasksResult"/>, returning the raw result from the client.
11201.             Any pagination needs to be managed by the caller.

11204.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServer.CancelTaskAsync(System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)">
11205.             <summary>
11206.             Cancels a running task on the client.
11207.             </summary>
11208.             <param name="taskId">The unique identifier of the task to cancel.</param>
11209.             <param name="cancellationToken">The <see cref="T:System.Threading.CancellationToken"/> to monitor for cancellation requests. The default is <see cref="P:System.Threading.CancellationToken.None"/>.</param>
11210.             <returns>The updated state of the task after cancellation.</returns>
11211.             <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="taskId"/> is <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
11212.             <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentException"><paramref name="taskId"/> is empty or composed entirely of whitespace.</exception>
11213.             <exception cref="T:System.InvalidOperationException">The client does not support tasks or task cancellation.</exception>
11214.             <exception cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.McpException">The request failed or the client returned an error response.</exception>
11215.             <remarks>
11216.             Cancelling a task requests that the client stop execution. The client may not immediately cancel the task,
11217.             and may choose to allow the task to complete if it's close to finishing.
11218.             </remarks>
11219.         </member>
11220.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServer.PollTaskUntilCompleteAsync(System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)">
11221.             <summary>
11222.             Polls a task on the client until it reaches a terminal state.
11223.             </summary>
11224.             <param name="taskId">The unique identifier of the task to poll.</param>
11225.             <param name="cancellationToken">The <see cref="T:System.Threading.CancellationToken"/> to monitor for cancellation requests. The default is <see cref="P:System.Threading.CancellationToken.None"/>.</param>
11226.             <returns>The task in its terminal state.</returns>
11227.             <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="taskId"/> is <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
11228.             <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentException"><paramref name="taskId"/> is empty or composed entirely of whitespace.</exception>
11229.             <exception cref="T:System.InvalidOperationException">The client does not support tasks.</exception>
11230.             <exception cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.McpException">The request failed or the client returned an error response.</exception>
11231.             <remarks>
11232.             <para>
11233.             This method repeatedly calls <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServer.GetTaskAsync(System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/> until the task reaches a terminal status.
11234.             It respects the <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTask.PollInterval"/> returned by the client to determine how long
11235.             to wait between polling attempts.
11236.             </para>
11237.             <para>
11238.             For retrieving the actual result of a completed task, use <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServer.GetTaskResultAsync``1(System.String,System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>
11239.             or <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServer.WaitForTaskResultAsync``1(System.String,System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>.
11240.             </para>
11241.             </remarks>
11242.         </member>
11243.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServer.WaitForTaskResultAsync``1(System.String,System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)">
11244.             <summary>
11245.             Waits for a task on the client to complete and retrieves its result.
11246.             </summary>
11247.             <typeparam name="TResult">The type to deserialize the task result into.</typeparam>
11248.             <param name="taskId">The unique identifier of the task whose result to retrieve.</param>
11249.             <param name="jsonSerializerOptions">Optional serializer options for deserializing the result.</param>
11250.             <param name="cancellationToken">The <see cref="T:System.Threading.CancellationToken"/> to monitor for cancellation requests. The default is <see cref="P:System.Threading.CancellationToken.None"/>.</param>
11251.             <returns>A tuple containing the final task state and its result.</returns>
11252.             <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="taskId"/> is <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
11253.             <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentException"><paramref name="taskId"/> is empty or composed entirely of whitespace.</exception>
11254.             <exception cref="T:System.InvalidOperationException">The client does not support tasks.</exception>
11255.             <exception cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.McpException">The task failed or was cancelled.</exception>
11256.             <remarks>
11257.             <para>
11258.             This method combines <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServer.PollTaskUntilCompleteAsync(System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/> and <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServer.GetTaskResultAsync``1(System.String,System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>
11259.             to provide a convenient way to wait for a task to complete and retrieve its result in a single call.
11260.             </para>
11261.             <para>
11262.             If the task completes with a status of <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatus.Failed"/> or <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatus.Cancelled"/>,
11263.             an <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.McpException"/> is thrown.
11264.             </para>
11265.             <para>
11266.             For sampling tasks, use <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CreateMessageResult"/> as <typeparamref name="TResult"/>.
11267.             For elicitation tasks, use <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ElicitResult"/> as <typeparamref name="TResult"/>.
11268.             </para>
11269.             </remarks>
11270.         </member>
11271.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServer.ElicitAsync``1(System.String,ModelContextProtocol.RequestOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)">
11272.             <summary>

11274.             public serializable properties of <typeparamref name="T"/> and deserializing the response into <typeparamref name="T"/>.

11277.             <param name="message">The message to present to the user.</param>

11348.             <param name="cancellationToken">The <see cref="T:System.Threading.CancellationToken"/> to monitor for cancellation requests.</param>

11350.             <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="task"/> is <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
11351.             <exception cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.McpException">The notification failed or the client returned an error response.</exception>
11352.             <remarks>
11353.             <para>
11354.             This method sends an optional status notification to inform the client of task state changes.
11355.             According to the MCP specification, receivers MAY send this notification but are not required to.
11356.             Clients must not rely on receiving these notifications and should continue polling via tasks/get.
11357.             </para>
11358.             <para>
11359.             The notification is sent using the standard <c>notifications/tasks/status</c> method and includes
11360.             the full task state information.
11361.             </para>
11362.             </remarks>
11363.         </member>

11398.             </remarks>
11399.         </member>

11410.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerHandlers">
11411.             <summary>
11412.             Provides a container for handlers used in the creation of an MCP server.
11413.             </summary>
11414.             <remarks>
11415.             <para>
11416.             This class provides a centralized collection of delegates that implement various capabilities of the Model Context Protocol.
11417.             Each handler in this class corresponds to a specific endpoint in the Model Context Protocol and
11418.             is responsible for processing a particular type of message. The handlers are used to customize
11419.             the behavior of the MCP server by providing implementations for the various protocol operations.
11420.             </para>
11421.             <para>
11422.             When a client sends a message to the server, the appropriate handler is invoked to process it
11423.             according to the protocol specification. Which handler is selected
11424.             is done based on an ordinal, case-sensitive string comparison.
11425.             </para>
11426.             </remarks>
11427.         </member>
11428.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerHandlers.ListToolsHandler">
11429.             <summary>
11430.             Gets or sets the handler for <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.ToolsList"/> requests.
11431.             </summary>
11432.             <remarks>
11433.             <para>
11434.             The handler should return a list of available tools when requested by a client.
11435.             It supports pagination through the cursor mechanism, where the client can make
11436.             repeated calls with the cursor returned by the previous call to retrieve more tools.
11437.             </para>
11438.             <para>
11439.             This handler works alongside any tools defined in the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerTool"/> collection.
11440.             Tools from both sources will be combined when returning results to clients.
11441.             </para>
11442.             </remarks>
11443.         </member>
11444.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerHandlers.CallToolHandler">
11445.             <summary>
11446.             Gets or sets the handler for <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.ToolsCall"/> requests.
11447.             </summary>
11448.             <remarks>
11449.             This handler is invoked when a client makes a call to a tool that isn't found in the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerTool"/> collection.
11450.             The handler should implement logic to execute the requested tool and return appropriate results.
11451.             </remarks>
11452.         </member>
11453.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerHandlers.ListPromptsHandler">
11454.             <summary>
11455.             Gets or sets the handler for <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.PromptsList"/> requests.
11456.             </summary>
11457.             <remarks>
11458.             <para>
11459.             The handler should return a list of available prompts when requested by a client.
11460.             It supports pagination through the cursor mechanism, where the client can make
11461.             repeated calls with the cursor returned by the previous call to retrieve more prompts.
11462.             </para>
11463.             <para>
11464.             This handler works alongside any prompts defined in the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPrompt"/> collection.
11465.             Prompts from both sources will be combined when returning results to clients.
11466.             </para>
11467.             </remarks>
11468.         </member>
11469.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerHandlers.GetPromptHandler">
11470.             <summary>
11471.             Gets or sets the handler for <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.PromptsGet"/> requests.
11472.             </summary>
11473.             <remarks>
11474.             This handler is invoked when a client requests details for a specific prompt that isn't found in the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPrompt"/> collection.
11475.             The handler should implement logic to fetch or generate the requested prompt and return appropriate results.
11476.             </remarks>
11477.         </member>
11478.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerHandlers.ListResourceTemplatesHandler">
11479.             <summary>
11480.             Gets or sets the handler for <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.ResourcesTemplatesList"/> requests.
11481.             </summary>
11482.             <remarks>
11483.             The handler should return a list of available resource templates when requested by a client.
11484.             It supports pagination through the cursor mechanism, where the client can make
11485.             repeated calls with the cursor returned by the previous call to retrieve more resource templates.
11486.             </remarks>
11487.         </member>
11488.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerHandlers.ListResourcesHandler">
11489.             <summary>
11490.             Gets or sets the handler for <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.ResourcesList"/> requests.
11491.             </summary>
11492.             <remarks>
11493.             The handler should return a list of available resources when requested by a client.
11494.             It supports pagination through the cursor mechanism, where the client can make
11495.             repeated calls with the cursor returned by the previous call to retrieve more resources.
11496.             </remarks>
11497.         </member>
11498.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerHandlers.ReadResourceHandler">
11499.             <summary>
11500.             Gets or sets the handler for <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.ResourcesRead"/> requests.
11501.             </summary>
11502.             <remarks>
11503.             This handler is invoked when a client requests the content of a specific resource identified by its URI.
11504.             The handler should implement logic to locate and retrieve the requested resource.
11505.             </remarks>
11506.         </member>
11507.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerHandlers.CompleteHandler">
11508.             <summary>
11509.             Gets or sets the handler for <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.CompletionComplete"/> requests.
11510.             </summary>
11511.             <remarks>
11512.             This handler provides auto-completion suggestions for prompt arguments or resource references in the Model Context Protocol.
11513.             The handler processes auto-completion requests, returning a list of suggestions based on the
11514.             reference type and current argument value.
11515.             </remarks>
11516.         </member>
11517.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerHandlers.SubscribeToResourcesHandler">
11518.             <summary>
11519.             Gets or sets the handler for <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.ResourcesSubscribe"/> requests.
11520.             </summary>
11521.             <remarks>
11522.             <para>
11523.             This handler is invoked when a client wants to receive notifications about changes to specific resources or resource patterns.
11524.             The handler should implement logic to register the client's interest in the specified resources
11525.             and set up the necessary infrastructure to send notifications when those resources change.
11526.             </para>
11527.             <para>
11528.             After a successful subscription, the server should send resource change notifications to the client
11529.             whenever a relevant resource is created, updated, or deleted.
11530.             </para>
11531.             </remarks>
11532.         </member>
11533.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerHandlers.UnsubscribeFromResourcesHandler">
11534.             <summary>
11535.             Gets or sets the handler for <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.ResourcesUnsubscribe"/> requests.
11536.             </summary>
11537.             <remarks>
11538.             <para>
11539.             This handler is invoked when a client wants to stop receiving notifications about previously subscribed resources.
11540.             The handler should implement logic to remove the client's subscriptions to the specified resources
11541.             and clean up any associated resources.
11542.             </para>
11543.             <para>
11544.             After a successful unsubscription, the server should no longer send resource change notifications
11545.             to the client for the specified resources.
11546.             </para>
11547.             </remarks>
11548.         </member>
11549.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerHandlers.SetLoggingLevelHandler">
11550.             <summary>
11551.             Gets or sets the handler for <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.LoggingSetLevel"/> requests.
11552.             </summary>
11553.             <remarks>
11554.             <para>
11555.             This handler processes <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.LoggingSetLevel"/> requests from clients. When set, it enables
11556.             clients to control which log messages they receive by specifying a minimum severity threshold.
11557.             </para>
11558.             <para>
11559.             After handling a level change request, the server typically begins sending log messages
11560.             at or above the specified level to the client as notifications/message notifications.
11561.             </para>
11562.             </remarks>
11563.         </member>
11564.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerHandlers.NotificationHandlers">
11565.             <summary>Gets or sets notification handlers to register with the server.</summary>
11566.             <remarks>
11567.             <para>
11568.             When constructed, the server will enumerate these handlers, which may contain multiple handlers per notification method key, once.
11569.             The server will not re-enumerate the sequence after initialization.
11570.             </para>
11571.             <para>
11572.             Notification handlers allow the server to respond to client-sent notifications for specific methods.
11573.             Each key in the collection is a notification method name, and each value is a callback that will be invoked
11574.             when a notification with that method is received.
11575.             </para>
11576.             <para>
11577.             Handlers provided via <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerHandlers.NotificationHandlers"/> will be registered with the server for the lifetime of the server.
11578.             For transient handlers, <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.McpSession.RegisterNotificationHandler(System.String,System.Func{ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcNotification,System.Threading.CancellationToken,System.Threading.Tasks.ValueTask})"/> may be used to register a handler that can
11579.             then be unregistered by disposing of the <see cref="T:System.IAsyncDisposable"/> returned from the method.
11580.             </para>
11581.             </remarks>
11582.         </member>
