11657.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerOptions">
11658.             <summary>
11659.             Provides configuration options for the MCP server.
11660.             </summary>
11661.         </member>
11662.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerOptions.ServerInfo">
11663.             <summary>
11664.             Gets or sets information about this server implementation, including its name and version.
11665.             </summary>
11666.             <remarks>
11667.             This information is sent to the client during initialization to identify the server.
11668.             It's displayed in client logs and can be used for debugging and compatibility checks.
11669.             </remarks>
11670.         </member>
11671.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerOptions.Capabilities">
11672.             <summary>
11673.             Gets or sets server capabilities to advertise to the client.
11674.             </summary>
11675.             <remarks>
11676.             These determine which features will be available when a client connects.
11677.             Capabilities can include "tools", "prompts", "resources", "logging", and other
11678.             protocol-specific functionality.
11679.             </remarks>
11680.         </member>
11681.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerOptions.ProtocolVersion">
11682.             <summary>
11683.             Gets or sets the protocol version supported by this server, using a date-based versioning scheme.
11684.             </summary>
11685.             <remarks>
11686.             The protocol version defines which features and message formats this server supports.
11687.             This uses a date-based versioning scheme in the format "YYYY-MM-DD".
11688.             If <see langword="null"/>, the server will advertise to the client the version requested
11689.             by the client if that version is known to be supported, and otherwise will advertise the latest
11690.             version supported by the server.
11691.             </remarks>
11692.         </member>
11693.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerOptions.InitializationTimeout">
11694.             <summary>
11695.             Gets or sets a timeout used for the client-server initialization handshake sequence.
11696.             </summary>
11697.             <remarks>
11698.             This timeout determines how long the server will wait for client responses during
11699.             the initialization protocol handshake. If the client doesn't respond within this timeframe,
11700.             the initialization process will be aborted.
11701.             </remarks>
11702.         </member>
11703.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerOptions.ServerInstructions">
11704.             <summary>
11705.             Gets or sets optional server instructions to send to clients.
11706.             </summary>
11707.             <remarks>
11708.             These instructions are sent to clients during the initialization handshake and provide
11709.             guidance on how to effectively use the server's capabilities. They should focus on
11710.             information that helps models use the server effectively and should not duplicate
11711.             tool, prompt, or resource descriptions already exposed elsewhere.
11712.             Client applications typically use these instructions as system messages for LLM interactions
11713.             to provide context about available functionality.
11714.             </remarks>
11715.         </member>
11716.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerOptions.ScopeRequests">
11717.             <summary>
11718.             Gets or sets a value that indicates whether to create a new service provider scope for each handled request.
11719.             </summary>
11720.             <value>
11721.             <see langword="true"/> if each invocation of a request handler is invoked within a new service scope.
11722.             The default is <see langword="true"/>.
11723.             </value>
11724.         </member>
11725.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerOptions.KnownClientInfo">
11726.             <summary>
11727.             Gets or sets preexisting knowledge about the client including its name and version to help support
11728.             stateless Streamable HTTP servers that encode this knowledge in the mcp-session-id header.
11729.             </summary>
11730.             <remarks>
11731.             <para>
11732.             When not specified, this information is sourced from the client's initialize request.
11733.             </para>
11734.             </remarks>
11735.         </member>
11736.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerOptions.KnownClientCapabilities">
11737.             <summary>
11738.             Gets or sets preexisting knowledge about the client's capabilities to support session migration
11739.             scenarios where the client will not re-send the initialize request.
11740.             </summary>
11741.             <remarks>
11742.             <para>
11743.             When not specified, this information is sourced from the client's initialize request.
11744.             This is typically set during session migration in conjunction with <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerOptions.KnownClientInfo"/>.
11745.             </para>
11746.             </remarks>
11747.         </member>
11748.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerOptions.Handlers">
11749.             <summary>
11750.             Gets or sets the container of handlers used by the server for processing protocol messages.
11751.             </summary>
11752.         </member>
11753.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerOptions.Filters">
11754.             <summary>
11755.             Gets or sets the filter collections for MCP server handlers.
11756.             </summary>
11757.             <remarks>
11758.             This property provides access to filter collections that can be used to modify the behavior
11759.             of various MCP server handlers. The first filter added is the outermost (first to execute),
11760.             and each subsequent filter wraps closer to the handler.
11761.             </remarks>
11762.         </member>
11763.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerOptions.ToolCollection">
11764.             <summary>
11765.             Gets or sets a collection of tools served by the server.
11766.             </summary>
11767.             <remarks>
11768.             Tools specified via <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerOptions.ToolCollection"/> augment the <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerHandlers.ListToolsHandler"/> and
11769.             <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerHandlers.CallToolHandler"/>, if provided. ListTools requests will output information about every tool
11770.             in <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerOptions.ToolCollection"/> and then also any tools output by <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerHandlers.ListToolsHandler"/>, if it's
11771.             non-<see langword="null"/>. CallTool requests will first check <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerOptions.ToolCollection"/> for the tool
11772.             being requested, and if the tool is not found in the <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerOptions.ToolCollection"/>, any specified <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerHandlers.CallToolHandler"/>
11773.             will be invoked as a fallback.
11774.             </remarks>
11775.         </member>
11776.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerOptions.ResourceCollection">
11777.             <summary>
11778.             Gets or sets a collection of resources served by the server.
11779.             </summary>
11780.             <remarks>
11781.             <para>
11782.             Resources specified via <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerOptions.ResourceCollection"/> augment the <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerHandlers.ListResourcesHandler"/>, <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerHandlers.ListResourceTemplatesHandler"/>
11783.             and <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerHandlers.ReadResourceHandler"/> handlers, if provided. Resources with template expressions in their URI templates are considered resource templates
11784.             and are listed via ListResourceTemplate, whereas resources without template parameters are considered static resources and are listed with ListResources.
11785.             </para>
11786.             <para>
11787.             ReadResource requests will first check the <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerOptions.ResourceCollection"/> for the exact resource being requested. If no match is found, they'll proceed to
11788.             try to match the resource against each resource template in <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerOptions.ResourceCollection"/>. If no match is still found, the request will fall back to
11789.             any handler registered for <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerHandlers.ReadResourceHandler"/>.
11790.             </para>
11791.             </remarks>
11792.         </member>
11793.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerOptions.PromptCollection">
11794.             <summary>
11795.             Gets or sets a collection of prompts that will be served by the server.
11796.             </summary>
11797.             <remarks>
11798.             <para>
11799.             The <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerOptions.PromptCollection"/> contains the predefined prompts that clients can request from the server.
11800.             This collection works in conjunction with <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerHandlers.ListPromptsHandler"/> and <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerHandlers.GetPromptHandler"/>
11801.             when those are provided:
11802.             </para>
11803.             <para>
11804.             - For <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.PromptsList"/> requests: The server returns all prompts from this collection
11805.               plus any additional prompts provided by the <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerHandlers.ListPromptsHandler"/> if it's set.
11806.             </para>
11807.             <para>
11808.             - For <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestMethods.PromptsGet"/> requests: The server first checks this collection for the requested prompt.
11809.               If not found, it will invoke the <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerHandlers.GetPromptHandler"/> as a fallback if one is set.
11810.             </para>
11811.             </remarks>
11812.         </member>
11813.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerOptions.MaxSamplingOutputTokens">
11814.             <summary>
11815.             Gets or sets the default maximum number of tokens to use for sampling requests when not explicitly specified.
11816.             </summary>
11817.             <value>
11818.             The default maximum number of tokens to use for sampling requests. The default value is 1000 tokens.
11819.             </value>
11820.             <remarks>
11821.             This value is used in <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServer.SampleAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatMessage},Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions,System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>
11822.             when <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatOptions.MaxOutputTokens"/> is not set in the request options.
11823.             </remarks>
11824.         </member>
11825.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerOptions.TaskStore">
11826.             <summary>
11827.             Gets or sets the task store for managing asynchronous task execution.
11828.             </summary>
11829.             <remarks>
11830.             <para>
11831.             When non-null, enables explicit task support with persistence, allowing clients to:
11832.             <list type="bullet">
11833.             <item><description>Execute operations asynchronously by augmenting requests with task metadata</description></item>
11834.             <item><description>Poll for task status via tasks/get requests</description></item>
11835.             <item><description>Retrieve task results via tasks/result requests</description></item>
11836.             <item><description>List all tasks via tasks/list requests</description></item>
11837.             <item><description>Cancel tasks via tasks/cancel requests</description></item>
11838.             </list>
11839.             </para>
11840.             <para>
11841.             When null, implicit task support may still be available for async methods (returning <see cref="T:System.Threading.Tasks.Task"/> or
11842.             <see cref="T:System.Threading.Tasks.ValueTask"/>), but tasks will be ephemeral and not persisted. Use <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.InMemoryMcpTaskStore"/>
11843.             for development/testing or implement <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.IMcpTaskStore"/> for production scenarios.
11844.             </para>
11845.             <para>
11846.             The server will automatically advertise task capabilities based on the presence of a task store
11847.             and the detection of async server primitives (tools, prompts, resources).
11848.             </para>
11849.             </remarks>
11850.         </member>
11851.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerOptions.SendTaskStatusNotifications">
11852.             <summary>
11853.             Gets or sets whether to send task status notifications to clients.
11854.             </summary>
11855.             <value>
11856.             <see langword="true"/> to send optional <c>notifications/tasks/status</c> notifications when task status changes;
11857.             <see langword="false"/> to not send notifications. The default is <see langword="false"/>.
11858.             </value>
11859.             <remarks>
11860.             <para>
11861.             When enabled, the server will send <c>notifications/tasks/status</c> notifications to inform clients
11862.             of task state changes. According to the MCP specification, these notifications are optional and
11863.             receivers MAY send them but are not required to.
11864.             </para>
11865.             <para>
11866.             Clients must not rely on receiving these notifications and should continue polling via <c>tasks/get</c>
11867.             requests to ensure they receive status updates.
11868.             </para>
11869.             <para>
11870.             Even when this is set to <see langword="true"/>, notifications are only sent when <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerOptions.TaskStore"/>
11871.             is configured, as task-augmented requests require a task store.
11872.             </para>
11873.             </remarks>
11874.         </member>

11970.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPrompt">
11971.             <summary>
11972.             Represents an invocable prompt used by Model Context Protocol clients and servers.
11973.             </summary>
11974.             <remarks>
11975.             <para>
11976.             <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPrompt"/> is an abstract base class that represents an MCP prompt for use in the server (as opposed
11977.             to <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Prompt"/>, which provides the protocol representation of a prompt, and <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClientPrompt"/>, which
11978.             provides a client-side representation of a prompt). Instances of <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPrompt"/> can be added into a
11979.             <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IServiceCollection"/> to be picked up automatically when <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServer"/> is used to create
11980.             an <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServer"/>, or added into a <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPrimitiveCollection`1"/>.
11981.             </para>
11982.             <para>
11983.             Most commonly, <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPrompt"/> instances are created using the static <see cref="M:McpServerPrompt.Create"/> methods.
11984.             These methods enable creating an <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPrompt"/> for a method, specified via a <see cref="T:System.Delegate"/> or
11985.             <see cref="T:System.Reflection.MethodInfo"/>, and are what are used implicitly by WithPromptsFromAssembly and WithPrompts. The <see cref="M:McpServerPrompt.Create"/> methods
11986.             create <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPrompt"/> instances capable of working with a large variety of .NET method signatures, automatically handling
11987.             how parameters are marshaled into the method from the JSON received from the MCP client, and how the return value is marshaled back
11988.             into the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.GetPromptResult"/> that's then serialized and sent back to the client.
11989.             </para>
11990.             <para>
11991.             By default, parameters are sourced from the <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.GetPromptRequestParams.Arguments"/> dictionary, which is a collection
11992.             of key/value pairs. Those parameters are deserialized from the
11993.             <see cref="T:System.Text.Json.JsonElement"/> values in that collection. There are a few exceptions to this:
11994.             <list type="bullet">
11995.               <item>
11996.                 <description>
11997.                   <see cref="T:System.Threading.CancellationToken"/> parameters are automatically bound to a <see cref="T:System.Threading.CancellationToken"/> provided by the
11998.                   <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServer"/> and that respects any <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CancelledNotificationParams"/>s sent by the client for this operation's
11999.                   <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestId"/>.
12000.                 </description>
12001.               </item>
12002.               <item>
12003.                 <description>
12004.                   <see cref="T:System.IServiceProvider"/> parameters are bound from the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.RequestContext`1"/> for this request.
12005.                 </description>
12006.               </item>
12007.               <item>
12008.                 <description>
12009.                   <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServer"/> parameters are bound directly to the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServer"/> instance associated
12010.                   with this request's <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.RequestContext`1"/>. Such parameters may be used to understand
12011.                   what server is being used to process the request, and to interact with the client issuing the request to that server.
12012.                 </description>
12013.               </item>
12014.               <item>
12015.                 <description>
12016.                   <see cref="T:System.IProgress`1"/> parameters accepting <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.ProgressNotificationValue"/> values
12017.                   are bound to an <see cref="T:System.IProgress`1"/> instance manufactured to forward progress notifications
12018.                   from the prompt to the client. If the client included a <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ProgressToken"/> in their request, progress reports issued
12019.                   to this instance will propagate to the client as <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.NotificationMethods.ProgressNotification"/> notifications with
12020.                   that token. If the client did not include a <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ProgressToken"/>, the instance will ignore any progress reports issued to it.
12021.                 </description>
12022.               </item>
12023.               <item>
12024.                 <description>
12025.                   When the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPrompt"/> is constructed, it may be passed an <see cref="T:System.IServiceProvider"/> via
12026.                   <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPromptCreateOptions.Services"/>. Any parameter that can be satisfied by that <see cref="T:System.IServiceProvider"/>
12027.                   according to <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IServiceProviderIsService"/> will be resolved from the <see cref="T:System.IServiceProvider"/> provided to
12028.                   <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPrompt.GetAsync(ModelContextProtocol.Server.RequestContext{ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.GetPromptRequestParams},System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/> rather than from the argument collection.
12029.                 </description>
12030.               </item>
12031.               <item>
12032.                 <description>
12033.                   Any parameter attributed with <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.FromKeyedServicesAttribute"/> will similarly be resolved from the
12034.                   <see cref="T:System.IServiceProvider"/> provided to <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPrompt.GetAsync(ModelContextProtocol.Server.RequestContext{ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.GetPromptRequestParams},System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/> rather than from the argument collection.
12035.                 </description>
12036.               </item>
12037.             </list>
12038.             </para>
12039.             <para>
12040.             All other parameters are deserialized from the <see cref="T:System.Text.Json.JsonElement"/>s in the <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.GetPromptRequestParams.Arguments"/> dictionary.
12041.             </para>
12042.             <para>
12043.             In general, the data supplied via the <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.GetPromptRequestParams.Arguments"/>'s dictionary is passed along from the caller and
12044.             should thus be considered unvalidated and untrusted. To provide validated and trusted data to the invocation of the prompt, consider having
12045.             the prompt be an instance method, referring to data stored in the instance, or using an instance or parameters resolved from the <see cref="T:System.IServiceProvider"/>
12046.             to provide data to the method.
12047.             </para>
12048.             <para>
12049.             Return values from a method are used to create the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.GetPromptResult"/> that is sent back to the client:
12050.             </para>
12051.             <list type="table">
12052.               <item>
12053.                 <term><see cref="T:System.String"/></term>
12054.                 <description>Converted to a list containing a single <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PromptMessage"/> with its <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PromptMessage.Content"/> set to contain the <see cref="T:System.String"/>.</description>
12055.               </item>
12056.               <item>
12057.                 <term><see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PromptMessage"/></term>
12058.                 <description>Converted to a list containing the single <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PromptMessage"/>.</description>
12059.               </item>
12060.               <item>
12061.                 <term><see cref="T:System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable`1"/> of <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PromptMessage"/></term>
12062.                 <description>Converted to a list containing all of the returned <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PromptMessage"/> instances.</description>
12063.               </item>
12064.               <item>
12065.                 <term><see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatMessage"/></term>
12066.                 <description>Converted to a list of <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PromptMessage"/> instances derived from the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatMessage"/> with <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.AIContentExtensions.ToPromptMessages(Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatMessage)"/>.</description>
12067.               </item>
12068.               <item>
12069.                 <term><see cref="T:System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable`1"/> of <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatMessage"/></term>
12070.                 <description>Converted to a list of <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PromptMessage"/> instances derived from all of the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatMessage"/> instances with <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.AIContentExtensions.ToPromptMessages(Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatMessage)"/>.</description>
12071.               </item>
12072.               <item>
12073.                 <term><see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.GetPromptResult"/></term>
12074.                 <description>Returned directly without modification.</description>
12075.               </item>
12076.             </list>
12077.             <para>
12078.             Other returned types will result in an <see cref="T:System.InvalidOperationException"/> being thrown.
12079.             </para>
12080.             <para>
12081.             Parameters of type <see cref="T:System.String"/> that are decorated with <c>AllowedValuesAttribute</c>
12082.             will automatically have their allowed values surfaced as completions in response to <c>completion/complete</c> requests from clients,
12083.             without requiring a custom <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerHandlers.CompleteHandler"/> to be configured.
12084.             </para>
12085.             </remarks>
12086.         </member>
12087.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPrompt.#ctor">
12088.             <summary>Initializes a new instance of the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPrompt"/> class.</summary>
12089.         </member>
12090.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPrompt.ProtocolPrompt">
12091.             <summary>Gets the protocol <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Prompt"/> type for this instance.</summary>
12092.             <remarks>
12093.             The ProtocolPrompt property represents the underlying prompt definition as defined in the
12094.             Model Context Protocol specification. It contains metadata like the prompt's name,
12095.             description, and acceptable arguments.
12096.             </remarks>
12097.         </member>

12103.             Contains attributes from the associated MethodInfo and declaring class (if any),

12106.         </member>
12107.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPrompt.GetAsync(ModelContextProtocol.Server.RequestContext{ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.GetPromptRequestParams},System.Threading.CancellationToken)">
12108.             <summary>
12109.             Gets the prompt, rendering it with the provided request parameters and returning the prompt result.
12110.             </summary>
12111.             <param name="request">
12112.             The request context containing information about the prompt invocation, including any arguments
12113.             passed to the prompt. This object provides access to both the request parameters and the server context.
12114.             </param>
12115.             <param name="cancellationToken">
12116.             The <see cref="T:System.Threading.CancellationToken"/> to monitor for cancellation requests. The default is <see cref="P:System.Threading.CancellationToken.None"/>.
12117.             </param>
12118.             <returns>
12119.             A <see cref="T:System.Threading.Tasks.Task"/> representing the asynchronous operation, containing a <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.GetPromptResult"/> with
12120.             the prompt content and messages.
12121.             </returns>
12122.             <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="request"/> is <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
12123.             <exception cref="T:System.InvalidOperationException">The prompt implementation returns <see langword="null"/> or an unsupported result type.</exception>
12124.         </member>
12125.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPrompt.Create(System.Delegate,ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPromptCreateOptions)">

12134.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPrompt.Create(System.Reflection.MethodInfo,System.Object,ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPromptCreateOptions)">

12136.             Creates an <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPrompt"/> instance for a method, specified via a <see cref="T:System.Reflection.MethodInfo"/> instance.

12138.             <param name="method">The method to be represented via the created <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPrompt"/>.</param>
12139.             <param name="target">The instance if <paramref name="method"/> is an instance method; otherwise, <see langword="null"/>.</param>

12141.             <returns>The created <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPrompt"/> for invoking <paramref name="method"/>.</returns>

12145.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPrompt.Create(System.Reflection.MethodInfo,System.Func{ModelContextProtocol.Server.RequestContext{ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.GetPromptRequestParams},System.Object},ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPromptCreateOptions)">
12146.             <summary>
12147.             Creates an <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPrompt"/> instance for a method, specified via a <see cref="T:System.Reflection.MethodInfo"/> for
12148.             an instance method, along with a <see cref="T:System.Type"/> representing the type of the target object to
12149.             instantiate each time the method is invoked.
12150.             </summary>
12151.             <param name="method">The instance method to be represented via the created <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPrompt"/>.</param>
12152.             <param name="createTargetFunc">
12153.             Callback used on each function invocation to create an instance of the type on which the instance method <paramref name="method"/>
12154.             will be invoked. If the returned instance is <see cref="T:System.IAsyncDisposable"/> or <see cref="T:System.IDisposable"/>, it will
12155.             be disposed of after method completes its invocation.
12156.             </param>
12157.             <param name="options">Optional options used in the creation of the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPrompt"/> to control its behavior.</param>
12158.             <returns>The created <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPrompt"/> for invoking <paramref name="method"/>.</returns>
12159.             <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="method"/> or <paramref name="createTargetFunc"/> is <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
12160.         </member>
12161.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPrompt.Create(Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction,ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPromptCreateOptions)">
12162.             <summary>Creates an <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPrompt"/> that wraps the specified <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/>.</summary>
12163.             <param name="function">The function to wrap.</param>
12164.             <param name="options">Optional options used in the creation of the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPrompt"/> to control its behavior.</param>
12165.             <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="function"/> is <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
12166.             <remarks>
12167.             Unlike the other overloads of Create, the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPrompt"/> created by <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPrompt.Create(Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction,ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPromptCreateOptions)"/>
12168.             does not provide all of the special parameter handling for MCP-specific concepts, like <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServer"/>.
12169.             </remarks>
12170.         </member>
12171.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPrompt.ToString">
12172.             <inheritdoc />
12173.         </member>
12174.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPrompt.ModelContextProtocol#Server#IMcpServerPrimitive#Id">
12175.             <inheritdoc />
12176.         </member>
12177.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPromptAttribute">
12178.             <summary>
12179.             Used to indicate that a method should be considered an <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPrompt"/>.
12180.             </summary>
12181.             <remarks>
12182.             <para>
12183.             This attribute is applied to methods that should be exposed as prompts in the Model Context Protocol. When a class
12184.             containing methods marked with this attribute is registered with McpServerBuilderExtensions,
12185.             these methods become available as prompts that can be called by MCP clients.
12186.             </para>
12187.             <para>
12188.             When methods are provided directly to <see cref="M:McpServerPrompt.Create"/>, the attribute is not required.
12189.             </para>
12190.             <para>
12191.             By default, parameters are sourced from the <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.GetPromptRequestParams.Arguments"/> dictionary, which is a collection
12192.             of key/value pairs. Those parameters are deserialized from the
12193.             <see cref="T:System.Text.Json.JsonElement"/> values in that collection. There are a few exceptions to this:
12194.             <list type="bullet">
12195.               <item>
12196.                 <description>
12197.                   <see cref="T:System.Threading.CancellationToken"/> parameters are automatically bound to a <see cref="T:System.Threading.CancellationToken"/> provided by the
12198.                   <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServer"/> and that respects any <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CancelledNotificationParams"/>s sent by the client for this operation's
12199.                   <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestId"/>.
12200.                 </description>
12201.               </item>
12202.               <item>
12203.                 <description>
12204.                   <see cref="T:System.IServiceProvider"/> parameters are bound from the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.RequestContext`1"/> for this request.
12205.                 </description>
12206.               </item>
12207.               <item>
12208.                 <description>
12209.                   <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServer"/> parameters are bound directly to the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServer"/> instance associated
12210.                   with this request's <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.RequestContext`1"/>. Such parameters may be used to understand
12211.                   what server is being used to process the request, and to interact with the client issuing the request to that server.
12212.                 </description>
12213.               </item>
12214.               <item>
12215.                 <description>
12216.                   <see cref="T:System.IProgress`1"/> parameters accepting <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.ProgressNotificationValue"/> values
12217.                   are bound to an <see cref="T:System.IProgress`1"/> instance manufactured to forward progress notifications
12218.                   from the prompt to the client. If the client included a <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ProgressToken"/> in their request, progress reports issued
12219.                   to this instance will propagate to the client as <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.NotificationMethods.ProgressNotification"/> notifications with
12220.                   that token. If the client did not include a <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ProgressToken"/>, the instance will ignore any progress reports issued to it.
12221.                 </description>
12222.               </item>
12223.               <item>
12224.                 <description>
12225.                   When the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPrompt"/> is constructed, it may be passed an <see cref="T:System.IServiceProvider"/> via
12226.                   <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPromptCreateOptions.Services"/>. Any parameter that can be satisfied by that <see cref="T:System.IServiceProvider"/>
12227.                   according to <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IServiceProviderIsService"/> will be resolved from the <see cref="T:System.IServiceProvider"/> provided to the
12228.                   prompt invocation rather than from the argument collection.
12229.                 </description>
12230.               </item>
12231.               <item>
12232.                 <description>
12233.                   Any parameter attributed with <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.FromKeyedServicesAttribute"/> will similarly be resolved from the
12234.                   <see cref="T:System.IServiceProvider"/> provided to the prompt invocation rather than from the argument collection.
12235.                 </description>
12236.               </item>
12237.             </list>
12238.             </para>
12239.             <para>
12240.             All other parameters are deserialized from the <see cref="T:System.Text.Json.JsonElement"/>s in the <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.GetPromptRequestParams.Arguments"/> dictionary.
12241.             </para>
12242.             <para>
12243.             In general, the data supplied via the <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.GetPromptRequestParams.Arguments"/>'s dictionary is passed along from the caller and
12244.             should thus be considered unvalidated and untrusted. To provide validated and trusted data to the invocation of the prompt, consider having
12245.             the prompt be an instance method, referring to data stored in the instance, or using an instance or parameters resolved from the <see cref="T:System.IServiceProvider"/>
12246.             to provide data to the method.
12247.             </para>
12248.             <para>
12249.             Return values from a method are used to create the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.GetPromptResult"/> that is sent back to the client:
12250.             </para>
12251.             <list type="table">
12252.               <item>
12253.                 <term><see cref="T:System.String"/></term>
12254.                 <description>Converted to a list containing a single <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PromptMessage"/> with its <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PromptMessage.Content"/> set to contain the <see cref="T:System.String"/>.</description>
12255.               </item>
12256.               <item>
12257.                 <term><see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PromptMessage"/></term>
12258.                 <description>Converted to a list containing the single <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PromptMessage"/>.</description>
12259.               </item>
12260.               <item>
12261.                 <term><see cref="T:System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable`1"/> of <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PromptMessage"/></term>
12262.                 <description>Converted to a list containing all of the returned <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PromptMessage"/> instances.</description>
12263.               </item>
12264.               <item>
12265.                 <term><see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatMessage"/></term>
12266.                 <description>Converted to a list of <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PromptMessage"/> instances derived from the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatMessage"/> with <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.AIContentExtensions.ToPromptMessages(Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatMessage)"/>.</description>
12267.               </item>
12268.               <item>
12269.                 <term><see cref="T:System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable`1"/> of <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatMessage"/></term>
12270.                 <description>Converted to a list of <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.PromptMessage"/> instances derived from all of the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatMessage"/> instances with <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.AIContentExtensions.ToPromptMessages(Microsoft.Extensions.AI.ChatMessage)"/>.</description>
12271.               </item>
12272.             </list>
12273.             <para>
12274.             Other returned types will result in an <see cref="T:System.InvalidOperationException"/> being thrown.
12275.             </para>
12276.             <para>
12277.             Parameters of type <see cref="T:System.String"/> that are decorated with <c>AllowedValuesAttribute</c>
12278.             will automatically have their allowed values surfaced as completions in response to <c>completion/complete</c> requests from clients,
12279.             without requiring a custom <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerHandlers.CompleteHandler"/> to be configured.
12280.             </para>
12281.             </remarks>
12282.         </member>
12283.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPromptAttribute.#ctor">
12284.             <summary>
12285.             Initializes a new instance of the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPromptAttribute"/> class.
12286.             </summary>
12287.         </member>
12288.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPromptAttribute.Name">
12289.             <summary>Gets or sets the name of the prompt.</summary>
12290.             <remarks>If <see langword="null"/>, the method name is used.</remarks>
12291.         </member>
12292.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPromptAttribute.Title">
12293.             <summary>Gets or sets the title of the prompt.</summary>
12294.         </member>
12295.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPromptAttribute.IconSource">
12296.             <summary>
12297.             Gets or sets the source URI for the prompt's icon.
12298.             </summary>
12299.             <remarks>
12300.             <para>
12301.             This value can be an HTTP/HTTPS URL pointing to an image file or a data URI with base64-encoded image data.
12302.             When specified, a single icon will be added to the prompt.
12303.             </para>
12304.             <para>
12305.             For more advanced icon configuration (multiple icons, MIME type specification, size characteristics),
12306.             use <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPromptCreateOptions.Icons"/> when creating the prompt programmatically.
12307.             </para>
12308.             </remarks>
12309.         </member>
12310.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPromptCreateOptions">
12311.             <summary>
12312.             Provides options for controlling the creation of an <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPrompt"/>.
12313.             </summary>
12314.             <remarks>
12315.             <para>
12316.             These options allow for customizing the behavior and metadata of prompts created with
12317.             <see cref="M:McpServerPrompt.Create"/>. They provide control over naming, description,
12318.             and dependency injection integration.
12319.             </para>
12320.             <para>
12321.             When creating prompts programmatically rather than using attributes, these options
12322.             provide the same level of configuration flexibility.
12323.             </para>
12324.             </remarks>
12325.         </member>
12326.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPromptCreateOptions.Services">
12327.             <summary>
12328.             Gets or sets optional services used in the construction of the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPrompt"/>.
12329.             </summary>
12330.             <remarks>
12331.             These services will be used to determine which parameters should be satisfied from dependency injection. As such,
12332.             what services are satisfied via this provider should match what's satisfied via the provider passed in at invocation time.
12333.             </remarks>
12334.         </member>
12335.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPromptCreateOptions.Name">
12336.             <summary>
12337.             Gets or sets the name to use for the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPrompt"/>.
12338.             </summary>
12339.             <remarks>
12340.             If <see langword="null"/>, but an <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPromptAttribute"/> is applied to the method,
12341.             the name from the attribute is used. If that's not present, a name based on the method's name is used.
12342.             </remarks>
12343.         </member>
12344.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPromptCreateOptions.Title">
12345.             <summary>
12346.             Gets or sets the title to use for the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPrompt"/>.
12347.             </summary>
12348.         </member>
12349.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPromptCreateOptions.Description">
12350.             <summary>
12351.             Gets or sets the description to use for the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPrompt"/>.
12352.             </summary>
12353.             <remarks>
12354.             If <see langword="null"/>, but a <see cref="T:System.ComponentModel.DescriptionAttribute"/> is applied to the method,
12355.             the description from that attribute is used.
12356.             </remarks>
12357.         </member>
12358.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPromptCreateOptions.SerializerOptions">
12359.             <summary>
12360.             Gets or sets the JSON serializer options to use when marshalling data to/from JSON.
12361.             </summary>
12362.             <remarks>
12363.             Defaults to <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.McpJsonUtilities.DefaultOptions"/> if left unspecified.
12364.             </remarks>
12365.         </member>
12366.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPromptCreateOptions.SchemaCreateOptions">
12367.             <summary>
12368.             Gets or sets the JSON schema options when creating an <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/> from a method.
12369.             </summary>
12370.             <remarks>
12371.             Defaults to <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIJsonSchemaCreateOptions.Default"/> if left unspecified.
12372.             </remarks>
12373.         </member>
12374.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPromptCreateOptions.Metadata">
12375.             <summary>
12376.             Gets or sets the metadata associated with the prompt.
12377.             </summary>
12378.             <remarks>
12379.             Metadata includes information such as the attributes extracted from the method and its declaring class.
12380.             If not provided, metadata will be automatically generated for methods created via reflection.
12381.             </remarks>
12382.         </member>
12383.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPromptCreateOptions.Icons">
12384.             <summary>
12385.             Gets or sets the icons for this prompt.
12386.             </summary>
12387.             <remarks>
12388.             This property can be used by clients to display the prompt's icon in a user interface.
12389.             </remarks>
12390.         </member>
12391.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPromptCreateOptions.Meta">
12392.             <summary>
12393.             Gets or sets metadata reserved by MCP for protocol-level metadata.
12394.             </summary>
12395.             <remarks>
12396.             <para>
12397.             This <see cref="T:System.Text.Json.Nodes.JsonObject"/> is used to seed the <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Prompt.Meta"/> property. Any metadata from
12398.             <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpMetaAttribute"/> instances on the method will be added to this object, but
12399.             properties already present in this <see cref="T:System.Text.Json.Nodes.JsonObject"/> will not be overwritten.
12400.             </para>
12401.             <para>
12402.             Implementations must not make assumptions about its contents.
12403.             </para>
12404.             </remarks>
12405.         </member>
12406.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPromptCreateOptions.Clone">
12407.             <summary>
12408.             Creates a shallow clone of the current <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPromptCreateOptions"/> instance.
12409.             </summary>
12410.         </member>
12411.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPromptTypeAttribute">
12412.             <summary>
12413.             Specifies that a type contains methods that should be exposed as <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPrompt"/>s.
12414.             </summary>
12415.             <remarks>
12416.             <para>
12417.             This attribute is used to mark a class containing methods that should be automatically
12418.             discovered and registered as <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPrompt"/> prompts. When combined with discovery methods like
12419.             WithPromptsFromAssembly, it enables automatic registration of prompts without explicitly listing each prompt class.
12420.             The attribute is not necessary when a reference to the type is provided directly to a method like WithPrompts.
12421.             </para>
12422.             <para>
12423.             Within a class marked with this attribute, individual methods that should be exposed as
12424.             prompts must be marked with the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPromptAttribute"/>.
12425.             </para>
12426.             </remarks>
12427.         </member>
12428.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerResource">
12429.             <summary>
12430.             Represents an invocable resource used by Model Context Protocol clients and servers.
12431.             </summary>
12432.             <remarks>
12433.             <para>
12434.             <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerResource"/> is an abstract base class that represents an MCP resource for use in the server (as opposed
12435.             to <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Resource"/> or <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ResourceTemplate"/>, which provide the protocol representations of a resource). Instances of
12436.             <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerResource"/> can be added into a <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IServiceCollection"/> to be picked up automatically when
12437.             <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServer"/> is used to create an <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServer"/>, or added into a <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPrimitiveCollection`1"/>.
12438.             </para>
12439.             <para>
12440.             Most commonly, <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerResource"/> instances are created using the static <see cref="M:McpServerResource.Create"/> methods.
12441.             These methods enable creating an <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerResource"/> for a method, specified via a <see cref="T:System.Delegate"/> or
12442.             <see cref="T:System.Reflection.MethodInfo"/>, and are what are used implicitly by WithResourcesFromAssembly and
12443.             <see cref="M:McpServerBuilderExtensions.WithResources"/>. The <see cref="M:McpServerResource.Create"/> methods
12444.             create <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerResource"/> instances capable of working with a large variety of .NET method signatures, automatically handling
12445.             how parameters are marshaled into the method from the URI received from the MCP client, and how the return value is marshaled back
12446.             into the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ReadResourceResult"/> that's then serialized and sent back to the client.
12447.             </para>
12448.             <para>
12449.             <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerResource"/> is used to represent both direct resources (for example,"resource://example") and templated
12450.             resources (for example,"resource://example/{id}").
12451.             </para>
12452.             <para>
12453.             Read resource requests do not contain separate arguments, only a URI. However, for templated resources, portions of that URI may be considered
12454.             as arguments and may be bound to parameters. Further, resource methods may accept parameters that will be bound to arguments based on their type.
12455.             <list type="bullet">
12456.               <item>
12457.                 <description>
12458.                   <see cref="T:System.Threading.CancellationToken"/> parameters are automatically bound to a <see cref="T:System.Threading.CancellationToken"/> provided by the
12459.                   <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServer"/> and that respects any <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CancelledNotificationParams"/>s sent by the client for this operation's
12460.                   <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestId"/>.
12461.                 </description>
12462.               </item>
12463.               <item>
12464.                 <description>
12465.                   <see cref="T:System.IServiceProvider"/> parameters are bound from the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.RequestContext`1"/> for this request.
12466.                 </description>
12467.               </item>
12468.               <item>
12469.                 <description>
12470.                   <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServer"/> parameters are bound directly to the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServer"/> instance associated
12471.                   with this request's <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.RequestContext`1"/>. Such parameters may be used to understand
12472.                   what server is being used to process the request, and to interact with the client issuing the request to that server.
12473.                 </description>
12474.               </item>
12475.               <item>
12476.                 <description>
12477.                   <see cref="T:System.IProgress`1"/> parameters accepting <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.ProgressNotificationValue"/> values
12478.                   are bound to an <see cref="T:System.IProgress`1"/> instance manufactured to forward progress notifications
12479.                   from the resource to the client. If the client included a <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ProgressToken"/> in their request, progress reports issued
12480.                   to this instance will propagate to the client as <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.NotificationMethods.ProgressNotification"/> notifications with
12481.                   that token. If the client did not include a <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ProgressToken"/>, the instance will ignore any progress reports issued to it.
12482.                 </description>
12483.               </item>
12484.               <item>
12485.                 <description>
12486.                   When the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerResource"/> is constructed, it may be passed an <see cref="T:System.IServiceProvider"/> via
12487.                   <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerResourceCreateOptions.Services"/>. Any parameter that can be satisfied by that <see cref="T:System.IServiceProvider"/>
12488.                   according to <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IServiceProviderIsService"/> will be resolved from the <see cref="T:System.IServiceProvider"/> provided to the
12489.                   resource invocation rather than from the argument collection.
12490.                 </description>
12491.               </item>
12492.               <item>
12493.                 <description>
12494.                   Any parameter attributed with <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.FromKeyedServicesAttribute"/> will similarly be resolved from the
12495.                   <see cref="T:System.IServiceProvider"/> provided to the resource invocation rather than from the argument collection.
12496.                 </description>
12497.               </item>
12498.               <item>
12499.                 <description>
12500.                   All other parameters are bound from the data in the URI.
12501.                 </description>
12502.               </item>
12503.             </list>
12504.             </para>
12505.             <para>
12506.             Return values from a method are used to create the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ReadResourceResult"/> that is sent back to the client:
12507.             </para>
12508.             <list type="table">
12509.               <item>
12510.                 <term><see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ResourceContents"/></term>
12511.                 <description>Wrapped in a list containing the single <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ResourceContents"/>.</description>
12512.               </item>
12513.               <item>
12514.                 <term><see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.TextContent"/></term>
12515.                 <description>Converted to a list containing a single <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.TextResourceContents"/>.</description>
12516.               </item>
12517.               <item>
12518.                 <term><see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.DataContent"/></term>
12519.                 <description>Converted to a list containing a single <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.BlobResourceContents"/>.</description>
12520.               </item>
12521.               <item>
12522.                 <term><see cref="T:System.String"/></term>
12523.                 <description>Converted to a list containing a single <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.TextResourceContents"/>.</description>
12524.               </item>
12525.               <item>
12526.                 <term><see cref="T:System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable`1"/> of <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ResourceContents"/></term>
12527.                 <description>Returned directly as a list of <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ResourceContents"/>.</description>
12528.               </item>
12529.               <item>
12530.                 <term><see cref="T:System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable`1"/> of <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIContent"/></term>
12531.                 <description>Converted to a list containing a <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.TextResourceContents"/> for each <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.TextContentBlock"/> and a <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.BlobResourceContents"/> for each <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.DataContent"/>.</description>
12532.               </item>
12533.               <item>
12534.                 <term><see cref="T:System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable`1"/> of <see cref="T:System.String"/></term>
12535.                 <description>Converted to a list containing a <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.TextResourceContents"/>, one for each <see cref="T:System.String"/>.</description>
12536.               </item>
12537.               <item>
12538.                 <term><see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ReadResourceResult"/></term>
12539.                 <description>Returned directly without modification.</description>
12540.               </item>
12541.             </list>
12542.             <para>
12543.             Other returned types will result in an <see cref="T:System.InvalidOperationException"/> being thrown.
12544.             </para>
12545.             <para>

12672.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerResourceAttribute">
12673.             <summary>
12674.             Indicates that a method or property should be considered an <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerResource"/>.
12675.             </summary>
12676.             <remarks>
12677.             <para>
12678.             This attribute is applied to methods or properties that should be exposed as resources in the Model Context Protocol. When a class
12679.             containing methods marked with this attribute is registered with McpServerBuilderExtensions,
12680.             these methods or properties become available as resources that can be called by MCP clients.
12681.             </para>
12682.             <para>
12683.             When methods are provided directly to <see cref="M:McpServerResource.Create"/>, the attribute is not required.
12684.             </para>
12685.             <para>
12686.             Read resource requests do not contain separate arguments, only a URI. However, for templated resources, portions of that URI may be considered
12687.             as arguments and may be bound to parameters. Further, resource methods may accept parameters that will be bound to arguments based on their type.
12688.             <list type="bullet">
12689.               <item>
12690.                 <description>
12691.                   <see cref="T:System.Threading.CancellationToken"/> parameters are automatically bound to a <see cref="T:System.Threading.CancellationToken"/> provided by the
12692.                   <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServer"/> and that respects any <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CancelledNotificationParams"/>s sent by the client for this operation's
12693.                   <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestId"/>.
12694.                 </description>
12695.               </item>
12696.               <item>
12697.                 <description>
12698.                   <see cref="T:System.IServiceProvider"/> parameters are bound from the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.RequestContext`1"/> for this request.
12699.                 </description>
12700.               </item>
12701.               <item>
12702.                 <description>
12703.                   <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServer"/> parameters are bound directly to the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServer"/> instance associated
12704.                   with this request's <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.RequestContext`1"/>. Such parameters may be used to understand
12705.                   what server is being used to process the request, and to interact with the client issuing the request to that server.
12706.                 </description>
12707.               </item>
12708.               <item>
12709.                 <description>
12710.                   <see cref="T:System.IProgress`1"/> parameters accepting <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.ProgressNotificationValue"/> values
12711.                   are bound to an <see cref="T:System.IProgress`1"/> instance manufactured to forward progress notifications
12712.                   from the resource to the client. If the client included a <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ProgressToken"/> in their request, progress reports issued
12713.                   to this instance will propagate to the client as <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.NotificationMethods.ProgressNotification"/> notifications with
12714.                   that token. If the client did not include a <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ProgressToken"/>, the instance will ignore any progress reports issued to it.
12715.                 </description>
12716.               </item>
12717.               <item>
12718.                 <description>
12719.                   When the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerResource"/> is constructed, it may be passed an <see cref="T:System.IServiceProvider"/> via
12720.                   <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerResourceCreateOptions.Services"/>. Any parameter that can be satisfied by that <see cref="T:System.IServiceProvider"/>
12721.                   according to <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IServiceProviderIsService"/> will be resolved from the <see cref="T:System.IServiceProvider"/> provided to the
12722.                   resource invocation rather than from the argument collection.
12723.                 </description>
12724.               </item>
12725.               <item>
12726.                 <description>
12727.                   Any parameter attributed with <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.FromKeyedServicesAttribute"/> will similarly be resolved from the
12728.                   <see cref="T:System.IServiceProvider"/> provided to the resource invocation rather than from the argument collection.
12729.                 </description>
12730.               </item>
12731.               <item>
12732.                 <description>
12733.                   All other parameters are bound from the data in the URI.
12734.                 </description>
12735.               </item>
12736.             </list>
12737.             </para>
12738.             <para>
12739.             Return values from a method are used to create the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ReadResourceResult"/> that is sent back to the client:
12740.             </para>
12741.             <list type="table">
12742.               <item>
12743.                 <term><see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ResourceContents"/></term>
12744.                 <description>Wrapped in a list containing the single <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ResourceContents"/>.</description>
12745.               </item>
12746.               <item>
12747.                 <term><see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.TextContentBlock"/></term>
12748.                 <description>Converted to a list containing a single <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.TextResourceContents"/>.</description>
12749.               </item>
12750.               <item>
12751.                 <term><see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.DataContent"/></term>
12752.                 <description>Converted to a list containing a single <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.BlobResourceContents"/>.</description>
12753.               </item>
12754.               <item>
12755.                 <term><see cref="T:System.String"/></term>
12756.                 <description>Converted to a list containing a single <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.TextResourceContents"/>.</description>
12757.               </item>
12758.               <item>
12759.                 <term><see cref="T:System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable`1"/> of <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ResourceContents"/></term>
12760.                 <description>Returned directly as a list of <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ResourceContents"/>.</description>
12761.               </item>
12762.               <item>
12763.                 <term><see cref="T:System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable`1"/> of <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIContent"/></term>
12764.                 <description>Converted to a list containing a <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.TextResourceContents"/> for each <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.TextContentBlock"/> and a <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.BlobResourceContents"/> for each <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.DataContent"/>.</description>
12765.               </item>
12766.               <item>
12767.                 <term><see cref="T:System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable`1"/> of <see cref="T:System.String"/></term>
12768.                 <description>Converted to a list containing a <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.TextResourceContents"/>, one for each <see cref="T:System.String"/>.</description>
12769.               </item>
12770.             </list>
12771.             <para>
12772.             Other returned types will result in an <see cref="T:System.InvalidOperationException"/> being thrown.
12773.             </para>
12774.             <para>
12775.             Parameters of type <see cref="T:System.String"/> that are decorated with <c>AllowedValuesAttribute</c>
12776.             will automatically have their allowed values surfaced as completions in response to <c>completion/complete</c> requests from clients,
12777.             without requiring a custom <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerHandlers.CompleteHandler"/> to be configured.
12778.             </para>
12779.             </remarks>
12780.         </member>

12933.             <para>
12934.             Implementations must not make assumptions about its contents.
12935.             </para>
12936.             </remarks>
12937.         </member>
12938.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerResourceCreateOptions.Clone">
