12943.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerResourceTypeAttribute">
12944.             <summary>
12945.             Indicates that a type contains members that should be exposed as <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerResource"/>s.
12946.             </summary>
12947.             <remarks>
12948.             <para>
12949.             This attribute is used to mark a class containing members that should be automatically
12950.             discovered and registered as <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerResource"/>s. When combined with discovery methods like
12951.             WithResourcesFromAssembly, it enables automatic registration of resources without explicitly listing each
12952.             resource class. The attribute is not necessary when a reference to the type is provided directly to a method
12953.             like McpServerBuilderExtensions.WithResources.
12954.             </para>
12955.             <para>
12956.             Within a class marked with this attribute, individual members that should be exposed as
12957.             resources must be marked with the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerResourceAttribute"/>.
12958.             </para>
12959.             </remarks>
12960.         </member>
12961.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerTool">
12962.             <summary>
12963.             Represents an invocable tool used by Model Context Protocol clients and servers.
12964.             </summary>
12965.             <remarks>
12966.             <para>
12967.             <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerTool"/> is an abstract base class that represents an MCP tool for use in the server (as opposed
12968.             to <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Tool"/>, which provides the protocol representation of a tool, and <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Client.McpClientTool"/>, which
12969.             provides a client-side representation of a tool). Instances of <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerTool"/> can be added into a
12970.             <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IServiceCollection"/> to be picked up automatically when <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServer"/> is used to create
12971.             an <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServer"/>, or added into a <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerPrimitiveCollection`1"/>.
12972.             </para>
12973.             <para>
12974.             Most commonly, <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerTool"/> instances are created using the static <see cref="M:McpServerTool.Create"/> methods.
12975.             These methods enable creating an <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerTool"/> for a method, specified via a <see cref="T:System.Delegate"/> or
12976.             <see cref="T:System.Reflection.MethodInfo"/>, and are what are used implicitly by WithToolsFromAssembly and WithTools. The <see cref="M:McpServerTool.Create"/> methods
12977.             create <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerTool"/> instances capable of working with a large variety of .NET method signatures, automatically handling
12978.             how parameters are marshaled into the method from the JSON received from the MCP client, and how the return value is marshaled back
12979.             into the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolResult"/> that's then serialized and sent back to the client.
12980.             </para>
12981.             <para>
12982.             By default, parameters are sourced from the <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolRequestParams.Arguments"/> dictionary, which is a collection
12983.             of key/value pairs, and are represented in the JSON schema for the function, as exposed in the returned <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerTool"/>'s
12984.             <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerTool.ProtocolTool"/>'s <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Tool.InputSchema"/>. Those parameters are deserialized from the
12985.             <see cref="T:System.Text.Json.JsonElement"/> values in that collection. There are a few exceptions to this:
12986.             <list type="bullet">
12987.               <item>
12988.                 <description>
12989.                   <see cref="T:System.Threading.CancellationToken"/> parameters are automatically bound to a <see cref="T:System.Threading.CancellationToken"/> provided by the
12990.                   <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServer"/> and that respects any <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CancelledNotificationParams"/>s sent by the client for this operation's
12991.                   <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestId"/>. The parameter is not included in the generated JSON schema.
12992.                 </description>
12993.               </item>
12994.               <item>
12995.                 <description>
12996.                   <see cref="T:System.IServiceProvider"/> parameters are bound from the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.RequestContext`1"/> for this request,
12997.                   and are not included in the JSON schema.
12998.                 </description>
12999.               </item>
13000.               <item>
13001.                 <description>
13002.                   <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServer"/> parameters are not included in the JSON schema and are bound directly to the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServer"/>
13003.                   instance associated with this request's <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.RequestContext`1"/>. Such parameters may be used to understand
13004.                   what server is being used to process the request, and to interact with the client issuing the request to that server.
13005.                 </description>
13006.               </item>
13007.               <item>
13008.                 <description>
13009.                   <see cref="T:System.IProgress`1"/> parameters accepting <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.ProgressNotificationValue"/> values
13010.                   are not included in the JSON schema and are bound to an <see cref="T:System.IProgress`1"/> instance manufactured
13011.                   to forward progress notifications from the tool to the client. If the client included a <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ProgressToken"/> in their request,
13012.                   progress reports issued to this instance will propagate to the client as <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.NotificationMethods.ProgressNotification"/> notifications with
13013.                   that token. If the client did not include a <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ProgressToken"/>, the instance will ignore any progress reports issued to it.
13014.                 </description>
13015.               </item>
13016.               <item>
13017.                 <description>
13018.                   When the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerTool"/> is constructed, it may be passed an <see cref="T:System.IServiceProvider"/> via
13019.                   <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerToolCreateOptions.Services"/>. Any parameter that can be satisfied by that <see cref="T:System.IServiceProvider"/>
13020.                   according to <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IServiceProviderIsService"/> will not be included in the generated JSON schema and will be resolved
13021.                   from the <see cref="T:System.IServiceProvider"/> provided to <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerTool.InvokeAsync(ModelContextProtocol.Server.RequestContext{ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolRequestParams},System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/> rather than from the argument collection.
13022.                 </description>
13023.               </item>
13024.               <item>
13025.                 <description>
13026.                   Any parameter attributed with <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.FromKeyedServicesAttribute"/> will similarly be resolved from the
13027.                   <see cref="T:System.IServiceProvider"/> provided to <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerTool.InvokeAsync(ModelContextProtocol.Server.RequestContext{ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolRequestParams},System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/> rather than from the argument
13028.                   collection, and will not be included in the generated JSON schema.
13029.                 </description>
13030.               </item>
13031.             </list>
13032.             </para>
13033.             <para>
13034.             All other parameters are deserialized from the <see cref="T:System.Text.Json.JsonElement"/>s in the <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolRequestParams.Arguments"/> dictionary,
13035.             using the <see cref="T:System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions"/> supplied in <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerToolCreateOptions.SerializerOptions"/>, or if none was provided,
13036.             using <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.McpJsonUtilities.DefaultOptions"/>.
13037.             </para>
13038.             <para>
13039.             In general, the data supplied via the <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolRequestParams.Arguments"/>'s dictionary is passed along from the caller and
13040.             should thus be considered unvalidated and untrusted. To provide validated and trusted data to the invocation of the tool, consider having
13041.             the tool be an instance method, referring to data stored in the instance, or using an instance or parameters resolved from the <see cref="T:System.IServiceProvider"/>
13042.             to provide data to the method.
13043.             </para>
13044.             <para>
13045.             The tool method is responsible for validating its own input arguments (e.g., checking required fields, value ranges, string lengths, or
13046.             any other business rules). Data annotations such as <c>RequiredAttribute</c> and
13047.             <c>MaxLengthAttribute</c> on parameter types influence the generated JSON schema exposed
13048.             to clients, but they are not enforced at runtime by the SDK. Validation should be performed explicitly within the tool method.
13049.             </para>
13050.             <para>
13051.             To signal an error (including validation failures) back to the client, either throw an <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.McpException"/>
13052.             or return a <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolResult"/> with <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolResult.IsError"/> set to <see langword="true"/>.
13053.             When a tool throws an <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.McpException"/>, its <see cref="P:System.Exception.Message"/> is included in the error result
13054.             sent to the client. Throwing any other exception type also results in an error <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolResult"/>, but with
13055.             a generic error message (to avoid leaking sensitive information). Alternatively, a tool can declare a return type of
13056.             <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolResult"/> to have full control over both success and error responses.
13057.             </para>
13058.             <para>
13059.             It is important to provide clear <see cref="T:System.ComponentModel.DescriptionAttribute"/> values on tool methods and their parameters.
13060.             These descriptions are surfaced to AI models and help them determine when and how to use the tool, what values to pass for each parameter,
13061.             and what constraints the parameters have. Well-written descriptions reduce incorrect tool invocations and improve the quality of
13062.             model interactions.
13063.             </para>
13064.             <para>
13065.             Return values from a method are used to create the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolResult"/> that is sent back to the client:
13066.             </para>
13067.             <list type="table">
13068.               <item>
13069.                 <term><see langword="null"/></term>
13070.                 <description>Returns an empty <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolResult.Content"/> list.</description>
13071.               </item>
13072.               <item>
13073.                 <term><see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIContent"/></term>
13074.                 <description>Converted to a single <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ContentBlock"/> object using <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.AIContentExtensions.ToContentBlock(Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIContent,System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions)"/>.</description>
13075.               </item>
13076.               <item>
13077.                 <term><see cref="T:System.String"/></term>
13078.                 <description>Converted to a single <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.TextContentBlock"/> object with its text set to the string value.</description>
13079.               </item>
13080.               <item>
13081.                 <term><see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ContentBlock"/></term>
13082.                 <description>Returned as a single-item <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ContentBlock"/> list.</description>
13083.               </item>
13084.               <item>
13085.                 <term><see cref="T:System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable`1"/> of <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIContent"/></term>
13086.                 <description>Each <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIContent"/> is converted to a <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ContentBlock"/> object using <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.AIContentExtensions.ToContentBlock(Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIContent,System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions)"/>.</description>
13087.               </item>
13088.               <item>
13089.                 <term><see cref="T:System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable`1"/> of <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ContentBlock"/></term>
13090.                 <description>Returned as the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ContentBlock"/> list.</description>
13091.               </item>
13092.               <item>
13093.                 <term><see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolResult"/></term>
13094.                 <description>Returned directly without modification.</description>
13095.               </item>
13096.               <item>
13097.                 <term>Other types</term>
13098.                 <description>Serialized to JSON and returned as a single <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ContentBlock"/> object with <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ContentBlock.Type"/> set to "text".</description>
13099.               </item>
13100.             </list>
13101.             </remarks>
13102.         </member>

13190.             <para>

13192.             of key/value pairs, and are represented in the JSON schema for the function, as exposed in the returned <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerTool"/>'s

13199.                   <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServer"/> and that respects any <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CancelledNotificationParams"/>s sent by the client for this operation's
13200.                   <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestId"/>. The parameter is not included in the generated JSON schema.
13201.                 </description>
13202.               </item>

13206.                   and are not included in the JSON schema.

13209.               <item>
13210.                 <description>
13211.                   <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServer"/> parameters are not included in the JSON schema and are bound directly to the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServer"/>
13212.                   instance associated with this request's <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.RequestContext`1"/>. Such parameters may be used to understand
13213.                   what server is being used to process the request, and to interact with the client issuing the request to that server.
13214.                 </description>
13215.               </item>

13221.                   progress reports issued to this instance will propagate to the client as <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.NotificationMethods.ProgressNotification"/> notifications with
13222.                   that token. If the client did not include a <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ProgressToken"/>, the instance will ignore any progress reports issued to it.
13223.                 </description>
13224.               </item>
13225.               <item>

13230.                   from the <see cref="T:System.IServiceProvider"/> provided when the tool is invoked rather than from the argument collection.
13231.                 </description>
13232.               </item>
13233.               <item>
13234.                 <description>
13235.                   Any parameter attributed with <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.FromKeyedServicesAttribute"/> will similarly be resolved from the
13236.                   <see cref="T:System.IServiceProvider"/> provided when the tool is invoked rather than from the argument
13237.                   collection, and will not be included in the generated JSON schema.
13238.                 </description>
13239.               </item>
13240.             </list>
13241.             </para>
13242.             <para>

13244.             using the <see cref="T:System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions"/> supplied in <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerToolCreateOptions.SerializerOptions"/>, or if none was provided,
13245.             using <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.McpJsonUtilities.DefaultOptions"/>.
13246.             </para>
13247.             <para>

13264.             a generic error message (to avoid leaking sensitive information). Alternatively, a tool can declare a return type of
13265.             <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolResult"/> to have full control over both success and error responses.
13266.             </para>
13267.             <para>
13268.             It is important to provide clear <see cref="T:System.ComponentModel.DescriptionAttribute"/> values on tool methods and their parameters.
13269.             These descriptions are surfaced to AI models and help them determine when and how to use the tool, what values to pass for each parameter,
13270.             and what constraints the parameters have. Well-written descriptions reduce incorrect tool invocations and improve the quality of
13271.             model interactions.
13272.             </para>
13273.             <para>
13274.             Return values from a method are used to create the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolResult"/> that is sent back to the client:
13275.             </para>
13276.             <list type="table">
13277.               <item>
13278.                 <term><see langword="null"/></term>
13279.                 <description>Returns an empty <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolResult.Content"/> list.</description>
13280.               </item>
13281.               <item>
13282.                 <term><see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIContent"/></term>
13283.                 <description>Converted to a single <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ContentBlock"/> object using <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.AIContentExtensions.ToContentBlock(Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIContent,System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions)"/>.</description>
13284.               </item>
13285.               <item>
13286.                 <term><see cref="T:System.String"/></term>
13287.                 <description>Converted to a single <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.TextContentBlock"/> object with its text set to the string value.</description>
13288.               </item>
13289.               <item>
13290.                 <term><see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ContentBlock"/></term>
13291.                 <description>Returned as a single-item <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ContentBlock"/> list.</description>
13292.               </item>
13293.               <item>
13294.                 <term><see cref="T:System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable`1"/> of <see cref="T:System.String"/></term>
13295.                 <description>Each <see cref="T:System.String"/> is converted to a <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ContentBlock"/> object with its text set to the string value.</description>
13296.               </item>
13297.               <item>
13298.                 <term><see cref="T:System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable`1"/> of <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIContent"/></term>
13299.                 <description>Each <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIContent"/> is converted to a <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ContentBlock"/> object using <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.AIContentExtensions.ToContentBlock(Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIContent,System.Text.Json.JsonSerializerOptions)"/>.</description>
13300.               </item>
13301.               <item>
13302.                 <term><see cref="T:System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable`1"/> of <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ContentBlock"/></term>
13303.                 <description>Returned as the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ContentBlock"/> list.</description>
13304.               </item>
13305.               <item>
13306.                 <term><see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolResult"/></term>
13307.                 <description>Returned directly without modification.</description>
13308.               </item>
13309.               <item>
13310.                 <term>Other types</term>
13311.                 <description>Serialized to JSON and returned as a single <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ContentBlock"/> object with <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ContentBlock.Type"/> set to "text".</description>
13312.               </item>
13313.             </list>
13314.             </remarks>
13315.         </member>
13316.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerToolAttribute.#ctor">
13317.             <summary>
13318.             Initializes a new instance of the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerToolAttribute"/> class.
13319.             </summary>
13320.         </member>
13321.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerToolAttribute.Name">
13322.             <summary>Gets the name of the tool.</summary>
13323.             <remarks>If <see langword="null"/>, the method name is used.</remarks>
13324.         </member>
13325.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerToolAttribute.Title">
13326.             <summary>
13327.             Gets or sets a human-readable title for the tool that can be displayed to users.
13328.             </summary>
13329.             <remarks>
13330.             <para>
13331.             The title provides a more descriptive, user-friendly name for the tool than the tool's
13332.             programmatic name. It is intended for display purposes and to help users understand
13333.             the tool's purpose at a glance.
13334.             </para>
13335.             <para>
13336.             Unlike the tool name (which follows programmatic naming conventions), the title can
13337.             include spaces, special characters, and be phrased in a more natural language style.
13338.             </para>
13339.             </remarks>
13340.         </member>
13341.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerToolAttribute.Destructive">
13342.             <summary>
13343.             Gets or sets a value that indicates whether the tool might perform destructive updates to its environment.
13344.             </summary>
13345.             <value>
13346.             <see langword="true"/> if the tool might perform destructive updates to its environment.
13347.             <see langword="false"/> if the tool performs only additive updates.
13348.             The default is <see langword="true"/>.
13349.             </value>
13350.             <remarks>
13351.             This property is most relevant when the tool modifies its environment (ReadOnly = false).
13352.             </remarks>
13353.         </member>
13354.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerToolAttribute.Idempotent">
13355.             <summary>
13356.             Gets or sets a value that indicates whether calling the tool repeatedly with the same arguments
13357.             has no additional effect on its environment.
13358.             </summary>
13359.             <value>
13360.             <see langword="true"/> if calling the tool repeatedly with the same arguments
13361.             has no additional effect on the environment; <see langword="false"/> if it does.
13362.             The default is <see langword="false"/>.
13363.             </value>
13364.             <remarks>
13365.             This property is most relevant when the tool modifies its environment (ReadOnly = false).
13366.             </remarks>
13367.         </member>
13368.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerToolAttribute.OpenWorld">
13369.             <summary>
13370.             Gets or sets a value that indicates whether this tool can interact with an "open world" of external entities.
13371.             </summary>
13372.             <value>
13373.             <see langword="true"/> if the tool can interact with an unpredictable or dynamic set of entities (like web search).
13374.             <see langword="false"/> if the tool's domain of interaction is closed and well-defined (like memory access).
13375.             The default is <see langword="true"/>.
13376.             </value>
13377.         </member>
13378.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerToolAttribute.ReadOnly">
13379.             <summary>
13380.             Gets or sets a value that indicates whether this tool does not modify its environment.
13381.             </summary>
13382.             <value>
13383.             <see langword="true"/> if the tool only performs read operations without changing state.
13384.             <see langword="false"/> if the tool might make modifications to its environment.
13385.             The default is <see langword="false"/>.
13386.             </value>
13387.             <remarks>
13388.             Read-only tools do not have side effects beyond computational resource usage.
13389.             They don't create, update, or delete data in any system.
13390.             </remarks>
13391.         </member>
13392.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerToolAttribute.UseStructuredContent">
13393.             <summary>
13394.             Gets or sets a value that indicates whether the tool should report an output schema for structured content.
13395.             </summary>
13396.             <value>
13397.             The default is <see langword="false"/>.
13398.             </value>
13399.             <remarks>
13400.             When enabled, the tool will attempt to populate the <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Tool.OutputSchema"/>
13401.             and provide structured content in the <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolResult.StructuredContent"/> property.
13402.             </remarks>
13403.         </member>
13404.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerToolAttribute.OutputSchemaType">
13405.             <summary>
13406.             Gets or sets a <see cref="T:System.Type"/> from which to generate the tool's output schema.
13407.             </summary>
13408.             <value>
13409.             The default is <see langword="null"/>, which means the output schema is inferred from the return type.
13410.             </value>
13411.             <remarks>
13412.             <para>
13413.             When set, a JSON schema is generated from the specified <see cref="T:System.Type"/> and used as the
13414.             <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Tool.OutputSchema"/> instead of the schema inferred from the tool method's return type.
13415.             This is particularly useful when a tool method returns <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolResult"/> directly
13416.             (to control properties like <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Result.Meta"/>, <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolResult.IsError"/>,
13417.             or <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolResult.StructuredContent"/>) but still needs to advertise a meaningful output
13418.             schema to clients.
13419.             </para>
13420.             <para>
13421.             <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerToolAttribute.UseStructuredContent"/> must also be set to <see langword="true"/> for this property to take effect.
13422.             </para>
13423.             </remarks>
13424.         </member>
13425.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerToolAttribute.IconSource">
13426.             <summary>
13427.             Gets or sets the source URI for the tool's icon.
13428.             </summary>
13429.             <remarks>
13430.             <para>
13431.             This value can be an HTTP/HTTPS URL pointing to an image file or a data URI with base64-encoded image data.
13432.             When specified, a single icon will be added to the tool.
13433.             </para>
13434.             <para>
13435.             For more advanced icon configuration (multiple icons, MIME type specification, size characteristics),
13436.             use <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerToolCreateOptions.Icons"/> when creating the tool programmatically.
13437.             </para>
13438.             </remarks>
13439.         </member>
13440.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerToolAttribute.TaskSupport">
13441.             <summary>
13442.             Gets or sets the task support configuration for the tool.
13443.             </summary>
13444.             <value>
13445.             A <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ToolTaskSupport"/> value indicating how the tool supports task-based invocation.
13446.             The default value is <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ToolTaskSupport.Forbidden"/>.
13447.             </value>
13448.             <remarks>
13449.             <para>
13450.             When set to <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ToolTaskSupport.Forbidden"/>, clients must not attempt to invoke the tool as a task.
13451.             When set to <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ToolTaskSupport.Optional"/>, clients may invoke the tool as a task or as a normal request.
13452.             When set to <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ToolTaskSupport.Required"/>, clients must invoke the tool as a task.
13453.             </para>
13454.             <para>
13455.             If this property is not explicitly set on the attribute, the task support behavior will be determined 
13456.             automatically based on the tool's characteristics (e.g., async methods default to <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ToolTaskSupport.Optional"/>).
13457.             </para>
13458.             </remarks>
13459.         </member>
13460.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerToolCreateOptions">
13461.             <summary>
13462.             Provides options for controlling the creation of an <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerTool"/>.
13463.             </summary>
13464.             <remarks>
13465.             <para>
13466.             These options allow for customizing the behavior and metadata of tools created with
13467.             <see cref="M:McpServerTool.Create"/>. They provide control over naming, description,
13468.             tool properties, and dependency injection integration.
13469.             </para>
13470.             <para>
13471.             When creating tools programmatically rather than using attributes, these options
13472.             provide the same level of configuration flexibility.
13473.             </para>
13474.             </remarks>
13475.         </member>
13476.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerToolCreateOptions.Services">
13477.             <summary>
13478.             Gets or sets optional services used in the construction of the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerTool"/>.
13479.             </summary>
13480.             <remarks>
13481.             These services will be used to determine which parameters should be satisfied from dependency injection. As such,
13482.             what services are satisfied via this provider should match what's satisfied via the provider passed in at invocation time.
13483.             </remarks>
13484.         </member>
13485.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerToolCreateOptions.Name">

13488.             </summary>
13489.             <remarks>
13490.             If <see langword="null"/>, but an <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerToolAttribute"/> is applied to the method,
13491.             the name from the attribute is used. If that's not present, a name based on the method's name is used.
13492.             </remarks>
13493.         </member>
13494.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerToolCreateOptions.Description">

13500.             the description from that attribute is used.
13501.             </remarks>
13502.         </member>
13503.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerToolCreateOptions.Title">

13506.             </summary>
13507.             <remarks>

13618.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerToolCreateOptions.Metadata">
13619.             <summary>

13621.             </summary>
13622.             <remarks>

13635.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerToolCreateOptions.Meta">
13636.             <summary>
13637.             Gets or sets metadata reserved by MCP for protocol-level metadata.
13638.             </summary>
13639.             <remarks>
13640.             <para>
13641.             This <see cref="T:System.Text.Json.Nodes.JsonObject"/> is used to seed the <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.Tool.Meta"/> property. Any metadata from
13642.             <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpMetaAttribute"/> instances on the method will be added to this object, but
13643.             properties already present in this <see cref="T:System.Text.Json.Nodes.JsonObject"/> will not be overwritten.
13644.             </para>
13645.             <para>
13646.             Implementations must not make assumptions about its contents.
13647.             </para>
13648.             </remarks>
13649.         </member>
13650.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerToolCreateOptions.Execution">
13651.             <summary>

13653.             </summary>
13654.             <remarks>
13655.             <para>
13656.             Execution hints provide information about how the tool should be invoked, including
13657.             task support level (<see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ToolTaskSupport"/>).
13658.             </para>
13659.             <para>
13660.             If <see langword="null"/>, the tool's execution settings are determined automatically based on
13661.             the method signature (async methods get <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ToolTaskSupport.Optional"/>; sync methods
13662.             get <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ToolTaskSupport.Forbidden"/>).
13663.             </para>
13664.             </remarks>
13665.         </member>
13666.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerToolCreateOptions.Clone">

13671.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerToolTypeAttribute">
13672.             <summary>
13673.             Indicates that a type contains methods that should be exposed as <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerTool"/>s.
13674.             </summary>
13675.             <remarks>
13676.             <para>
13677.             This attribute is used to mark a class containing methods that should be automatically
13678.             discovered and registered as <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerTool"/>s. When combined with discovery methods like
13679.             WithToolsFromAssembly, it enables automatic registration of tools without explicitly listing each tool
13680.             class. The attribute is not necessary when a reference to the type is provided directly to a method like WithTools.
13681.             </para>
13682.             <para>
13683.             Within a class marked with this attribute, individual methods that should be exposed as
13684.             tools must be marked with the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.McpServerToolAttribute"/>.
13685.             </para>
13686.             </remarks>
13687.         </member>

13914.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.Server.SseResponseStreamTransport">
13915.             <summary>
13916.             Provides an <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ITransport"/> implementation using Server-Sent Events (SSE) for server-to-client communication.
13917.             </summary>
13918.             <remarks>
13919.             <para>
13920.             This transport provides one-way communication from server to client using the SSE protocol over HTTP,
13921.             while receiving client messages through a separate mechanism. It writes messages as
13922.             SSE events to a response stream, typically associated with an HTTP response.
13923.             </para>
13924.             <para>
13925.             This transport is used in scenarios where the server needs to push messages to the client in real-time,
13926.             such as when streaming completion results or providing progress updates during long-running operations.
13927.             </para>
13928.             <para>
13929.             <b>Backpressure consideration:</b> The SSE transport separates request and response channels — the client POSTs
13930.             messages to a separate endpoint while responses flow over the SSE stream. If the HTTP handler for incoming
13931.             messages returns immediately (e.g., <c>202 Accepted</c>) after calling <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.Server.SseResponseStreamTransport.OnMessageReceivedAsync(ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcMessage,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>,
13932.             there is no HTTP-level backpressure on handler concurrency. The ASP.NET Core integration disables legacy SSE
13933.             endpoints by default for this reason. If you are using this type directly, consider holding the POST response
13934.             open until the handler completes, or applying rate-limiting at the HTTP layer.
13935.             </para>
13936.             </remarks>
13937.             <param name="sseResponseStream">The response stream to write MCP JSON-RPC messages as SSE events to.</param>
13938.             <param name="messageEndpoint">
13939.             The relative or absolute URI the client should use to post MCP JSON-RPC messages for this session.
13940.             These messages should be passed to <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.Server.SseResponseStreamTransport.OnMessageReceivedAsync(ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcMessage,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>.
13941.             Defaults to "/message".
13942.             </param>
13943.             <param name="sessionId">The identifier corresponding to the current MCP session.</param>
13944.         </member>
13945.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.Server.SseResponseStreamTransport.#ctor(System.IO.Stream,System.String,System.String)">
13946.             <summary>
13947.             Provides an <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ITransport"/> implementation using Server-Sent Events (SSE) for server-to-client communication.
13948.             </summary>
13949.             <remarks>
13950.             <para>
13951.             This transport provides one-way communication from server to client using the SSE protocol over HTTP,
13952.             while receiving client messages through a separate mechanism. It writes messages as
13953.             SSE events to a response stream, typically associated with an HTTP response.
13954.             </para>
13955.             <para>
13956.             This transport is used in scenarios where the server needs to push messages to the client in real-time,
13957.             such as when streaming completion results or providing progress updates during long-running operations.
13958.             </para>
13959.             <para>
13960.             <b>Backpressure consideration:</b> The SSE transport separates request and response channels — the client POSTs
13961.             messages to a separate endpoint while responses flow over the SSE stream. If the HTTP handler for incoming
13962.             messages returns immediately (e.g., <c>202 Accepted</c>) after calling <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.Server.SseResponseStreamTransport.OnMessageReceivedAsync(ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcMessage,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>,
13963.             there is no HTTP-level backpressure on handler concurrency. The ASP.NET Core integration disables legacy SSE
13964.             endpoints by default for this reason. If you are using this type directly, consider holding the POST response
13965.             open until the handler completes, or applying rate-limiting at the HTTP layer.
13966.             </para>
13967.             </remarks>
13968.             <param name="sseResponseStream">The response stream to write MCP JSON-RPC messages as SSE events to.</param>
13969.             <param name="messageEndpoint">
13970.             The relative or absolute URI the client should use to post MCP JSON-RPC messages for this session.
13971.             These messages should be passed to <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.Server.SseResponseStreamTransport.OnMessageReceivedAsync(ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcMessage,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>.
13972.             Defaults to "/message".
13973.             </param>
13974.             <param name="sessionId">The identifier corresponding to the current MCP session.</param>
13975.         </member>
13976.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.Server.SseResponseStreamTransport.RunAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)">
13977.             <summary>
13978.             Starts the transport and writes the JSON-RPC messages sent via <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.Server.SseResponseStreamTransport.SendMessageAsync(ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcMessage,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>
13979.             to the SSE response stream until cancellation is requested or the transport is disposed.
13980.             </summary>
13981.             <param name="cancellationToken">The <see cref="T:System.Threading.CancellationToken"/> to monitor for cancellation requests. The default is <see cref="P:System.Threading.CancellationToken.None"/>.</param>
13982.             <returns>A task representing the send loop that writes JSON-RPC messages to the SSE response stream.</returns>
13983.         </member>

13996.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.Server.SseResponseStreamTransport.OnMessageReceivedAsync(ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcMessage,System.Threading.CancellationToken)">
13997.             <summary>
13998.             Handles incoming JSON-RPC messages received on the /message endpoint.
13999.             </summary>
14000.             <param name="message">The JSON-RPC message received from the client.</param>
14001.             <param name="cancellationToken">The <see cref="T:System.Threading.CancellationToken"/> to monitor for cancellation requests. The default is <see cref="P:System.Threading.CancellationToken.None"/>.</param>
14002.             <returns>A task representing the asynchronous operation to buffer the JSON-RPC message for processing.</returns>
14003.             <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="message"/> is <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
14004.             <exception cref="T:System.InvalidOperationException">There is an attempt to process a message before calling <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.Server.SseResponseStreamTransport.RunAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>.</exception>
14005.             <remarks>
14006.             <para>
14007.             This method is the entry point for processing client-to-server communication in the SSE transport model.
14008.             While the SSE protocol itself is unidirectional (server to client), this method allows bidirectional
14009.             communication by handling HTTP POST requests sent to the message endpoint.
14010.             </para>
14011.             <para>
14012.             When a client sends a JSON-RPC message to the /message endpoint, the server calls this method to
14013.             process the message and make it available to the MCP server via the <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.SseResponseStreamTransport.MessageReader"/> channel.
14014.             </para>
14015.             <para>
14016.             If an authenticated <see cref="T:System.Security.Claims.ClaimsPrincipal"/> sent the message, that can be included in the <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcMessage.Context"/>.
14017.             No other part of the context should be set.
14018.             </para>
14019.             </remarks>
14020.         </member>

14093.             Gets or initializes a value that indicates whether the transport should be in stateless mode that does not require all requests for a given session
14094.             to arrive to the same ASP.NET Core application process. Unsolicited server-to-client messages are not supported in this mode,
14095.             so calling <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.Server.StreamableHttpServerTransport.HandleGetRequestAsync(System.IO.Stream,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/> results in an <see cref="T:System.InvalidOperationException"/>.
14096.             Server-to-client requests are also unsupported, because the responses might arrive at another ASP.NET Core application process.
14097.             Client sampling and roots capabilities are also disabled in stateless mode, because the server cannot make requests.

14115.         <member name="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.StreamableHttpServerTransport.OnSessionInitialized">

14121.             initialization handshake. This can be used to persist session data for cross-instance migration.

14127.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.Server.StreamableHttpServerTransport.HandleInitializeRequestAsync(ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.InitializeRequestParams)">
14128.             <summary>
14129.             Handles initialization by capturing the negotiated protocol version and optionally invoking
14130.             <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.StreamableHttpServerTransport.OnSessionInitialized"/> so session data can be persisted.
14131.             </summary>
14132.             <remarks>
14133.             This is called automatically when an <c>initialize</c> request is processed via
14134.             <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.Server.StreamableHttpServerTransport.HandlePostRequestAsync(ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcMessage,System.IO.Stream,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>. It can also be called
14135.             directly when restoring a migrated session with known <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.InitializeRequestParams"/>.
14136.             </remarks>
14137.             <param name="initParams">The initialization parameters from the client, or <see langword="null"/> if unavailable.</param>

14139.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.Server.StreamableHttpServerTransport.HandleGetRequestAsync(System.IO.Stream,System.Threading.CancellationToken)">
14140.             <summary>
14141.             Handles an optional SSE GET request a client using the Streamable HTTP transport might make by
14142.             writing any unsolicited JSON-RPC messages sent via <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.Server.StreamableHttpServerTransport.SendMessageAsync(ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcMessage,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>
14143.             to the SSE response stream until cancellation is requested or the transport is disposed.
14144.             </summary>
14145.             <param name="sseResponseStream">The response stream to write MCP JSON-RPC messages as SSE events to.</param>
14146.             <param name="cancellationToken">The <see cref="T:System.Threading.CancellationToken"/> to monitor for cancellation requests. The default is <see cref="P:System.Threading.CancellationToken.None"/>.</param>
14147.             <returns>A task representing the send loop that writes JSON-RPC messages to the SSE response stream.</returns>
14148.             <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="sseResponseStream"/> is <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
14149.             <exception cref="T:System.InvalidOperationException">
14150.             <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Server.StreamableHttpServerTransport.Stateless"/> is <see langword="true"/> and GET requests are not supported in stateless mode.
14151.             </exception>
14152.         </member>
14153.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.Server.StreamableHttpServerTransport.HandlePostRequestAsync(ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcMessage,System.IO.Stream,System.Threading.CancellationToken)">
14154.             <summary>
14155.             Handles a Streamable HTTP POST request processing both the request body and response body ensuring that
14156.             <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcResponse"/> and other correlated messages are sent back to the client directly in response
14157.             to the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcRequest"/> that initiated the message.
14158.             </summary>
14159.             <param name="message">The JSON-RPC message received from the client via the POST request body.</param>
14160.             <param name="cancellationToken">The <see cref="T:System.Threading.CancellationToken"/> to monitor for cancellation requests. The default is <see cref="P:System.Threading.CancellationToken.None"/>.</param>
14161.             <param name="responseStream">The POST response body to write MCP JSON-RPC messages to.</param>
14162.             <returns>
14163.             <see langword="true"/> if data was written to the response body.
14164.             <see langword="false"/> if nothing was written because the request body did not contain any <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcRequest"/> messages to respond to.
14165.             The HTTP application should typically respond with an empty "202 Accepted" response in this scenario.
14166.             </returns>
14167.             <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="message"/> or <paramref name="responseStream"/> is <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
14168.             <remarks>
14169.             If an authenticated <see cref="T:System.Security.Claims.ClaimsPrincipal"/> sent the message, that can be included in the <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcMessage.Context"/>.
14170.             No other part of the context should be set.
14171.             </remarks>
14172.         </member>

14246.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.IMcpTaskStore">
14247.             <summary>
14248.             Provides an interface for pluggable task storage implementations in MCP servers.
14249.             </summary>
14250.             <remarks>
14251.             <para>
14252.             The task store is responsible for managing the lifecycle of tasks, including creation,
14253.             status updates, result storage, and retrieval. Implementations must be thread-safe and
14254.             may support session-based isolation for multi-session scenarios.
14255.             </para>
14256.             <para>
14257.             TTL (Time To Live) Management: Implementations may override the requested TTL value in
14258.             <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskMetadata.TimeToLive"/> to enforce resource limits. The actual TTL
14259.             used is returned in the <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTask.TimeToLive"/> property. A null TTL indicates
14260.             unlimited lifetime. Tasks may be deleted after their TTL expires, regardless of status.
14261.             </para>
14262.             </remarks>
14263.         </member>
14264.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.IMcpTaskStore.CreateTaskAsync(ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskMetadata,ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestId,ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcRequest,System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)">
14265.             <summary>
14266.             Creates a new task for tracking an asynchronous operation.
14267.             </summary>
14268.             <param name="taskParams">Metadata for the task, including requested TTL.</param>
14269.             <param name="requestId">The JSON-RPC request ID that initiated this task.</param>
14270.             <param name="request">The original JSON-RPC request that triggered task creation.</param>
14271.             <param name="sessionId">Optional session identifier for multi-session isolation.</param>
14272.             <param name="cancellationToken">Cancellation token for the operation.</param>
14273.             <returns>
14274.             A new <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTask"/> with a unique task ID, initial status of <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatus.Working"/>,
14275.             and the actual TTL that will be used (which may differ from the requested TTL).
14276.             </returns>
14277.             <remarks>
14278.             Implementations must generate a unique task ID and set the <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTask.CreatedAt"/>
14279.             and <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTask.LastUpdatedAt"/> timestamps. The implementation may override the
14280.             requested TTL to enforce storage limits.
14281.             </remarks>
14282.         </member>
14283.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.IMcpTaskStore.GetTaskAsync(System.String,System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)">
14284.             <summary>
14285.             Retrieves a task by its unique identifier.
14286.             </summary>
14287.             <param name="taskId">The unique identifier of the task to retrieve.</param>
14288.             <param name="sessionId">Optional session identifier for access control.</param>
14289.             <param name="cancellationToken">Cancellation token for the operation.</param>
14290.             <returns>
14291.             The <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTask"/> if found and accessible, otherwise <see langword="null"/>.
14292.             </returns>
14293.             <remarks>
14294.             Returns null if the task does not exist or if session-based access control denies access.
14295.             </remarks>
14296.         </member>
14297.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.IMcpTaskStore.StoreTaskResultAsync(System.String,ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatus,System.Text.Json.JsonElement,System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)">
14298.             <summary>
14299.             Stores the final result of a task that has reached a terminal status.
14300.             </summary>
14301.             <param name="taskId">The unique identifier of the task.</param>
14302.             <param name="status">The terminal status: <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatus.Completed"/> or <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatus.Failed"/>.</param>
14303.             <param name="result">The operation result to store as a JSON element.</param>
14304.             <param name="sessionId">Optional session identifier for access control.</param>
14305.             <param name="cancellationToken">Cancellation token for the operation.</param>
14306.             <returns>The updated <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTask"/> with the new status and result stored.</returns>
14307.             <remarks>
14308.             <para>
14309.             The <paramref name="status"/> must be either <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatus.Completed"/> or
14310.             <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatus.Failed"/>. This method updates the task status and stores
14311.             the result for later retrieval via <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.IMcpTaskStore.GetTaskResultAsync(System.String,System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>.
14312.             </para>
14313.             <para>
14314.             Implementations should throw <see cref="T:System.InvalidOperationException"/> if called on a task
14315.             that is already in a terminal state, to prevent result overwrites.
14316.             </para>
14317.             </remarks>
14318.         </member>
14319.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.IMcpTaskStore.GetTaskResultAsync(System.String,System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)">
14320.             <summary>
14321.             Retrieves the stored result of a completed or failed task.
14322.             </summary>
14323.             <param name="taskId">The unique identifier of the task.</param>
14324.             <param name="sessionId">Optional session identifier for access control.</param>
14325.             <param name="cancellationToken">Cancellation token for the operation.</param>
14326.             <returns>The stored operation result as a JSON element.</returns>
14327.             <remarks>
14328.             This method should only be called on tasks in terminal states (<see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatus.Completed"/>
14329.             or <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatus.Failed"/>). The result contains the JSON representation of the
14330.             original operation result (e.g., <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.CallToolResult"/> for tools/call).
14331.             </remarks>
14332.         </member>
14333.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.IMcpTaskStore.UpdateTaskStatusAsync(System.String,ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatus,System.String,System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)">
14334.             <summary>
14335.             Updates the status and optional status message of a task.
14336.             </summary>
14337.             <param name="taskId">The unique identifier of the task.</param>
14338.             <param name="status">The new status to set.</param>
14339.             <param name="statusMessage">Optional diagnostic message describing the status change.</param>
14340.             <param name="sessionId">Optional session identifier for access control.</param>
14341.             <param name="cancellationToken">Cancellation token for the operation.</param>
14342.             <returns>The updated <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTask"/> with the new status applied.</returns>
14343.             <remarks>
14344.             This method updates the task's <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTask.Status"/>, <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTask.StatusMessage"/>,
14345.             and <see cref="P:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTask.LastUpdatedAt"/> properties. Common uses include transitioning to
14346.             <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatus.Cancelled"/>, <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatus.InputRequired"/>, or updating
14347.             progress messages while in <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatus.Working"/> status.
14348.             </remarks>
14349.         </member>
14350.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.IMcpTaskStore.ListTasksAsync(System.String,System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)">
14351.             <summary>
14352.             Lists tasks with pagination support.
14353.             </summary>
14354.             <param name="cursor">Optional cursor for pagination, from a previous call's nextCursor value.</param>
14355.             <param name="sessionId">Optional session identifier for filtering tasks by session.</param>
14356.             <param name="cancellationToken">Cancellation token for the operation.</param>
14357.             <returns>A <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.ListTasksResult"/> containing the tasks and an optional cursor for the next page.</returns>
14358.             <remarks>
14359.             When <paramref name="sessionId"/> is provided, implementations should filter to only return
14360.             tasks associated with that session. The cursor format is implementation-specific.
14361.             </remarks>
14362.         </member>
14363.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.IMcpTaskStore.CancelTaskAsync(System.String,System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)">
14364.             <summary>
14365.             Attempts to cancel a task, transitioning it to <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatus.Cancelled"/> status.
14366.             </summary>
14367.             <param name="taskId">The unique identifier of the task to cancel.</param>
14368.             <param name="sessionId">Optional session identifier for access control.</param>
14369.             <param name="cancellationToken">Cancellation token for the operation.</param>
14370.             <returns>
14371.             The updated <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTask"/>. If the task is already in a terminal state
14372.             (<see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatus.Completed"/>, <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatus.Failed"/>, or
14373.             <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatus.Cancelled"/>), the task is returned unchanged.
14374.             </returns>
14375.             <remarks>
14376.             <para>
14377.             This method must be idempotent. If called on a task that is already in a terminal state,
14378.             it returns the current task without error. This behavior differs from the MCP specification
14379.             but ensures idempotency and avoids race conditions between cancellation and task completion.
14380.             </para>
14381.             <para>
14382.             For tasks not in a terminal state, the implementation should attempt to stop the underlying
14383.             operation and transition the task to <see cref="F:ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskStatus.Cancelled"/> status before returning.
14384.             </para>
14385.             </remarks>
14386.         </member>
14387.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.InMemoryMcpTaskStore">
14388.             <summary>
14389.             Provides an in-memory implementation of <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.IMcpTaskStore"/> for development and testing.
14390.             </summary>
14391.             <remarks>
14392.             <para>
14393.             This implementation uses thread-safe concurrent collections and is suitable for single-server
14394.             scenarios and testing. It is not recommended for production multi-server deployments as tasks
14395.             are stored only in memory and are lost on server restart.
14396.             </para>
14397.             <para>
14398.             Features:
14399.             <list type="bullet">
14400.             <item><description>Thread-safe operations using <see cref="T:System.Collections.Concurrent.ConcurrentDictionary`2"/></description></item>
14401.             <item><description>Automatic TTL-based cleanup via background task</description></item>
14402.             <item><description>Session-based isolation when sessionId is provided</description></item>
14403.             <item><description>Configurable default TTL and maximum TTL limits</description></item>
14404.             </list>
14405.             </para>
14406.             </remarks>
14407.         </member>
14408.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.InMemoryMcpTaskStore.#ctor(System.Nullable{System.TimeSpan},System.Nullable{System.TimeSpan},System.Nullable{System.TimeSpan},System.Nullable{System.TimeSpan},System.Int32,System.Nullable{System.Int32},System.Nullable{System.Int32})">
14409.             <summary>
14410.             Initializes a new instance of the <see cref="T:ModelContextProtocol.InMemoryMcpTaskStore"/> class.
14411.             </summary>
14412.             <param name="defaultTtl">
14413.             Default TTL to use when task creation does not specify a TTL. Null means unlimited.
14414.             </param>
14415.             <param name="maxTtl">
14416.             Maximum TTL allowed. If a task requests a longer TTL, it will be capped to this value.
14417.             Null means no maximum limit.
14418.             </param>
14419.             <param name="pollInterval">
14420.             Advertised polling interval for tasks. Default is 1 second.
14421.             This value is used when creating new tasks to indicate how frequently clients should poll for updates.
14422.             </param>
14423.             <param name="cleanupInterval">
14424.             Interval for running background cleanup of expired tasks. Default is 1 minute.
14425.             Pass <see cref="F:System.Threading.Timeout.InfiniteTimeSpan"/> to disable automatic cleanup.
14426.             </param>
14427.             <param name="pageSize">
14428.             Maximum number of tasks to return per page in <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.InMemoryMcpTaskStore.ListTasksAsync(System.String,System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>. Default is 100.
14429.             </param>
14430.             <param name="maxTasks">
14431.             Maximum number of tasks allowed in the store globally. Null means unlimited.
14432.             When the limit is reached, <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.InMemoryMcpTaskStore.CreateTaskAsync(ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskMetadata,ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestId,ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcRequest,System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/> will throw <see cref="T:System.InvalidOperationException"/>.
14433.             </param>
14434.             <param name="maxTasksPerSession">
14435.             Maximum number of tasks allowed per session. Null means unlimited.
14436.             When the limit is reached for a session, <see cref="M:ModelContextProtocol.InMemoryMcpTaskStore.CreateTaskAsync(ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.McpTaskMetadata,ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.RequestId,ModelContextProtocol.Protocol.JsonRpcRequest,System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/> will throw <see cref="T:System.InvalidOperationException"/>.
14437.             </param>
14438.         </member>

14505.         <member name="M:ModelContextProtocol.UriTemplate.UriTemplateExpression">
14506.             <remarks>
14507.             Pattern:<br/>
14508.             <code>{                                                       # opening brace\r\n    (?<operator>[+#./;?&]?)                             # optional operator\r\n    (?<varname>\r\n        (?:[A-Za-z0-9_]|%[0-9A-Fa-f]{2})                # varchar: letter, digit, underscore, or pct-encoded\r\n        (?:\\.?(?:[A-Za-z0-9_]|%[0-9A-Fa-f]{2}))*        # optionally dot-separated subsequent varchars\r\n    )\r\n    (?: :[1-9][0-9]{0,3} )?                             # optional prefix modifier (1–4 digits)\r\n    \\*?                                                 # optional explode\r\n    (?:,                                                # comma separator, followed by the same as above\r\n        (?<varname>\r\n            (?:[A-Za-z0-9_]|%[0-9A-Fa-f]{2})\r\n            (?:\\.?(?:[A-Za-z0-9_]|%[0-9A-Fa-f]{2}))*\r\n        )\r\n        (?: :[1-9][0-9]{0,3} )?\r\n        \\*?\r\n    )*                                                  # zero or more additional vars\r\n}                                                       # closing brace</code><br/>
14509.             Options:<br/>
14510.             <code>RegexOptions.IgnorePatternWhitespace</code><br/>
14511.             Explanation:<br/>
14512.             <code>
14513.             ○ Match '{'.<br/>
14514.             ○ "operator" capture group.<br/>
14515.                 ○ Match a character in the set [#&+./;?] atomically, optionally.<br/>
14516.             ○ "varname" capture group.<br/>
14517.                 ○ Match with 2 alternative expressions.<br/>
14518.                     ○ Match a character in the set [0-9A-Z_a-z].<br/>
14519.                     ○ Match a sequence of expressions.<br/>
14520.                         ○ Match an empty string.<br/>
14521.                         ○ Match a character in the set [0-9A-Fa-f] exactly 2 times.<br/>
14522.                 ○ Loop greedily any number of times.<br/>
14523.                     ○ Match '.' atomically, optionally.<br/>
14524.                     ○ Match with 2 alternative expressions.<br/>
14525.                         ○ Match a character in the set [0-9A-Z_a-z].<br/>
14526.                         ○ Match a sequence of expressions.<br/>
14527.                             ○ Match an empty string.<br/>
14528.                             ○ Match a character in the set [0-9A-Fa-f] exactly 2 times.<br/>
14529.             ○ Optional (greedy).<br/>
14530.                 ○ Match ':'.<br/>
14531.                 ○ Match a character in the set [1-9].<br/>
14532.                 ○ Match a character in the set [0-9] greedily at most 3 times.<br/>
14533.             ○ Match '*' greedily, optionally.<br/>
14534.             ○ Loop greedily any number of times.<br/>
14535.                 ○ Match ','.<br/>
14536.                 ○ "varname" capture group.<br/>
14537.                     ○ Match with 2 alternative expressions.<br/>
14538.                         ○ Match a character in the set [0-9A-Z_a-z].<br/>
14539.                         ○ Match a sequence of expressions.<br/>
14540.                             ○ Match an empty string.<br/>
14541.                             ○ Match a character in the set [0-9A-Fa-f] exactly 2 times.<br/>
14542.                     ○ Loop greedily any number of times.<br/>
14543.                         ○ Match '.' atomically, optionally.<br/>
14544.                         ○ Match with 2 alternative expressions.<br/>
14545.                             ○ Match a character in the set [0-9A-Z_a-z].<br/>
14546.                             ○ Match a sequence of expressions.<br/>
14547.                                 ○ Match an empty string.<br/>
14548.                                 ○ Match a character in the set [0-9A-Fa-f] exactly 2 times.<br/>
14549.                 ○ Optional (greedy).<br/>
14550.                     ○ Match ':'.<br/>
14551.                     ○ Match a character in the set [1-9].<br/>
14552.                     ○ Match a character in the set [0-9] greedily at most 3 times.<br/>
14553.                 ○ Match '*' atomically, optionally.<br/>
14554.             ○ Match '}'.<br/>
14555.             </code>
14556.             </remarks>

14572.         <member name="T:ModelContextProtocol.UriTemplate.UriTemplateComparer">
14573.              <summary>
14574.              Defines an equality comparer for Uri templates as follows:
14575.              1. Non-templated Uris use regular System.Uri equality comparison (host name is case insensitive).
14576.              2. Templated Uris use regular string equality.

14578.              We do this because non-templated resources are looked up directly from the resource dictionary
14579.              and we need to make sure equality is implemented correctly. Templated Uris are resolved in a
14580.              fallback step using linear traversal of the resource dictionary, so their equality is only
14581.              there to distinguish between different templates.

             <summary>
             Provides helper methods for monotonic ID generation.

         <member name="M:System.IdHelpers.CreateMonotonicId(System.DateTimeOffset)">
             <summary>
             Creates a strictly monotonically increasing identifier string using 64-bit timestamp ticks
             and a 64-bit counter, formatted as a 32-character hexadecimal string (GUID-like).
             </summary>
             <param name="timestamp">The timestamp to embed in the identifier.</param>
             <returns>A new strictly monotonically increasing identifier string.</returns>
             <remarks>
             <para>
             This method creates a 128-bit identifier composed of two 64-bit values:
             - High 64 bits: <see cref="P:System.DateTimeOffset.Ticks"/> from the timestamp
             - Low 64 bits: A globally monotonically increasing counter
             </para>
             <para>
             The resulting string is strictly monotonically increasing when compared lexicographically,
             which is required for keyset pagination to work correctly. Unlike <c>Guid.CreateVersion7</c>,
             which uses random bits for intra-millisecond uniqueness, this implementation guarantees
             strict ordering for all identifiers regardless of when they were created.
             </para>
             </remarks>
         </member>
